title: harness-pi: add baseline coverage before auto-routing promotion
description: pi is a registered supported subprocess harness but should remain explicit-only until it has full baseline coverage comparable to primary harnesses. Add or audit coverage for argument construction, prompt/workdir delivery, model selection/listing, reasoning/thinking support, token usage, error/cancel behavior, permission semantics, final metadata, and live or recorded evidence. Only promote pi to auto-routing eligibility after the evidence exists.\n\nIn-scope files are expected to include internal/harnesses/pi/, service/routing tests, capability metadata, and docs evidence.
acceptance:
1. pi runner tests cover model flag, thinking/reasoning flag, workdir or subprocess Dir behavior, prompt delivery, timeout/cancellation cleanup, stderr/exit mapping, final text, and usage parsing if supported.
2. Model listing and reasoning/thinking support are implemented with source metadata or explicitly declared unsupported with routing tests.
3. Permission semantics are documented and tested.
4. Capability matrix/docs include evidence pointers and blockers.
5. pi is promoted to auto-routing eligibility only if AC1-AC4 are satisfied; otherwise it remains explicit-only.
6. go test ./... passes.

title: service: implement DdxAgent.ListHarnesses with rich HarnessInfo (install, perms, efforts, quota)
description:
Implement DdxAgent.ListHarnesses(ctx) per CONTRACT-001. Returns []HarnessInfo where each entry includes:
- Name, Type ("native" | "subprocess")
- Available, Path, Error
- IsLocal, IsSubscription, ExactPinSupport
- SupportedPermissions (subset of safe/supervised/unrestricted) — derived from PermissionArgs map keys
- SupportedEfforts (subset of low/medium/high) — derived from ReasoningLevels
- CostClass (local | cheap | medium | expensive)
- Quota (live; nil if not applicable; for claude/codex this consults the cache + tmux probe)

Backed by the registry+state ports (agent-X for registry+state). Per-harness Quota field populated from the harness implementation when available.
acceptance:
- [ ] DdxAgent.ListHarnesses(ctx) returns one HarnessInfo per registered harness
- [ ] SupportedPermissions and SupportedEfforts are accurate per harness (claude has all 3 of each; gemini has different subsets)
- [ ] Quota field is non-nil for claude/codex when their tmux probe succeeds
- [ ] Test exercises at least claude + 1 other harness

rationale: closed focused bead with small acceptance surface
title: BLOCKER FIXED: tests leak GIT_DIR into parent repo .git/config
description: Lefthook sets GIT_DIR during pre-commit and Go tests inherit it when spawning git subprocesses, mutating the parent repo config with stray 'worktree = /tmp/TestXxx/NNN' lines. Fixed in commit 2032819 by adding TestMain GIT_* prefix scrub to internal/git, internal/server, internal/agent, internal/bead, and cmd test packages. This was the root cause of all the .git/config corruption during concurrent execute-bead runs.
acceptance: FIXED in commit 2032819 (main). Integration test needed: run execute-bead from inside a linked worktree and assert shared .git/config is unchanged after completion.

rationale: closed focused bead with small acceptance surface
title: Create skills/ddx/reference/beads.md — execution-ready bead authoring
description:
Create skills/ddx/reference/beads.md — the domain reference file a harness reads when the user asks to create, plan, or update beads.

Content required:
- **Inline context, not links.** Every bead description must be readable cold by an automated execute-bead worker that has no chat history and no open tabs.
- **Explicit in-scope file list.** Repo-relative paths. Bad: 'the auth module'. Good: 'cli/internal/auth/pipeline.go and cli/internal/auth/pipeline_test.go'.
- **Explicit do-NOT-touch scope negation** (non-goals). Prevents scope creep during agent execution.
- **Favor many small beads.** Rule of thumb: if a bead would touch more than ~3 files or more than one subsystem, decompose into child beads under an epic.
- **Acceptance is a command that passes, not a sentence.** 'X works' is not AC. 'cd cli && go test ./internal/foo/... passes and returns 0' is AC.
- **Implementations include tests.** The acceptance criteria must reference a test command.
- **Rollback/cleanup expectations** for partial changes — what 'leave it unclaimed' means in practice.
- **spec-id linkage** when a governing artifact exists (FEAT-*, SD-*, TD-*, ADR-*); omit when it doesn't.
- **CLI reference** at the end: ddx bead create/update/close/list/show/ready/blocked/dep/import/export/evidence.

Source material: existing /skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md (already contains much of this — especially the top-level "Execution-Ready" section added in an earlier refinement). Strip HELIX-specific rules: remove the mandatory 'helix' label and the phase:frame|design|test|build|deploy|iterate|review enumeration. Keep generic guidance ('label beads with area, kind, and any workflow tags your project uses').

In-scope files:
- skills/ddx/reference/beads.md (new)

Out-of-scope:
- skills/ddx/SKILL.md (separate bead ddx-bd852e86)
- skills/ddx/reference/{work,review,agents,status}.md (separate beads)
acceptance: File exists at skills/ddx/reference/beads.md. Contains sections covering: inline context requirement, explicit file scope (in + out), small-bead rule of thumb, command-based acceptance criteria, tests-included requirement, rollback expectations, spec-id guidance, CLI reference. No occurrences of 'phase:(frame|design|test|build|deploy|iterate|review)' in body. No mandatory 'helix' label requirement (grep -E 'helix.*(required|must|required)' returns no matches). Linked from skills/ddx/SKILL.md intent-router.

title: integration suite: cost cap + determinism controls + parity comparison reporting
description:
Add safety + reproducibility controls to the integration suite (depends on agent-X scaffolding bead).

- --max-cost-usd <N> flag (default $0.50). Halts the sweep when accumulated cost exceeds cap. Per-task estimate checked against remaining budget pre-invocation; over-budget tasks skip with explicit "skipped: cost cap" reason.
- temperature=0 + per-task seed for harnesses/providers that support deterministic sampling. Seeds recorded alongside results so reruns are reproducible.
- Non-deterministic harnesses (no seed support) flagged in the report ("non-deterministic: {reason}"); their parity-comparison results treated as advisory only.
- Parity comparison method: tool-call sequence equality is primary signal — same sequence of (tool_name, normalized_args) = behaviorally equivalent. String-similarity (cosine on character n-grams) as secondary signal flagging "looks similar but not identical." NO automated semantic equivalence — humans review divergences.
- Future scope (out, file follow-up beads): embedding-similarity, LLM-as-judge.
acceptance:
- [ ] --max-cost-usd cap halts sweep when exceeded; per-task pre-check works
- [ ] temperature=0 + seed set on every supporting harness call; recorded in results
- [ ] Non-deterministic harnesses flagged in report
- [ ] Parity report tabulates tool-call sequences and final-string-similarity for human review

title: bug: /v1/models is not a liveness probe — providers shows 'connected' when LM Studio has no model loaded
description:
Discovered 2026-04-15 while routing work to vidar-coder (LM Studio on vidar:1234).

## Two-part bug

### Part 1: bare-name JIT miss (the actual root cause)

Initial probe: `POST /v1/chat/completions {model: 'qwen3-coder-next', ...}` → 400 'No models loaded'.

We assumed LM Studio had no model loaded. Wrong. The actual model is listed by /v1/models as 'qwen/qwen3-coder-next' (with the 'qwen/' prefix). LM Studio's just-in-time loading IS enabled and DOES auto-load models on demand — but only when the requested model ID matches one in /v1/models exactly. Bare-name 'qwen3-coder-next' didn't match, so JIT couldn't fire, and LM Studio returned the misleading 'No models loaded' error instead of 'unknown model'.

Verified by re-probing with 'qwen/qwen3-coder-next' — JIT fired, model loaded, responded 'OK' in ~60s on cold start.

### Part 2: discovery should be standard practice

The routing/probe layer should never trust user-supplied bare model names against an LM Studio provider. The standard discovery flow should be:

1. GET /v1/models → enumerate the catalog the server is offering
2. For each catalog ID, optionally probe POST /v1/chat/completions with a 1-token request and a generous timeout (60s+ on cold start, 3s on warm)
3. Cache the (provider, model_id) → inference-ready bool with a short TTL
4. When user code asks for 'model qwen3-coder-next' against this provider, normalize against the discovered catalog: if there's exactly one match by suffix, accept it; if zero or multiple, error with the candidate list

This converts the 'No models loaded' misleading error into a clear 'requested model qwen3-coder-next not in catalog; closest match: qwen/qwen3-coder-next' (or just transparent normalization).

## Impact on cost-tiered routing

The cost-tiered routing policy (DDx bead ddx-dc231144 tier-based auto-escalation) needs reliable provider readiness signals. Without discovery + normalization:
- bare-name configs silently fail and force escalation
- the 'connected (N models)' status is unreliable as a routing input
- every misrouted attempt burns latency and forces the next tier

## Fix scope

- Update routing/health probe in cmd/ddx-agent/routing.go (or equivalent) to enumerate /v1/models and probe each listed model with its exact ID
- Add a model-id normalization layer that maps bare names to catalog IDs by suffix-match (with explicit error on ambiguity)
- Cache probe results per (provider, model) for a short TTL (30s reasonable)
- Surface the distinction in 'ddx-agent providers' output: http-connected (from /v1/models) vs inference-ready-models (the subset that actually responds)
acceptance: (1) GET /v1/models is enumerated by the discovery layer; the catalog is the canonical model-ID source per provider. (2) Bare model names ('qwen3-coder-next') passed by callers are normalized to canonical IDs ('qwen/qwen3-coder-next') by suffix-match against the catalog, with explicit ambiguity errors. (3) providers command shows http-connected and inference-ready-models per provider. (4) Routing policy consumes inference-ready-models, not 'connected (N models)'. (5) Regression test uses a mock LM Studio that lists 'qwen/foo' but rejects 'foo' on chat/completions; discovery normalizes 'foo' → 'qwen/foo' and the probe succeeds.

title: correctness: define mixed-provenance root cost rollup semantics
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-001-otel-telemetry-capture.md:221
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: CONTRACT-001 says the root invoke_agent span should emit a session cost rollup whenever every contributing chat span has known cost, and only become unknown when any child cost is unknown. But the contract never defines what ddx.cost.source, ddx.cost.currency, and ddx.cost.pricing_ref should look like when all child chat costs are known yet their provenance differs across turns, such as one provider-reported turn plus one configured-cost turn. That makes the required root shape ambiguous: ddx.cost.source is required when amount is known, but the allowed enum only models single-attempt provenance. The current implementation fell into this gap and downgraded a known total to unknown when provenance metadata differed.
Suggested fix: Amend the root-span cost-rollup section to specify a conformant representation for mixed known-cost provenance. Either add an explicit aggregate/root rollup source shape, or state that the root may still emit ddx.cost.amount and currency while omitting or summarizing provenance fields that cannot be represented cleanly. Add a contract-focused regression test or checklist entry covering mixed provider-reported/configured sessions.
acceptance: CONTRACT-001 explicitly defines how the root invoke_agent span represents known session totals when child chat spans have mixed known provenance; the contract no longer requires an impossible or ambiguous ddx.cost.source/pricing_ref combination at the root; conformance tests or checklist coverage include a mixed provider-reported/configured session example.

title: cache: cost attribution at the native loop using already-existing manifest fields
description:
Bead D of the prompt-caching plan (codex-refined). Wire the cache-token costs that Anthropic and OpenAI already report into core.CostAttribution. Today the configured-cost fallback at internal/core/loop.go:303 prices only Input + Output; cache-read tokens are counted at full input price for cost attribution even though Anthropic/OpenAI charge ~10% / ~50% of base. Depends on bead A (agent-cccc2df7) for the CachePolicy="off" semantics.

Codex correctly diagnosed the wiring: manifest v4 already has CostCacheReadPerM and CostCacheWritePerM at internal/modelcatalog/manifest.go:44-45 (model entries) and :139-140 (target entries) with validation. CostAttribution already has CacheReadAmount and CacheWriteAmount at internal/core/agent.go:198-199. Telemetry emission already wires them at internal/core/loop.go:936-940. The only missing piece is: PricingFor at internal/modelcatalog/catalog.go:388 drops the cache fields when projecting to core.ModelPricing, and core.ModelPricing at internal/core/pricing.go:5 lacks the slots, so loop.go:303 has no source-of-truth to read.

Three coordinated edits:

1. internal/core/pricing.go:5 — add CacheReadPerM, CacheWritePerM float64 to core.ModelPricing.

2. internal/modelcatalog/catalog.go:388 (PricingFor) — preserve CostCacheReadPerM and CostCacheWritePerM from the manifest model/target onto the returned ModelPricing.

3. internal/core/loop.go:303 — extend the configured-cost computation to add cache-read and cache-write costs:
   computed := input/1e6 * InputPerMTok + output/1e6 * OutputPerMTok
            + cacheRead/1e6 * CacheReadPerM
            + cacheWrite/1e6 * CacheWritePerM
   Source the cache token counts from resp.Usage.CacheReadTokens and CacheWriteTokens. Populate CostAttribution.CacheReadAmount = &cacheReadCost and CacheWriteAmount = &cacheWriteCost (slots already there).

4. Honor CachePolicy="off" from bead A: when off, emit CacheReadAmount and CacheWriteAmount as explicit *float64(0.0), NOT nil. nil = harness/provider didn't report; explicit zero = caller asked for no caching. This matches the existing zero-vs-unknown convention from agent-a8cbdb87.

5. Update CONTRACT-003 prose: paragraph in the Cost Attribution / UsageReport section explaining that cache_read and cache_write tokens are priced at the manifest's CostCacheReadPerM/CostCacheWritePerM rates; nil cache amounts mean the harness or provider didn't report cache usage; explicit zero means caching was opted out via CachePolicy="off".

Historical session-log recalculation is OUT OF SCOPE per codex: existing logs cannot be re-priced reliably from UsageReport alone without changing semantics. New runs price correctly going forward; old logs keep their existing numbers.

In-scope files:
- internal/core/pricing.go (struct fields)
- internal/modelcatalog/catalog.go (PricingFor preservation)
- internal/core/loop.go (cost computation at line 303)
- internal/core/loop_test.go (or new file: cache-cost test cases)
- internal/modelcatalog/catalog_test.go (PricingFor preservation test)
- docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-003-ddx-agent-service.md (cost-semantics prose)

Out of scope:
- Anthropic cache_control writes (bead C)
- OpenAI prefix-stability test (bead B)
- UsageReport historical recalculation
- New CHANGELOG entry beyond what the bead's commit message produces
acceptance:
1. core.ModelPricing has CacheReadPerM and CacheWritePerM float64 fields. AST-checked: TestModelPricingHasCacheFields.
2. PricingFor preserves cache fields: TestPricingForPreservesCacheFieldsFromManifest loads a manifest fixture with non-zero cost_cache_read_per_m / cost_cache_write_per_m on a model entry, calls PricingFor, asserts the returned ModelPricing carries those values verbatim.
3. Native loop populates cache cost: TestNativeLoopPopulatesCacheCostAttribution drives a fixture catalog through a virtual provider that emits resp.Usage.CacheReadTokens=1000 and CacheWriteTokens=500. Asserts CostAttribution.CacheReadAmount = (1000 / 1e6) * CostCacheReadPerM and CacheWriteAmount = (500 / 1e6) * CostCacheWritePerM, both as non-nil *float64.
4. CachePolicy=off semantics: TestCachePolicyOffZerosCacheAttribution drives the same loop with ServiceExecuteRequest.CachePolicy="off"; asserts CacheReadAmount and CacheWriteAmount are non-nil *float64 with value 0.0 (NOT nil).
5. CONTRACT-003 has the cost-semantics prose paragraph; doc-link grep test asserts the prose mentions "cost_cache_read_per_m", "explicit zero", and "opted out".
6. go test ./internal/core ./internal/modelcatalog -count=1 passes.
7. go test ./... -count=1 passes.

bead.title: cache: cost attribution at the native loop using already-existing manifest fields
bead.description:
Bead D of the prompt-caching plan (codex-refined). Wire the cache-token costs that Anthropic and OpenAI already report into core.CostAttribution. Today the configured-cost fallback at internal/core/loop.go:303 prices only Input + Output; cache-read tokens are counted at full input price for cost attribution even though Anthropic/OpenAI charge ~10% / ~50% of base. Depends on bead A (agent-cccc2df7) for the CachePolicy="off" semantics.

Codex correctly diagnosed the wiring: manifest v4 already has CostCacheReadPerM and CostCacheWritePerM at internal/modelcatalog/manifest.go:44-45 (model entries) and :139-140 (target entries) with validation. CostAttribution already has CacheReadAmount and CacheWriteAmount at internal/core/agent.go:198-199. Telemetry emission already wires them at internal/core/loop.go:936-940. The only missing piece is: PricingFor at internal/modelcatalog/catalog.go:388 drops the cache fields when projecting to core.ModelPricing, and core.ModelPricing at internal/core/pricing.go:5 lacks the slots, so loop.go:303 has no source-of-truth to read.

Three coordinated edits:

1. internal/core/pricing.go:5 — add CacheReadPerM, CacheWritePerM float64 to core.ModelPricing.

2. internal/modelcatalog/catalog.go:388 (PricingFor) — preserve CostCacheReadPerM and CostCacheWritePerM from the manifest model/target onto the returned ModelPricing.

3. internal/core/loop.go:303 — extend the configured-cost computation to add cache-read and cache-write costs:
   computed := input/1e6 * InputPerMTok + output/1e6 * OutputPerMTok
            + cacheRead/1e6 * CacheReadPerM
            + cacheWrite/1e6 * CacheWritePerM
   Source the cache token counts from resp.Usage.CacheReadTokens and CacheWriteTokens. Populate CostAttribution.CacheReadAmount = &cacheReadCost and CacheWriteAmount = &cacheWriteCost (slots already there).

4. Honor CachePolicy="off" from bead A: when off, emit CacheReadAmount and CacheWriteAmount as explicit *float64(0.0), NOT nil. nil = harness/provider didn't report; explicit zero = caller asked for no caching. This matches the existing zero-vs-unknown convention from agent-a8cbdb87.

5. Update CONTRACT-003 prose: paragraph in the Cost Attribution / UsageReport section explaining that cache_read and cache_write tokens are priced at the manifest's CostCacheReadPerM/CostCacheWritePerM rates; nil cache amounts mean the harness or provider didn't report cache usage; explicit zero means caching was opted out via CachePolicy="off".

Historical session-log recalculation is OUT OF SCOPE per codex: existing logs cannot be re-priced reliably from UsageReport alone without changing semantics. New runs price correctly going forward; old logs keep their existing numbers.

In-scope files:
- internal/core/pricing.go (struct fields)
- internal/modelcatalog/catalog.go (PricingFor preservation)
- internal/core/loop.go (cost computation at line 303)
- internal/core/loop_test.go (or new file: cache-cost test cases)
- internal/modelcatalog/catalog_test.go (PricingFor preservation test)
- docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-003-ddx-agent-service.md (cost-semantics prose)

Out of scope:
- Anthropic cache_control writes (bead C)
- OpenAI prefix-stability test (bead B)
- UsageReport historical recalculation
- New CHANGELOG entry beyond what the bead's commit message produces
bead.acceptance:
1. core.ModelPricing has CacheReadPerM and CacheWritePerM float64 fields. AST-checked: TestModelPricingHasCacheFields.
2. PricingFor preserves cache fields: TestPricingForPreservesCacheFieldsFromManifest loads a manifest fixture with non-zero cost_cache_read_per_m / cost_cache_write_per_m on a model entry, calls PricingFor, asserts the returned ModelPricing carries those values verbatim.
3. Native loop populates cache cost: TestNativeLoopPopulatesCacheCostAttribution drives a fixture catalog through a virtual provider that emits resp.Usage.CacheReadTokens=1000 and CacheWriteTokens=500. Asserts CostAttribution.CacheReadAmount = (1000 / 1e6) * CostCacheReadPerM and CacheWriteAmount = (500 / 1e6) * CostCacheWritePerM, both as non-nil *float64.
4. CachePolicy=off semantics: TestCachePolicyOffZerosCacheAttribution drives the same loop with ServiceExecuteRequest.CachePolicy="off"; asserts CacheReadAmount and CacheWriteAmount are non-nil *float64 with value 0.0 (NOT nil).
5. CONTRACT-003 has the cost-semantics prose paragraph; doc-link grep test asserts the prose mentions "cost_cache_read_per_m", "explicit zero", and "opted out".
6. go test ./internal/core ./internal/modelcatalog -count=1 passes.
7. go test ./... -count=1 passes.
bead.notes:
`internal/core/loop.go:316` / `internal/core/loop.go:317` set `CacheReadAmount` and `CacheWriteAmount` pointers unconditionally during configured-cost computation, even when `resp.Usage.CacheRead == 0` and `resp.Usage.CacheWrite == 0`. That makes explicit zero mean both “no reported cache tokens under configured pricing” and `CachePolicy="off"`, contradicting the required convention that explicit zero identifies opt-out and nil means not reported/unknown.
`go test ./... -count=1` was not verifiable in this sandbox because multiple existing tests using `httptest.NewServer` panic on localhost bind with `socket: operation not permitted`; rerun in an environment that permits local test servers.

title: Instrument loop and tools with CONTRACT-001 spans
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>go-std | testing</concerns>
<practices>go fmt ./... before every commit · go test ./... and go test -race ./... for concurrent code · golangci-lint run · gosec ./... · govulncheck ./... · log with slog, wrap errors with context, and keep tests table-driven with testify</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 keeps JSONL replay separate from OTel analytics, preserves reported cost when available, uses exact runtime pricing only for known systems/models, and leaves cost unknown otherwise so replay fidelity and cross-tool analytics both survive.</adrs>
<governing>CONTRACT-001 span instrumentation — wire invoke_agent/chat/execute_tool spans into loop.go and preserve JSONL replay behavior; review hx-0b94868b keeps it after the root telemetry package and provider metadata work.</governing>
</context-digest>

Wire telemetry into loop.go so each run emits one invoke_agent span, each provider attempt emits one chat span with stable turn/attempt indexing, and each tool execution emits one execute_tool span with standard OTel error semantics. Preserve existing JSONL replay behavior while adding trace correlation and root/session rollups.
acceptance: loop.go emits invoke_agent/chat/execute_tool spans with required turn and attempt identity, tool failures use standard OTel error semantics, JSONL replay behavior remains unchanged, and tests cover turn indexing, retry indexing, and tool span error handling

title: Add benchmark-mode preset and non-interactive completion behavior
description: Add an explicit benchmark-mode prompt/preset or equivalent runtime profile for ddx-agent after the fixed eval set and baseline are defined. The goal is to bias non-interactive evaluation runs toward making reasonable assumptions, proceeding without clarification, and finishing with concrete outputs under harness conditions, while preserving task completion quality on the approved benchmark subset.
acceptance: DDX Agent exposes a benchmark-mode preset/profile that can be selected from CLI/config. On the committed non-interactive benchmark-mode eval set defined by agent-82042311, benchmark-mode runs reduce clarification-question outputs relative to the baseline artifact and meet or improve the resolved-task-rate floor defined in the benchmark spec. The eval fixture and scoring rules are checked in before this bead is considered complete.

title: routing: return structured no-candidate and attempted-route evidence
description: Expose enough structured route evidence for callers to own retry decisions. In-scope: RouteDecision/ServiceEvent/Result route evidence structs and tests. When routing fails, return hard constraints, requested power bounds, full rejected candidate trace, and typed reasons. When dispatch fails after a candidate is selected, report attempted harness/provider/endpoint/model plus availability/transport/protocol failure class. Do not add retry behavior.
acceptance: 1. No-candidate errors include requested Model/Provider/Harness/MinPower/MaxPower and candidate rejection reasons. 2. Dispatch failures include attempted route identity and typed availability/transport/protocol class. 3. Successful runs include selected route evidence with power/cost/speed score components when available. 4. Tests prove agent does not attempt candidate 2 after candidate 1 dispatch failure. 5. go test ./... -run 'RouteEvidence|NoCandidate|Attempted|NoRetry' passes or a documented narrower equivalent passes.

Category: integration
Severity: medium

The actual Bun CI remediation for hx-76c04de5 landed earlier in commit a5b8d2f0971cc4b9f99cdde438824261485f1655, which updates .github/workflows/ci.yml to use oven-sh/setup-bun, bun install, bunx playwright, and bun run test:e2e. FEAT-019 uses closing_commit_sha as replay/audit provenance, so hx-76c04de5 now points replay at unrelated metadata instead of the implementation that satisfied its acceptance criteria.
Suggested fix: Clear hx-76c04de5.closing_commit_sha if no isolated governing fix commit exists, or retarget it to the commit that actually applies the Bun CI change and document that implementation boundary explicitly.

title: Wire go-std quality gates into CI
description: Discovered by alignment review hx-0b94868b. The active go-std concern requires golangci-lint, gosec, and govulncheck as quality gates in CI or pre-commit configuration. The repo Makefile defines targets for those commands, but .github/workflows/ci.yml currently runs only build, go vet, format check, go test, and go test -race. title: correctness: preserve known root cost rollups across mixed known cost sources
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: loop.go:289
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new root-span cost rollup logic marks the invoke_agent span as unknown whenever known child chat spans disagree on ddx.cost.source, currency, or pricing_ref. That drops a valid total for mixed but fully known sessions, which violates CONTRACT-001's rule that the root cost only becomes unknown when a contributing chat span has unknown cost. Reproduced against the current code by running a two-turn session with a provider-reported first turn and an exact configured-cost second turn: result.CostUSD=0.03 while the root span still emits ddx.cost.source=unknown.
Suggested fix: Roll up root ddx.cost.amount whenever every contributing chat span has known cost, and only force ddx.cost.source=unknown when a contributing span is actually unknown. If metadata such as pricing_ref or source cannot be represented cleanly at the root, omit or summarize those fields rather than discarding the known total. Add a regression test covering mixed known-cost provenance across turns.
acceptance: A session whose chat spans all have known cost preserves a root invoke_agent ddx.cost.amount equal to the sum of child costs even when provenance differs across turns; the root span only emits ddx.cost.source=unknown when at least one contributing chat span has unknown cost; automated tests cover mixed provider-reported/configured cost rollups.

title: docs-corpus: add legacy routing docs grep check
description:
Add an enforceable docs-corpus guardrail for legacy DDx agent command/routing vocabulary. This is the final gate for ddx-02335e0b after active docs are scrubbed.

In-scope files:
- A new or existing docs lint/check script, Make target, or test file chosen to match repo conventions.
- A checked-in allowlist file for historical/deprecation references, if needed.
- Documentation of the check near the script/allowlist.

Required behavior:
- The check scans active docs for stale public surfaces: `ddx agent run`, `ddx agent execute-bead`, `ddx agent execute-loop`, `--profile`, `--quorum`, `--harnesses`, execution-path ResolveRoute/RouteDecision, profile_ladders, and model_overrides.
- The check fails unallowlisted hits.
- Allowlisted hits must include a reason such as historical archive, migration note, or explicit deprecation handler.

Out of scope:
- Scrubbing the specific active docs; sibling beads own that.
- Code routinglint for Go execution paths; separate implementation beads own that.
acceptance:

2. The check fails when a temporary unallowlisted `ddx agent run` reference is added to an active docs file.

4. `make skill-schema` and `ddx skills check cli/internal/skills/ddx .agents/skills/ddx .claude/skills/ddx` still pass if skill files are touched.

title: FEAT-008 P7: US-086a worker live stream + tool cards + reconnect
description: Subscribe to workerProgress via graphql-ws. Live region is a NAMED <section role=region aria-label='Live response'> containing an aria-live=polite inner log — bare role=log does NOT satisfy the test. Contiguous text_delta frames MUST concatenate into readable text in the DOM (two adjacent frames render as joined text, not separate nodes). tool_call frames → collapsible <details> cards interleaved with text. On reconnect: fetch worker(id).recentEvents to catch up; show reconnect banner while bridging. Terminal state: stop appending, render completed timestamp + 'Evidence bundle' link (link must be present even if mocked evidenceBundleUrl is missing — use worker id to synthesize the path).
acceptance: US-086a.a-c green in e2e/workers.spec.ts.

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: routing: effort/permissions gating per provider+model (not just per harness)
description:
## Provider-specific honoring of --effort and --permissions (sibling of ddx-4817edfd).

Today, Harness.EffortFlag is checked at routing.go:rejectIfNotViable, so harnesses without effort support are rejected when --effort is set. But provider-level gating is missing: e.g., openrouter doesn't honor --effort on non-reasoning models, and --permissions unrestricted may not apply to every harness+provider combination. Routing should reject or downgrade rather than dispatch and silently no-op the flag.

## Scope

- Add effort/permissions capability fields to the curated capability table (same file added by the tool-calling sub-bead, or co-locate here if that one ships first).
  - SupportsEffort tri-state per (provider, model).
  - SupportsPermission(permission_class) — at minimum 'unrestricted'.
- evaluateCandidate / rejectIfNotViable: when RouteRequest names an --effort or --permissions value not honored by the resolved (provider, model) combo, reject with a clear reason. Unknown = pass with -v warning.
- Document expected behavior for each known provider in capabilities.go comments (openrouter non-reasoning models = no effort, etc.).

## Out of scope

- Context-window, tool-calling, structured-output gating (sibling beads).
- Building a 'downgrade' path (e.g., silently strip --effort and continue). Rejection is sufficient for v1; downgrade is a follow-up.

## Files

- cli/internal/agent/routing.go
- cli/internal/agent/capabilities.go (shared with tool-calling bead — or new file if order differs)
- cli/internal/agent/types.go
acceptance:
1. Capability table tracks SupportsEffort and SupportsPermission per (provider, model_pattern).
2. evaluateCandidate rejects candidates that don't honor a RouteRequest's --effort or --permissions, with a debug reason that names which capability and which provider/model.
3. Unknown capability does NOT reject; logs at -v.
4. Unit tests cover: known-no-effort rejection, known-yes pass, unknown pass with warning, no-effort-requested disables the check (parallel structure for permissions).
5. go test ./cli/... is green.

title: correctness: preserve non-envelope JSON with kind+title
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cmd/ddx-agent/main.go:375
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: `isPromptEnvelopeProbe` now treats any JSON object with top-level `kind` plus `title` as a DDx prompt envelope candidate. That fixes malformed `{"kind":"prompt","title":"..."}` envelopes, but it also re-breaks backward compatibility for plain JSON prompts whose `kind` is not the DDx envelope discriminator. Repro: `go run ./cmd/ddx-agent -p '{"kind":"note","title":"hello"}' -provider nonexistent` now returns `invalid prompt envelope: kind, id, and prompt are required` instead of passing the JSON through as raw prompt text; the same regression occurs for stdin and `@file` because all three paths call `resolvePrompt` -> `parsePromptInput`. This violates the earlier plain-JSON compatibility requirement while over-matching objects that are not DDx prompt envelopes.\nSuggested fix: narrow the probe so `title` only contributes to envelope detection when the object is actually a DDx prompt envelope candidate, for example by requiring `kind == "prompt"` before `title` triggers validation; add regression coverage proving `{"kind":"note","title":"hello"}` stays raw text for inline, stdin, and `@file`, while malformed `{"kind":"prompt","title":"Inspect main"}` still fails validation.
acceptance: A plain JSON prompt like {"kind":"note","title":"hello"} remains raw prompt text for -p, stdin, and @file inputs, while malformed DDx prompt envelopes like {"kind":"prompt","title":"Inspect main"} still fail with an invalid prompt envelope error; regression tests cover both behaviors.

title: modelcatalog: bootstrap embedded model power values
description: Populate the embedded catalog after schema and provenance support exist. In-scope: internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml and catalog tests only. Assign power values for the small supported starter set: local/openai-compatible Qwen models known to this repo, subscription harness models exposed by Claude/Codex/Gemini discovery, and selected popular OpenRouter models already represented or explicitly added to the catalog. Mark older same-family models exact-pin-only unless there is an explicit cost/power override reason. Apply provider/deployment class rules so local/community/self-hosted models do not tie managed cloud frontier models solely from benchmarks.
acceptance: 1. Every embedded model intended for automatic routing has power in the agreed scale. 2. Models with no power or older same-family status are exact_pin_only unless an override reason is present. 3. The embedded catalog includes enough local, OpenRouter, Claude, Codex, and Gemini subscription entries to support the first --list-models inventory. 4. Catalog values demonstrate provider/deployment class separation: local/community gpt-oss/minimax-class entries, if present, do not share frontier power with cloud Sonnet/Opus/GPT without an explicit documented override. 5. A test fails if any target candidate intended for auto-routing lacks power. 6. go test ./internal/modelcatalog passes.

title: layer-2 design: should execute-bead workers create beads in-band?
description:
During investigation of ddx-7eab13a6 (bead-id resolver worktree bug), a design question surfaced: is it correct for execute-bead workers to call `ddx bead create` directly in-band?

Three alternatives:

(a) Append to parent bead. Worker appends discovered sub-tasks as structured items in the parent's notes or a new discovered-subtask event. The parent becomes the durable record; future runs or the operator can decompose.

(b) Surface via result. Agent emits a structured discovered_subtasks: [...] field in result.json; the execute-loop / operator decides whether to file new beads. Keeps tracker mutations gated by an explicit decision point.

(c) Create beads in-band (current behavior). Worker calls ddx bead create directly. Pro: fastest decomposition. Con: sub-tasks land in the queue without operator review; can flood the queue on over-decomposition.

Today's behavior is (c) but undocumented. One worker spawned 11 read-coverage children in a single run (ddx-44236615), all P0, without operator review. The work may be valid but the pattern needs a documented position.

Additionally, 22 existing beads have malformed IDs of the form .execute-bead-wt-<parent>-<timestamp>-<random>-<hex> (caused by the layer-1 bug fixed in ddx-7eab13a6). A mass-repair or close+refile decision should be made once the design position is set.

Scope: pick (a), (b), or (c) and document in a spec amendment (FEAT-006 or FEAT-010 if applicable); record the rationale. Also decide whether to repair the 22 existing malformed-ID beads.

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: Supported-models allow-list in routing inputs; gating rejects non-allowed pins even when ExactPinSupport is true
description:
## Confirmed by code inspection

`HarnessEntry` in `internal/routing/engine.go` has no field for the harness's supported-models allow-list. `internal/routing/gating.go:88` checks only `ExactPinSupport` when validating a model pin. `engine.go:456` `resolveModel` returns `req.Model` directly when no provider discovery is present. Result: any harness with `ExactPinSupport: true` and `Available: true` is eligible for **any** model, regardless of the allow-list declared in `internal/harnesses/registry.go`.

Concrete failure observed on a DDx consumer (2026-04-22): `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Model: "minimax/minimax-m2.7")` returned `harness: gemini` on a machine where gemini CLI was installed. Gemini's registry declares `Models: [gemini-2.5-pro, gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.5-flash-lite]` — explicitly excluding minimax. The engine ignored the allow-list because it never reached the routing layer. DDx consumer then invoked the gemini CLI with the minimax model; CLI rejected it; escalation retried 19 times; 19 bead claims burned.

## Scope

1. Extend `HarnessEntry` in `internal/routing/engine.go` with a `SupportedModels []string` field (nil = no allow-list / rely on provider-side validation).
2. Populate `SupportedModels` in `service_routing.go`'s `buildRoutingInputs` from each harness's `cfg.Models`.
3. In `internal/routing/gating.go`, add a gate: when `h.SupportedModels != nil` AND `req.Model != ""` AND `req.Model ∉ h.SupportedModels`, mark candidate ineligible with reason `"model not in harness allow-list"`. This gate applies **regardless** of `ExactPinSupport` — `ExactPinSupport: true` means "accepts exact pins within its allow-list," not "accepts anything."
4. For harnesses with `SupportedModels: nil` (e.g., any that genuinely proxy to any provider), behavior unchanged — rely on provider-side validation.
5. Fuzzy matching against provider's `DiscoveredIDs` continues to take precedence when the harness has a provider entry with live discovery.

## Regression test

Seed a routing.Inputs with:
- `gemini` harness, Available=true, ExactPinSupport=true, SupportedModels=[gemini-2.5-pro, gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.5-flash-lite]
- `agent` harness with a live endpoint whose DiscoveredIDs contains `minimax/minimax-m2.7`

`Resolve(Request{Profile: "default", Model: "minimax/minimax-m2.7"}, inputs)` must return `Decision{Harness: "agent", ...}`. Must **never** return gemini. Remove the `agent` entry and assert the engine returns `no viable candidate` typed error, not a gemini pick.

Add an inverse test: `Model: "gemini-2.5-flash"` must successfully pick gemini when it's the only viable candidate.
acceptance:
1. `HarnessEntry.SupportedModels []string` field added. Nil retains "no allow-list" semantics.
2. `buildRoutingInputs` populates `SupportedModels` from `cfg.Models` for every registered harness.
3. `gating.go` gate: `h.SupportedModels != nil && req.Model != "" && req.Model ∉ h.SupportedModels` → ineligible with explicit reason.
4. Gate applies regardless of `ExactPinSupport`. Test asserts `ExactPinSupport: true` + allow-list mismatch = ineligible.
5. Regression test (from description) passes and stays in suite as a named guard (e.g., `TestResolveRoute_GeminiRejectsNonGeminiModel`).
6. Inverse test passes: allow-list match with ExactPinSupport succeeds.
7. No existing `TestResolveRoute_*` or engine tests regress.
8. Live-discovery fuzzy-match path is NOT affected: when a harness has `Providers` with `DiscoveredIDs`, that matching takes precedence over the static allow-list for models served by the provider.
9. Rejection reason surfaces in `Candidate.Reason` so callers can log which gate failed.

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: docs: revise Windows reasoning plan after Claude review
description: Claude review of the Windows local-inference reasoning plan found several plan-level issues: the vLLM curl example uses SDK-only extra_body instead of the wire shape, the recommendation does not state vLLM/WSL2 hardware prerequisites, the LM Studio row understates current Bragi evidence, the capability matrix omits routing-critical columns (hardware/runtime prerequisites, model format, budget surface, tools-with-reasoning verification), the probe plan lacks explicit numeric thresholds even though the probe code already uses them, and the Ollama-vs-llama.cpp ordering is not justified. Revise the checked-in plan and related benchmark docs so the recommendation is technically defensible and matches current evidence. In-scope files: docs/research/windows-local-inference-reasoning.md, scripts/beadbench/README.md. Out-of-scope: changing runtime/provider code or the probe thresholds themselves.
acceptance: 1. The Windows reasoning plan states its optimization target and vLLM/WSL2 hardware/runtime preconditions. 2. The matrix includes hardware/runtime prerequisites, model format, budget surface, and tools_with_reasoning_verified. 3. LM Studio wording reflects current evidence as a per-model/template classification, not a weaker engine-wide unknown. 4. The live probe plan includes explicit numeric thresholds and distinguishes single-shot classification from evidence-grade promotion. 5. The vLLM example uses the actual wire shape rather than SDK-only extra_body, and the Ollama-vs-llama.cpp ordering is either justified or made provisional. 6. Any related README text stays consistent with the revised plan.

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: Add reporting and observability-resilience tests for usage output modes and best-effort failures
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Reporting surfaces are part of the contract, not optional polish · Best-effort observability semantics need end-to-end tests at the run and CLI boundary</principles>
<concerns>usage output formats | unknown-cost accounting | log/telemetry failure resilience</concerns>
<practices>Use seeded session logs for deterministic usage tests and temp dirs/mock exporters for failure-path tests · Prefer end-to-end assertions over unit-only coverage here</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 and CONTRACT-001</adrs>
<governing>FEAT-005 AC-FEAT-005-05 and AC-FEAT-005-06</governing>
</context-digest>

Telemetry conformance is well-covered, but the user-facing reporting and resilience edges are not. Add tests that:
- prove `ddx-agent usage` supports every documented output mode, including CSV, while preserving known-cost vs unknown-cost semantics and time-window filtering
- prove unwritable log directories or telemetry export failures do not abort a successful run, emit an operator-visible warning, and leave any partial artifacts readable
- where appropriate, assert that correlation metadata survives through both session start and session end so replay/usage tools can attribute runs consistently

Relevant files: `cmd/ddx-agent/usage_cli_test.go`, `session/logger_test.go`, `telemetry/telemetry_test.go`, and CLI/session integration helpers.
acceptance: Tests covering AC-FEAT-005-05 and AC-FEAT-005-06 exist and prove table, JSON, and CSV usage output, known-versus-unknown cost preservation, and best-effort behavior when logging or telemetry export fails; the CLI, session, telemetry, and default Go test suites pass.

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: Design the single-project execute-bead loop and supervisor contract
description:
<context-digest>
Review area: execute-bead automation and single-project worker supervision. Evidence covers FEAT-006, FEAT-010, SD-012, the execute-bead contract bead ddx-5448b39c, and the requirement to ship a tested single-project loop before broader multi-project or multi-host server work.
</context-digest>
Design the long-running single-project loop that watches for execution-ready beads and drives `ddx agent execute-bead` safely.

## Goals
1. Define a single-project loop that monitors one project queue for execution-ready beads using the generic DDx execution validator rather than HELIX-specific hidden policy
2. Define the exact state machine: claim/select bead, validate structural executability, execute in an isolated worktree, run post-run validity checks / required executions, classify outcome, and continue scanning
3. Make success and failure outcomes explicit: on failed execution or failed post-run validity checks, mark the bead/result accordingly and preserve the iteration/evidence exactly as the execute-bead contract specifies; on successful validation, land by rebase + fast-forward, reset the worker worktree to the updated branch tip, and continue
4. Keep the first shipped loop single-project scoped; do not require multi-project discovery, cross-project scheduling, or multi-host coordination in this bead
5. Define the minimal control surface for running the loop standalone or under `ddx server`, without coupling the design to later multi-project topology decisions

## Required outputs
- Single-project loop and worker state machine
- Failure/preservation semantics and retry surfaces
- Post-run validation / required-execution integration
- Minimal operator control surface and observability for the shipped loop
acceptance: The design stack defines a single-project loop that watches execution-ready beads, consumes the generic DDx execution validator, executes beads in isolated worktrees, runs required post-run checks, preserves failed iterations with explicit bead/result state, lands successful runs by rebase+fast-forward, and is explicitly scoped for first release without depending on multi-project or multi-host server behavior

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: correctness: preserve existing project plugin dir unless --force is set
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/install.go:282
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: When a local plugin manifest sets install.root.target under ~/.ddx/plugins/<name>, installLocal now unconditionally removes .ddx/plugins/<name> in the current project before creating the compatibility symlink. That bypasses the command's existing force guard and silently deletes any pre-existing project-local plugin checkout or files even when --force was not provided.
Evidence: Reproduced by creating an existing worktree directory at .ddx/plugins/sample-plugin with a sentinel file, then running ddx install sample-plugin --local <path> for a manifest rooted at ~/.ddx/plugins/sample-plugin. The command returned success, sentinel_exists=false, and the directory was replaced by a symlink.
Suggested fix: Apply the same collision policy used for the declared root target to the project-local compatibility path: only replace an existing entry when it is already a symlink or when --force is set, and add a regression test covering the non-force collision case.
acceptance: Given .ddx/plugins/<name> already exists as a real directory in the project, when ddx install <name> --local <path> installs a manifest whose root target is under ~/, then the command fails without --force and preserves the directory contents; with --force, it replaces the path with the compatibility symlink

rationale: closed focused bead with small acceptance surface
title: agent: populate correlation.bead_id in agent-logs/sessions.jsonl writer
description: SD-016 line 89 designates 'correlation.bead_id' as the canonical join key between sessions and beads. Verified on live sessions.jsonl: top-level bead_id and correlation are null. Process metrics cost aggregation (cli/internal/processmetrics/processmetrics.go) falls back to secondary key Extra[session_id], which is fragile and per-bead cost rollup is incomplete.
acceptance: All new rows in .ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl carry correlation.bead_id (when the session is bead-driven). cli/internal/processmetrics tests verify cost aggregation joins on bead_id. Backfill script (optional) documented.

bead.acceptance: All new rows in .ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl carry correlation.bead_id (when the session is bead-driven). cli/internal/processmetrics tests verify cost aggregation joins on bead_id. Backfill script (optional) documented.
bead.notes:
<verification>
Verification 2026-04-21: claim in AR-2026-04-20 (null bead_id in live sessions.jsonl) was a biased-sample artifact.

Evidence:
- Scanned ~/.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl (895 total records): 283 carry a non-nil correlation and ALL 283 carry correlation.bead_id. The remaining 612 are non-bead-scoped invocations (plain ddx agent run, doctor probes) which correctly have no bead_id to record.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:568-573 populates RunOptions.Correlation with bead_id, base_rev, attempt_id, session_id.
- cli/internal/agent/runner.go:880 copies opts.Correlation into SessionEntry.Correlation and writes to sessions.jsonl.
- All four call sites route through this path: runner.go, claude_stream.go, virtual.go, agent_runner_service.go.

Closing as already-correct. If processmetrics coverage needs to extend to additional session types (non-bead invocations attributed to beads out-of-band), that is scope for a narrower follow-up bead, not this one.
</verification>

title: P0 bug: server runWorker never calls LandBeadResult; commits silently lost
acceptance: Regression test in cli/internal/server/workers_test.go: starts a WorkerManager against a temp project (real git repo fixture), seeds one open bead, runs a worker whose agent-run produces a single commit, waits for runWorker to exit, asserts (a) git log on the project's target branch shows the worker's commit SHA, (b) the WorkerRecord.LastResult.ResultRev matches the SHA visible in git log, (c) ddx bead show returns status=closed with the commit SHA recorded as the closing session. Test must fail pre-fix and pass post-fix. The workers.go:runWorker closure at line 248 calls LandBeadResult + ApplyLandingToResult before returning from the executor. `ddx agent execute-loop --harness claude --model sonnet` (no --local) run against the ddx repo with one ready bead advances main by at least one commit, visible via git log main -1.

child_titles: Server runWorker: land commits to target branch + script-harness regression test
child_acceptance: New test TestRunWorker_ScriptHarness_LandsCommit in cli/internal/server/workers_test.go (or cli/internal/server/workers_land_test.go) that uses the script harness + integration helper to spawn one server-managed worker, waits for it to exit, and asserts: (1) record.LastResult.ResultRev matches `git log main -1 --format=%H` on the temp repo, (2) main has exactly one new commit from the initial seed, (3) the bead is closed with closing_commit_sha=record.LastResult.ResultRev, (4) no refs/ddx/iterations/ refs were created on the success path, (5) `git log --merges main | wc -l` is 0. Test must fail against current workers.go:runWorker (no LandBeadResult call) and pass after the fix. Fix is either (a) interim: add LandBeadResult + ApplyLandingToResult to the workers.go:248 closure using RealOrchestratorGitOps, or (b) canonical: route through the land coordinator from ddx-8746d8a6. Whichever is shipped, the test locks the contract: server-managed workers MUST advance the target branch. Runs under `go test ./internal/server -run RunWorker_ScriptHarness -race -count=1` in under 15s.

rationale: closed focused bead with small acceptance surface
title: Update library/personas/README.md with quality bar and cross-harness consumption contract
description:
Update library/personas/README.md to document the new 5-persona roster and the quality bar every shipped persona must meet.

Content to add/update:
1. **Cross-harness consumption contract** — explain that DDx, not the harness, consumes personas. DDx loads the persona body and injects it as a system-prompt addendum to the underlying ddx agent run invocation. Same persona works identically across claude, codex, gemini, etc.
2. **Quality bar** for shipped personas:
   - Opinionated voice — no generic tutorial summaries; personas must take positions
   - Portable content — no DDx-internal paths; must work for any downstream project
   - Traceable lineage — every persona ends with a Sources section citing external canon (Anthropic Prompt Library, OpenAI Cookbook, Superpowers, fspec, etc.)
   - Role binding verified — roles: field names a role actually referenced by some shipped workflow or skill
   - Schema conformance — frontmatter has name, roles, description, tags; body structure is Philosophy / Approach / Anti-patterns / Sources
3. **Shipped roster** — the 5 personas (code-reviewer, test-engineer, implementer, architect, specification-enforcer), one-line description each
4. **External canon** — primary canon (Anthropic Prompt Library, OpenAI Cookbook: Prompt Personalities), specific-use canon (Superpowers for two-stage review, fspec for spec discipline), demoted-to-inspiration (awesome-claude-skills)
5. **Extending with custom personas** — how a project adds its own personas (drop a file in .ddx/plugins/<plugin>/personas/ or library/personas/ locally); how bindings are set (ddx persona bind)

Cross-reference FEAT-006-agent-service.md Personas section (already contains the consumption contract).

In-scope:
- library/personas/README.md

Out-of-scope:
- Persona content itself (separate rewrite beads)
- Deprecation of dropped personas (separate bead)
- FEAT-006 edits (already done)
acceptance: library/personas/README.md exists and documents: cross-harness consumption contract (DDx injects, not the harness); 5 quality-bar requirements (opinionated voice, portable content, traceable lineage, role binding verified, schema conformance); the 5-persona target roster with one-line descriptions; external canon list (Anthropic Prompt Library, OpenAI Cookbook, Superpowers, fspec). Cross-references FEAT-006-agent-service.md Personas section.

title: exec: execute-loop --project flag to target a specific project when server is multi-project
description:
'ddx agent execute-loop' help says 'execute-loop scans the current project's execution-ready bead queue.' When submitting to a multi-project server, there is no way to say 'run this loop against project X'. The server picks whichever project is the current server context, ignoring the caller's CWD.

## Observed

From CWD /Users/erik/Projects/axon:

  $ ddx agent execute-loop --once --harness claude --model claude-sonnet-4-6 --json
  {
    "project_root": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx",  # WRONG
    "state": "running",
    "worker_id": "worker-20260415T011331-d0a4"
  }

I wanted the worker to pick an axon bead. The server ran the worker against the ddx repo instead. There is no --project flag, no env var, nothing in the CLI to redirect the server's project scope.

Currently the only way to target a specific project with execute-loop is --local mode (which spawns a subprocess using the caller's CWD as the project root). This defeats the purpose of having a server in a multi-project deployment.

## Scope

- --project <path> or --project <name> flag on execute-loop, execute-bead, and 'ddx agent run' that tells the server which project_root to use
- Env var fallback: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT or similar
- Default to CWD if unset and no flag
- Server refuses ambiguous/unmatched project names with a clean error
- Integration test: from CWD A, submit a loop targeting project B; assert worker runs in B's project_root

## Why this matters for the user's workflow

Without this, the ONLY way to drive multi-project execution from one shell is --local, which (a) loses the server's queue visibility, (b) ties the parent process to the child, and (c) negates the 'ddx agent execute-loop submits to server by default' ergonomic that execute-loop's --help promises.

## Acceptance

- [ ] --project flag wired through execute-loop/execute-bead/run
- [ ] Env var fallback documented
- [ ] Default behavior: CWD-based project detection
- [ ] Integration test for cross-project submission
- [ ] execute-loop --help updated to describe project targeting

title: Rewrite implementer persona with opinionated YAGNI/KISS stance
description:
Replace library/personas/pragmatic-implementer.md with library/personas/implementer.md. The existing file has a generic tutorial tone; rewrite with real opinions.

Core stances:
- YAGNI — implement only what is specified; no speculative features or "while we are here" additions
- KISS — simplest solution that meets requirements; avoid clever code and premature optimization
- DOWITYTD (Do Only What I Told You To Do) — stop when the task is complete
- Ship tests with code — implementations without a test command referenced in AC are half-done (but: test-first vs test-alongside vs test-after is a workflow choice, not mandated by this persona — see the test-engineer persona for the testing stance; this persona requires tests exist and pass, not a specific methodology)
- Do not design for hypothetical future requirements. Three similar lines is better than a premature abstraction.
- **Performance claims require baselines.** Do not add code "for performance" without a baseline measurement and a clear boundary describing what is being measured. "This is faster" without a number is not a justification. (Cross-reference test-engineer for the measurement discipline.)
- **Favor real e2e validation at the integration boundary.** When in doubt between a layer of abstraction/mocking and writing a real integration test, write the real integration test. The cost of mocks compounds; the cost of a real e2e test is paid once.

Body structure: Philosophy / Approach / Anti-patterns / Sources.

Anti-patterns to call out:
- Speculative abstraction (base classes, interfaces, config knobs for "someday")
- Refactoring unrelated code during a bug fix ("while we are here...")
- Adding error handling for scenarios that cannot happen (trust internal invariants; validate at system boundaries)
- Leaving TODO comments for problems that should block the current change
- Comments that restate what the code does (well-named identifiers already do that)
- Adding a mock or stub when a real integration test would be simpler and catch more bugs
- Making performance claims without baselines or boundaries

Sources:
- YAGNI principle (c2.com wiki, various Martin Fowler articles)
- Anthropic Prompt Library entries for coding agents, if any fit
- Relevant DDx system-prompt content from library/prompts/claude/system-prompts/focused.md (already documents YAGNI/KISS/DOWITYTD)
- Brendan Gregg on performance measurement discipline (baseline + boundary)

Frontmatter:
---
name: implementer
roles: [implementer, software-engineer]
description: Ships the specified scope, nothing more — YAGNI/KISS/DOWITYTD with tests included, real e2e favored over mocks, performance claims only with baselines
tags: [implementation, shipping, minimalism]
---

In-scope: library/personas/implementer.md (new)
Out-of-scope: Deleting pragmatic-implementer.md (deprecation bead); other persona rewrites (separate beads)
acceptance: File library/personas/implementer.md exists with valid frontmatter. Body has Philosophy / Approach / Anti-patterns / Sources sections. Philosophy section names YAGNI, KISS, and DOWITYTD by name and includes the "performance claims require baselines" stance and the "favor real e2e over adding mocks" stance. Anti-patterns section lists at least 5 concrete patterns (speculative abstraction, unrelated refactor during bug fix, error handling for impossible cases, comments restating code, performance claims without baseline). Sources section cites at least two external works. grep -E "cli/internal/|docs/helix/" library/personas/implementer.md returns no matches.

title: beadbench: avoid concurrent run report directory collisions
description:
Running multiple beadbench dry-run slices in parallel within the same second created the same benchmark-results/beadbench/run-YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ directory and raced on report/latest artifacts. This happened while validating expanded filters on 2026-04-23: parallel smoke, axon, rust-auth, and expensive dry-runs all wrote under run-20260423T034124Z. Parallel slicing is expected for the expanded matrix, so run directories must be unique even when invocations share a timestamp.

In-scope files:
- scripts/beadbench/run_beadbench.py
- scripts/beadbench/README.md

Out-of-scope:
- Changing report JSON schema beyond artifact path naming.
- Locking latest.json across processes.
acceptance:
1. Concurrent beadbench invocations started in the same second write distinct run directories.
2. Report paths include a uniqueness component beyond the UTC second timestamp.
3. README documents the new run directory pattern.
4. python3 -m py_compile scripts/beadbench/run_beadbench.py passes.
5. Two same-second dry-run invocations produce different report paths.

events.body: Verification on 2026-04-23: python3 scripts/beadbench/run_beadbench.py --dry-run --tier smoke --arm-tier core --limit-arms 2 wrote benchmark-results/beadbench/run-20260423T034154Z-3693848/report.json while a parallel axon dry-run wrote benchmark-results/beadbench/run-20260423T034154Z-3693865/report.json. Later smoke dry-run wrote benchmark-results/beadbench/run-20260423T035041Z-3707769/report.json. This satisfies the uniqueness requirement by adding the process id to run directory names.

rationale: closed focused bead with small acceptance surface
title: docs: reconcile ADR-005 power scale and retry boundary
description: Update the routing ADR after FEAT-004 is power-first and has dropped named providers/named routing profiles as target abstractions. In-scope: docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-005-smart-routing-replaces-model-routes.md only. Normalize the power scale, remove 10..100 examples, make ddx-agent --list-models the named inventory surface, and ensure retry is explicitly not an agent responsibility. The ADR should describe automatic routing from provider source/endpoint discovery plus catalog join; no complicated route rules in config; no named profile aliases such as cheap/fast/standard/smart/local/offline/air-gapped as target routing concepts; hard pins; exact-pin-only; provider/deployment class as an input to power; local/free preference when constraints are satisfied; and top-1 dispatch without broadening constraints.
acceptance: 1. ADR-005 uses the same 1..10 power scale and terminology as FEAT-004. 2. ADR-005 defines auto-routable vs exact-pin-only models. 3. ADR-005 names ddx-agent --list-models as the primary inventory CLI surface and reserves route-status for route explanation/status. 4. ADR-005 states exact model/provider-source/endpoint/harness pins never widen and no automatic retry/escalation occurs in agent. 5. ADR-005 defines provider/deployment class in power so local gpt-oss/minimax-class models cannot tie cloud frontier Sonnet/Opus/GPT solely because of one benchmark. 6. ADR-005 states the local/free preference invariant: when unpinned local/free candidates satisfy requested power, tools, context, and availability constraints, route to local/free before paid cloud. 7. ADR-005 states that routing config declares provider sources/endpoints only; automatic routing discovers models and chooses the best/cheapest viable model for requested power, or best/cheapest viable auto-routable model when no power is supplied. 8. ADR-005 scrubs named profile/power-band aliases such as cheap, fast, standard, smart, local, offline, and air-gapped from target routing semantics. 9. rg -n '10\.\.39|50\.\.79|power 52|available-models|candidate 2|silently widen|cheap|fast|standard|smart|air-gapped|offline|profile' docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-005-smart-routing-replaces-model-routes.md has no unresolved target-architecture wording.

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: service: automate primary harness quota cache refresh
description:
Claude and Codex automatic routing correctly consumes durable quota caches, but cache refresh is currently owned only by explicit HealthCheck calls. That leaves humans or external polling responsible for keeping caches fresh. Move ownership into the service: normal service lifecycle/activity should trigger throttled asynchronous refresh for primary subscription harnesses while foreground routing continues reading only durable cache state.

In-scope files: service.go, service_providers.go, service_routing.go, service_status/list harness tests, service provider tests.

Out-of-scope: synchronous live probes in ResolveRoute or promoting secondary harnesses.
acceptance:
1. New service instances and normal status/routing calls trigger best-effort asynchronous Claude/Codex quota refresh when cache is missing or stale, with throttle/cooldown so calls do not spawn repeated PTY probes.
2. ResolveRoute remains cache-only in the foreground: it does not block on live probes, and stale/missing cache still makes the primary harness ineligible until refresh succeeds.
3. Tests prove refresh is initiated automatically without explicit HealthCheck, and that repeated calls within the margin do not repeatedly probe.
4. go test ./... passes.

bead.notes: Implemented service-owned primary quota refresh. New services start a best-effort Claude/Codex refresh for missing/stale/incomplete caches and wait only the bounded startup window; ListHarnesses, RouteStatus, and ResolveRoute trigger debounced async refresh without blocking foreground routing; optional QuotaRefreshInterval enables server timer refresh. Added tests for automatic/throttled refresh, startup wait/timeout continuation, timer refresh, and ResolveRoute nonblocking behavior. Verified go test ./... passes.

title: test: migrate TestExecuteBeadNoChanges to real git + script harness
description:
Migrate cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_test.go TestExecuteBeadNoChanges() from fakeExecuteBeadGit + fakeAgentRunner to real git + script harness.

Pattern:
- Use cli/internal/agent/integration_helper_test.go newScriptHarnessRepo(t, beadCount) to build an isolated repo with real git.
- Use runGitInteg (from cli/internal/git/git_basic_test.go) for any supplemental git invocations, so GIT_* env is scrubbed.
- Replace fakeExecuteBeadGit with the RealLandingGitOps flow used in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go.
- Replace fakeAgentRunner with the script harness (cli/internal/agent/script.go) driven by a per-attempt directive file. When the test needs to simulate 'agent emits no commits' or 'agent emits a dirty worktree', encode that as script directives rather than a canned Result struct.
- Preserve every assertion — the point is that the test now exercises real git + real merge/preserve semantics, not that the surface shrinks.

Scope limit: ONLY this test. Do not migrate any other test in the same file; touching another test expands blast radius outside the bead.

Verification: cd cli && go test ./cmd -run '^TestExecuteBeadNoChanges$' -count=1 passes. No struct ending in -GitOps or -Runner that fakes the thing under test is referenced from this test after the migration.

Governing concern: concerns.md §testing 'no mocks, period' + 'never mock the thing you are testing'. Parent: ddx-d9df348d.
acceptance: 1) TestExecuteBeadNoChanges no longer references fakeExecuteBeadGit or fakeAgentRunner. 2) Test dispatches through RealLandingGitOps + newScriptHarnessRepo (or equivalent real-git harness). 3) cd cli && go test ./cmd -run '^TestExecuteBeadNoChanges$' -count=1 green. 4) No other test in agent_execute_bead_test.go is modified.

title: correctness: normalize closing_commit_sha to full git SHAs
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:6
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit fb9bdf1 closes ddx-7e88c022 with closing_commit_sha=4563fa7, an abbreviated SHA. The close store documents this field as the exact closing commit SHA when known, but manual close evidence is persisted verbatim. Replay later resolves closing_commit_sha through git rev-parse to choose the baseline commit, so storing a short SHA weakens audit and replay determinism as the repository grows and abbreviations can become ambiguous or invalid. In the current tracker, this reviewed row is one of only two abbreviated closing_commit_sha values; the durable convention elsewhere is full 40-character SHAs.
Suggested fix: Normalize ddx bead close --commit input through git rev-parse before storing it, repair ddx-7e88c022 to the full 40-character 4563fa77d88cfb0f242925a927e913841b43868c value, and add regression coverage for manual close provenance normalization.
acceptance: Closing a bead with 'ddx bead close <id> --commit <short-sha>' stores the full 40-character commit SHA in bead evidence; ddx bead show ddx-7e88c022 --json reports closing_commit_sha=4563fa77d88cfb0f242925a927e913841b43868c; and the relevant bead close acceptance tests pass.

title: correctness: define canonical project-scoped FEAT-002 routes
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-002-server.md:65
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: FEAT-002 now introduces a canonical multi-project contract in the new Project Registry and Routing section (`GET /api/projects`, `GET /api/projects/:project`, explicit selection rules, and unscoped routes as compatibility aliases only), but the numbered functional requirements still define only legacy unscoped `/api/...` endpoints and unqualified MCP tool names for documents, beads, doc graph, agent sessions, executions, bead mutations, and document writes. An implementation can satisfy the formal requirements without ever exposing the canonical `/api/projects/:project/...` resource families or explicit project-selection semantics that SD-019 and bead ddx-efd858c4 require.
Suggested fix: Thread project scoping through the FEAT-002 requirements for every affected surface, or add one explicit normative rule that all project-aware server routes and MCP tools are scoped by a selected project context while unscoped forms remain singleton compatibility aliases only.
acceptance: FEAT-002 explicitly defines the canonical project-scoped route family and project-selection rule for documents, beads, doc graph, agent sessions, executions, bead mutations, and document-write/history surfaces; unscoped aliases are documented only as singleton compatibility behavior

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: integration: compaction ignores system prompt tokens in Run continuity path
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review of commit 7e225853fcb708575f478050865d4eb9e0322c87.
File: loop.go:60; loop.go:109; loop.go:174
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The new continuity refactor removes `SystemPrompt` from the persisted `messages` slice and only prepends it to `providerMessages` immediately before each provider call. `req.Compactor` still runs against `messages`, so its token estimate and cut-point decisions no longer include the system prompt even though the next provider request does. In long sessions with a large system prompt or AGENTS/developer wrapper, compaction can now decide that the history fits when the actual provider request exceeds the configured context budget. SD-006 explicitly requires the loop to keep the system prompt out of persisted history while still treating it as part of the live request prefix and context-window math. Current tests cover continuity and resumed compaction state, but none exercise compaction with a non-empty `SystemPrompt`, so this regression is not caught.
Suggested fix: Include the system prompt in compaction token accounting without persisting it into `Result.Messages`/`Request.History`, and add a regression test that runs `Run` with both `SystemPrompt` and `Compactor` enabled and verifies compaction triggers based on the full provider request size rather than the history slice alone.
acceptance: A Run configured with both SystemPrompt and Compactor accounts for the system prompt when deciding whether to compact; a regression test demonstrates compaction still triggers when the history alone is under budget but the provider request (system prompt + history) exceeds the context limit; Result.Messages remains free of duplicated system messages.

title: execution: port provider_deadline.go semantics into internal/execution/
description:
Port /Users/erik/Projects/ddx/cli/internal/agent/provider_deadline.go (deadline wrapping logic) into agent internal/execution/. Three timeout knobs (Timeout, IdleTimeout, ProviderTimeout) all enforced internally per CONTRACT-001.

Existing wrapProviderWithDeadlines / timeoutProvider / streamingTimeoutProvider patterns get rebuilt as internal middleware around the provider call sites. ErrProviderRequestTimeout becomes an internal error that the routing engine sees for failover classification.

Tests (provider_deadline_test.go, 188 lines) port to assert against the new internal API — likely a mechanical rewrite, not verbatim.
acceptance:
- [ ] internal/execution/deadline.go (or equivalent) implements wall-clock + idle + per-request deadlines
- [ ] All three timeout knobs are independently configurable per Execute call
- [ ] ErrProviderRequestTimeout (or successor) classified as failover-eligible by routing engine
- [ ] provider_deadline_test.go cases pass against the new code

title: correctness: vendor/ skip may be too aggressive for vendored Go projects
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: tool/glob.go:16 and tool/grep.go:90
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The skipDirs map unconditionally skips vendor/ during WalkDir traversal. Go projects using 'go mod vendor' store dependencies in vendor/ and may need to search them. While this matches default behavior of tools like ripgrep, downstream users of this library may need an override mechanism.
Suggested fix: Consider adding a configurable skip list (e.g. via GlobTool/GrepTool struct field) that defaults to the current skipDirs but can be overridden. Alternatively, document the limitation and accept it as a deliberate trade-off matching standard tool behavior.
acceptance: Either: (a) GlobTool and GrepTool accept an optional ExcludeDirs field that overrides skipDirs, with tests; OR (b) documented as intentional in tool schema descriptions, with a decision recorded

title: refactor(svelte): migrate to houdini@next or commit to STACK.md Option A/B before Stage 4.12
description:
Opus Stage 3 batch review (2026-04-15): Houdini 1.5.10 has unresolved peer-dep incompatibility with vite 8 + kit 2.57. STACK.md recommended Option A (downgrade vite) or Option B (drop Houdini for graphql-request). Stage 3.4 shipped with neither — works for now because the single NodeInfo query is trivial.

Stage 4.12 Workers page requires graphql-ws subscriptions (workerProgress). Houdini 1.5.10 subscription support on Vite 8 is the most likely place the peer-dep workaround falls apart.

Decision required BEFORE Stage 4.12: either (a) migrate to houdini@next (removes the 'upgrade to houdini@next' noise and fixes Vite 8 support), or (b) drop Houdini entirely for graphql-request + graphql-ws (lower abstraction, no codegen).
acceptance: (1) Decision recorded in STACK.md. (2) Either houdini@next installed and regen passes, or graphql-request + graphql-ws wired up with equivalent code paths. (3) bun run build succeeds without peer-dep warnings. (4) Stage 4.12 workerProgress subscription round-trips in a smoke test.

title: Remove lossy agent_runner mirror from .ddx/config.yaml and close env-var gap in native config path
description:
Remove DDx's lossy embedded-agent config mirror and close the env-var forwarding gap in the native config path.

## Context

FEAT-006 defines native `.agent/config.yaml` as the authoritative config source for the embedded harness. The current `.ddx/config.yaml` `agent_runner` section is a deprecated mirror that duplicates native runtime config fields. Its precedence semantics are documented in FEAT-006 "Embedded Harness Configuration Ownership".

## Goals

- Remove `AgentRunnerConfig` type and `agent_runner` YAML fields from `.ddx/config.yaml` schema once no project uses it
- Remove `AgentYAMLConfig`, `LLMPresetYAML` mirror types from `cli/internal/agent/runner.go`
- Remove the `AgentConfigLoader` injection hook and `resolveAgentConfig` fallback path from `agent_runner.go`
- Apply `AGENT_*` env vars (`AGENT_MODEL`, `AGENT_PROVIDER`, `AGENT_BASE_URL`, `AGENT_API_KEY`, `AGENT_PRESET`) when resolving from native config, closing the gap documented in `TestConfigPrecedenceEnvVarAppliesInDdxFallbackPath`
- Wire the `agentRunner()` cmd factory to pass a working-directory path to native config resolution instead of loading the `agent_runner` block
- Update `TestConfigPrecedenceEnvVarAppliesInDdxFallbackPath` to verify env vars work in the native path after the fix

## Acceptance Criteria

- `go build ./...` and `go test ./...` pass with no regressions
- `.ddx/config.yaml` no longer accepts or uses `agent_runner` fields
- `AGENT_MODEL` set in the environment overrides the model resolved from native `.agent/config.yaml`
- Existing `.ddx/config.yaml` files with `agent_runner` blocks emit a deprecation warning or are silently ignored (no crash)
- The migration path in FEAT-006 remains valid
acceptance: agent_runner mirror removed from DDx config schema; AGENT_* env vars apply in native config path; all tests pass
