title: Phase 5: migrate agent_cmd.go + server/workers.go routing to service.ResolveRoute
description:
Discovered while preparing the file-delete beads (ddx-f0cab7b3 etc.) — there are unplanned legacy-routing callers blocking the deletion of routing.go from DDx:

cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go uses agent.NormalizeRouteRequest + agent.RankCandidates + agent.SelectBestCandidate + runner.ProbeAndBuildCandidatePlans at lines 304, 321-323, 1508-1511, 1543-1558.

cli/internal/server/workers.go uses the same primitives at lines 461-475.

Both should migrate to service.ResolveRoute (per CONTRACT-003 — single routing engine consolidated in agent-1a486c2e). After this lands the file-delete beads (ddx-ea7f041c routing.go, ddx-f0cab7b3 state+registry, ddx-70c48576 routing observability, ddx-1bf6c0df tier_escalation, ddx-2912e7cd discovery+deadline+probe) become unblocked.
acceptance:
- [ ] cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go has zero references to NormalizeRouteRequest/RankCandidates/SelectBestCandidate/BuildCandidatePlans/ProbeAndBuildCandidatePlans
- [ ] cli/internal/server/workers.go same
- [ ] Both call service.ResolveRoute (or service.Execute with bare profile/model-ref) and consume RouteDecision
- [ ] All affected tests pass
- [ ] DDx builds clean

Fresh-eyes review complete for commit 91f17a05b054b92d6d2b883bb4563e18fc2ce4d8.
Finding filed: hx-03e23ec9.
Pass 1 correctness: the patch fixes the original metadata-only review-close bug, but it broadens suppression too far by omitting `closing_commit_sha` for every review-labeled bead rather than only metadata-only review closes.
Pass 2 integration: `ddx agent replay` and audit flows depend on `closing_commit_sha` as the original implementation boundary. With the new label-only check, non-metadata review beads that govern real docs/code changes lose exact provenance even when the caller provides `--commit` or the close lands with real staged work.
Pass 3 concern-aware quality: the change stays within the declared Go/test stack and adds acceptance coverage, but the new behavior violates FEAT-019 replay semantics and Make Intent Explicit by conflating review labels with metadata-only close behavior.
Pass 4 operational learnings: no AGENTS.md change required; no learnings bead required.
Verification: reread AGENTS.md; loaded active principles and concerns; inspected bead hx-ae141d75; reviewed the last-commit diff plus surrounding close-path code in cli/cmd/bead.go and store semantics in cli/internal/bead/store.go; cross-checked FEAT-019 replay requirements and FEAT-004 custom-field/provenance rules; ran `cd cli && go test ./cmd -run 'TestBeadCommandsClose(OmitsTrackerOnlyClosingCommitSha|OmitsReviewBeadClosingCommitSha|RecordsClosingCommitForMixedClose)' -count=1`; built `/tmp/ddx-review-bin`; and reproduced that `ddx bead close --commit <sha>` records `closing_commit_sha` for a normal bead but omits it for an otherwise identical `action:review` bead.
No AGENTS.md update required.

title: Step 4b — matrix-aggregate subcommand
description: Add cmd/bench/matrix_aggregate.go (or ddx-agent-bench matrix-aggregate). Reads <out>/ produced by NEW10 and emits matrix.json (raw cells), matrix.md (reward grid mean +/- SD over reps + per-task pass count + costs), costs.json (input/output/cached/retried tokens × profile pricing -> $). Null handling per v7 D4: nulls are dropped from means (denominator shrinks), reported as n/a in matrix.md, n_reported emitted alongside. Aggregator is unit-tested against a fixture matrix that includes nulls and budget_halted runs.
acceptance: matrix.json + matrix.md + costs.json produced in v7-documented schemas; aggregator unit-tested with a fixture matrix; null handling and budget_halted accounting verified.

rationale: closed focused bead with small acceptance surface
title: correctness: move semantic failures out of route feedback
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-003-ddx-agent-service.md:1313 and docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-003-ddx-agent-service.md:1371
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: CONTRACT-003 says DDx reports semantic task outcomes through RecordRouteAttempt and that model-quality/task-failure contributes to model/harness reliability for future ranking. The clarified boundary is that agent does not track semantic success/failure directly. Agent only reports availability/network/protocol-level failure. Semantic task outcomes belong to the caller and may be contributed to the model catalog or catalog-derived power rating, not to agent's transient route feedback store.
Suggested fix: Remove model-quality/task-failure from RouteAttempt route-health semantics, redefine RecordRouteAttempt or a separate catalog-feedback mechanism for caller-provided semantic evidence, and document how that evidence affects catalog power ratings outside the agent dispatch loop.
acceptance: CONTRACT-003 no longer states that semantic task failures directly demote route candidates in agent; docs distinguish provider availability feedback from caller-owned semantic catalog feedback; failure classes exclude model-quality/task-failure from agent route-health penalties

bead.acceptance: CONTRACT-003 no longer states that semantic task failures directly demote route candidates in agent; docs distinguish provider availability feedback from caller-owned semantic catalog feedback; failure classes exclude model-quality/task-failure from agent route-health penalties
bead.notes: Incorporated in docs: semantic task failures are caller/catalog evidence, not agent route-health feedback. RecordRouteAttempt is limited to availability outcomes for externally routed work; agent route health tracks provider/endpoint availability only.

title: land safety: post-land build gate reverts failed automatic lands
description:
Parent: ddx-efe058be. Add the final safety net: after an automatic land advances the target ref, run the project-level post-land verification command from .ddx/config.yaml or a conservative default, and automatically revert the local target ref if the command fails. Preserve the result under refs/ddx/iterations for operator review.

In-scope files:
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go and tests, or server worker landing wrapper if that is the correct ownership point
- config schema/types if a post-land check command must be introduced

Out-of-scope:
- Implementing every project-specific build command
- Changing execute-bead post-run checks before land
- Remote force-push behavior; never force-push.
acceptance:
1. A regression test configures a failing post-land command and verifies the target branch is restored to its pre-land SHA.
2. The result is preserved under refs/ddx/iterations with a reason naming the failed post-land gate.
3. A passing command leaves the target branch advanced.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run 'TestLand_.*PostLand' passes.

title: bug: embedded agent stalls in post-commit compaction loop
description:
The embedded 'agent' harness can complete a task successfully (commit produced, tests pass) but then fail to terminate, getting stuck in a compaction retry loop with compaction always failing. The parent execute-loop never observes task_succeeded and hangs forever.

## Observed

Running 'ddx agent execute-loop --once --local --harness agent --model qwen/qwen3-coder-next' against axon-43ed769b (axon auth-core-types bead):

1. qwen3-coder-next ran the task correctly, producing commit 91d4bdb with 636 LOC of types + 109 green tests + clippy clean. The commit was present in the worktree at /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-axon-43ed769b-20260415T005652-ebe7a16a.

2. Instead of terminating, the agent kept generating events. After ~20 minutes the session log was 33 MB (8461+ events, 249 messages) and stuck in:

    {"type":"compaction.start",...}
    {"type":"compaction.end",...,"data":{"messages_after":249,"messages_before":249,"no_compaction":true,"success":false}}
    {"type":"compaction.start",...}
    {"type":"compaction.end",...,"data":{"messages_after":249,"messages_before":249,"no_compaction":true,"success":false}}
    ...

3. Parent ddx process in __futex_wait. Had to SIGTERM the parent (PID 4141811) and manually fast-forward-merge the worktree commit to land the work.

4. A1 (previous bead) ran the same harness/model and terminated correctly in ~14 min with identical-looking commit shape. The difference was bead size: A2's output was a single 633-line Rust file, vs A1's 350-line SQL + 290-line test file. A2 didn't exceed what A1 did — so the context-limit explanation doesn't fully account for it.

## Expected

- After the agent commits and has nothing more to do, it should emit task_succeeded and terminate
- Compaction should either succeed (messages_after < messages_before) or bail out cleanly, not retry indefinitely
- If the context genuinely cannot be compacted, the agent should report a structured failure and preserve the commits, not spin

## Scope

- Detect mid-turn compaction returning success=false repeatedly and bail with a structured 'compaction_stuck' status
- Bound the total number of compaction attempts in a single turn
- Ensure task termination happens immediately after a successful commit + no pending tool calls
- Add a watchdog at the execute-loop level: kill the child if no commit+progress event is observed within a budget

## Evidence

- Session log: /Users/erik/Projects/axon/.ddx/executions/20260415T005652-ebe7a16a/embedded/agent-1776214612561376672.jsonl (33 MB)
- Rescued commit: 91d4bdb on axon master
- Parent process: PID 4141811 (killed)

## Acceptance

- [ ] Compaction retry loop is bounded and fails structurally
- [ ] Post-commit termination path is deterministic
- [ ] Watchdog detects and terminates stuck sessions with a preserved-commit outcome
- [ ] Regression test reproduces the stuck state (seeded session with large no-op tool calls)

events.summary: Upstream native fix requested at github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent: bead agent-e5228424 (compaction loop native stuck detection). DDx circuit breaker is defense-in-depth; can be removed once upstream fix lands.
events.body:
The DDx-side circuit breaker in commit 604151773f9b905346ee95b051e024a02f850cf0 is a caller-layer workaround. The root cause (compaction driver producing 8461+ no-op events without a native bound) belongs upstream in github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent.

Filed upstream bead agent-e5228424 in /home/erik/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl with:
- The bug description
- Pointer to DDx workaround commit as defense-in-depth
- Requested native fix (bounded compaction attempts + structured ErrStuck)
- Regression test requirement (seeded stuck-state reproduction)

When the upstream fix lands and DDx bumps the agent library version, the caller-side circuit breaker can be removed.

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: execute-loop: push_failed should NOT set a 1-year cooldown — race-with-remote is recoverable
description:
Today, when execute-bead lands locally but push fails (e.g. non-fast-forward because remote advanced), the loop sets execute-loop-retry-after to ONE YEAR in the future. This is wrong on multiple axes:

1. Push race is fundamentally recoverable: pull/merge/push is the standard fix and takes seconds.
2. A 1-year cooldown means the bead never re-runs in any reasonable working horizon.
3. The work is already done locally — the bead is functionally complete; reopening it for a year is misleading.

Observed in this session on ddx-98e6e9ef (routing-preflight-gate): worker landed commit 978131c6, push rejected because origin/main had advanced (687fb4ca + 4a6c6e8a pushed remotely between the local land and the push). Loop set retry_after=2027-04-29T03:43:59Z. Operator had to manually merge, push, and clear the cooldown.

What is needed:

1. push_failed should auto-pull/merge/retry-push at least once before giving up. The merge case where origin advanced with non-overlapping work is the common case in a multi-worker / multi-operator setup.

2. If the auto-merge has conflicts that the loop cannot resolve, push_failed should set a SHORT cooldown (5-15 min), not 1 year. The operator just needs time to resolve the conflict.

3. push_failed-with-conflicts should mark the bead as needing-human-review (a structured outcome similar to declined_needs_decomposition from ddx-fba752b9), not as a generic execution failure.

4. The loop's cooldown duration should NEVER exceed some reasonable cap (e.g. 24h) for any outcome. Year-scale cooldowns mean 'never' and that should be a deliberate operator choice via 'ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...', not an automatic loop decision.
acceptance: 1. push_failed flow attempts auto pull --rebase / merge / retry-push at least once before exiting with push_failed. 2. push_failed cooldown caps at 24h regardless of underlying error. 3. push_failed-with-conflicts (auto-merge fails) emits a structured outcome distinct from generic execution_failed; bead is parked for human review with the conflict context recorded as a structured event (kind:push-conflict). 4. Regression test: a fake git that returns non-fast-forward on first push, then fast-forward after pull/merge, exercises the new flow and asserts the bead lands cleanly without operator intervention. 5. Regression test: a fake git that produces an unresolvable merge conflict exercises the structured-outcome path and asserts cooldown <= 24h. 6. CHANGELOG entry. 7. Update ddx-fba752b9's CHANGELOG section (or a new section) noting that the 24h cap applies to all loop-set cooldowns, not just push failures.

bead.title: execute-loop: push_failed should NOT set a 1-year cooldown — race-with-remote is recoverable
bead.description:
Today, when execute-bead lands locally but push fails (e.g. non-fast-forward because remote advanced), the loop sets execute-loop-retry-after to ONE YEAR in the future. This is wrong on multiple axes:

1. Push race is fundamentally recoverable: pull/merge/push is the standard fix and takes seconds.
2. A 1-year cooldown means the bead never re-runs in any reasonable working horizon.
3. The work is already done locally — the bead is functionally complete; reopening it for a year is misleading.

Observed in this session on ddx-98e6e9ef (routing-preflight-gate): worker landed commit 978131c6, push rejected because origin/main had advanced (687fb4ca + 4a6c6e8a pushed remotely between the local land and the push). Loop set retry_after=2027-04-29T03:43:59Z. Operator had to manually merge, push, and clear the cooldown.

What is needed:

1. push_failed should auto-pull/merge/retry-push at least once before giving up. The merge case where origin advanced with non-overlapping work is the common case in a multi-worker / multi-operator setup.

2. If the auto-merge has conflicts that the loop cannot resolve, push_failed should set a SHORT cooldown (5-15 min), not 1 year. The operator just needs time to resolve the conflict.

3. push_failed-with-conflicts should mark the bead as needing-human-review (a structured outcome similar to declined_needs_decomposition from ddx-fba752b9), not as a generic execution failure.

4. The loop's cooldown duration should NEVER exceed some reasonable cap (e.g. 24h) for any outcome. Year-scale cooldowns mean 'never' and that should be a deliberate operator choice via 'ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...', not an automatic loop decision.
bead.acceptance: 1. push_failed flow attempts auto pull --rebase / merge / retry-push at least once before exiting with push_failed. 2. push_failed cooldown caps at 24h regardless of underlying error. 3. push_failed-with-conflicts (auto-merge fails) emits a structured outcome distinct from generic execution_failed; bead is parked for human review with the conflict context recorded as a structured event (kind:push-conflict). 4. Regression test: a fake git that returns non-fast-forward on first push, then fast-forward after pull/merge, exercises the new flow and asserts the bead lands cleanly without operator intervention. 5. Regression test: a fake git that produces an unresolvable merge conflict exercises the structured-outcome path and asserts cooldown <= 24h. 6. CHANGELOG entry. 7. Update ddx-fba752b9's CHANGELOG section (or a new section) noting that the 24h cap applies to all loop-set cooldowns, not just push failures.

title: Migrate TC-001 dashboard tests to fixture node/project routes
description:
## Context

TC-001 in `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/app.spec.ts` still assumes that visiting `/` renders a Dashboard `h1`. That is stale. Since Stage 3.8 commit `3bf655cf`, `src/routes/+page.svelte` redirects to `/nodes/<node-id>` when a node is selected, and the app's concrete project views live under `/nodes/<fixture-node-id>/projects/<fixture-project-id>/...`. `e2e/navigation.spec.ts` already contains the route shape to mirror around line 177.

After the fixture-backed Playwright harness exists, rewrite only the TC-001 Dashboard tests so they navigate through the fixture node/project route and assert the current UI behavior. Keep the test intent: dashboard-level smoke coverage should prove the shell and expected project entry points render, but it must not depend on `/` being a Dashboard page.

## In-scope files

- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/app.spec.ts` — TC-001 block only, currently around lines 9-57.

## Out-of-scope

- `cli/internal/server/frontend/playwright.config.ts` and `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/fixtures/`; those belong to the harness prerequisite.
- TC-002, TC-003, TC-005, TC-007, and TC-008 in `e2e/app.spec.ts`.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/navigation.spec.ts` except as a read-only reference for route patterns.
- Frontend route/component changes under `src/routes/`.
- Test deletion, `.skip`, or comment-out changes to make the suite pass.

## Rollback / cleanup

Keep TC-001 present and meaningful. If fixture IDs change during the harness bead, align TC-001 to the fixture constants or helpers rather than hard-coding unrelated local state.

Replace the manual Codex session-path dependency with stable auto-discovery of native session state so DDx can read current quota and headroom without per-shell configuration.
acceptance: Codex current quota and headroom are discovered automatically from the local native session or state store; agent doctor --routing and agent usage report them without requiring manual env configuration; and regression tests cover discovery, parsing, and stale or missing-state behavior.

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: Visibility 5: ddx agent check [--provider X] — runtime availability
description:
Add a new DDx CLI subcommand `ddx agent check [--provider X]`. Semantic distinction from existing `ddx agent doctor`: doctor answers "is my config failed?" (config validation, malformed YAML, missing API keys); check answers "what agents can I use right now?" (runtime liveness, which providers respond, which models are currently available). Both commands coexist.

Implementation lives in cli/cmd/agent_check.go. Mirrors ddx-agent's `cmdCheck` — probes each configured provider's /v1/models endpoint, reports reachability and current model inventory, exits 0 if at least one provider is reachable and has at least one usable model, exits 1 otherwise. Uses the Go package, not shell-out.
acceptance: (1) `ddx agent check` reports runtime liveness for every configured provider, not config sanity. (2) `ddx agent check --provider vidar` probes only vidar and reports result. (3) `ddx agent doctor` still exists and is about config/registry health, NOT provider liveness (semantic separation preserved). (4) Exit code 0 when at least one provider is reachable and has at least one model; 1 otherwise. (5) `go build ./...` passes. (6) Manual: stop lmstudio on vidar temporarily, run `ddx agent check --provider vidar`, see explicit unreachable status; restart, run again, see connected + model count.

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: service: surface Codex usage from DDx-owned session logs
description:
Improve operator visibility into Codex usage using DDx-owned session logs, not private Codex sessions. The public HarnessInfo type already has UsageWindows, but Codex does not populate it. Claude/Codex review agreed that ~/.codex/sessions should be used only as fresh quota/status evidence, not as general historical analytics. Historical usage should come from DDx's own session logging and internal/session.AggregateUsage.

Required behavior:
- Add a service-side usage status path that can populate Codex-related UsageWindows from DDx-owned session logs when a log directory is configured/known.
- Use internal/session.AggregateUsage or a narrow shared helper rather than reading ~/.codex/sessions for historical usage.
- Report input/output/total/cache/reasoning tokens and known/unknown cost status only where DDx final events contain that data.
- Preserve privacy: do not read private Codex session files, raw prompts, responses, or tool payloads for this status surface.
- Keep this as status/reporting only; it must not affect routing eligibility or quota refresh.

In-scope files:
- service.go if UsageWindow fields need helper population
- service status/list harness implementation files
- internal/session usage helpers if needed
- service status/list harness tests
- cmd/agent usage/status output tests only if CLI output changes

Out-of-scope:
- Parsing ~/.codex/sessions for historical usage.
- Changing quota routing or refresh.
- Adding provider price/cost estimates where final events report unknown cost.
acceptance:
1. `go test . ./internal/session -run 'Usage|Harness|Status|ListHarnesses' -count=1` passes with fixtures proving Codex UsageWindows are derived from DDx-owned session logs.
2. Tests prove private Codex session directories are not read for HarnessInfo.UsageWindows.
3. Tests prove usage reporting is status-only and does not affect routing inputs or quota eligibility.
4. `go test ./...` passes.

rationale: closed focused bead with small acceptance surface
title: Wire Makefile copy-skills target and update embed.go for single ddx skill
description:
Go embed patterns cannot traverse upward out of the package directory. //go:embed all:../../skills/ddx is illegal and will fail at go build. Commit to copy-at-build approach.

Steps:
1. Add Makefile target copy-skills in cli/Makefile that rsyncs skills/ddx/ into cli/internal/skills/ddx/ before every build. Example:
     copy-skills:
         rsync -a --delete ../skills/ddx/ ./internal/skills/ddx/
2. Wire copy-skills as a prerequisite of make build, make test, make dev (and any other targets that invoke go build or go test).
3. Update cli/internal/skills/embed.go to replace the current multi-dir embed line with: //go:embed all:ddx
4. Delete cli/internal/skills/ddx-{bead,agent,doctor,install,status,review,run}/ — the new embed picks up the copied ddx/ tree instead. (Deletion of top-level /skills/ddx-* is Phase 2; this bead only touches cli/internal/skills/.)
5. Commit cli/internal/skills/ddx/ as a tracked copy so contributors who run go build directly (without make) still get a valid binary. Add a pre-commit hook (lefthook.yml) that re-runs make copy-skills if skills/ddx/ is dirty and cli/internal/skills/ddx/ is stale; this keeps the copy in sync without manual intervention.

In-scope files:
- cli/Makefile
- cli/internal/skills/embed.go
- cli/internal/skills/ddx/ (committed copy — gets populated by copy-skills)
- lefthook.yml (add pre-commit hook)

Out-of-scope:
- skills/ddx/SKILL.md and reference files — separate beads
- ddx init / ddx update changes — separate beads
- Old /skills/ddx-* (top-level) deletion — Phase 2

Prerequisite: skills/ddx/SKILL.md + reference/*.md exist before make copy-skills runs, else the rsync copies an empty dir.
acceptance: make build in cli/ succeeds after running make copy-skills (manually or as a prerequisite). ls cli/internal/skills/ shows only embed.go, validate.go, skills_test.go, and ddx/ (no ddx-bead, ddx-run, etc.). diff -r ../skills/ddx/ ./internal/skills/ddx/ reports no differences after make copy-skills. A built ddx binary, when run, embeds the single ddx skill (not 7 separate ones). lefthook.yml has a pre-commit hook wired for copy-skills.

rationale: closed focused bead with small acceptance surface
title: ddx doctor should verify running binary is in canonical install location
description: install.sh installs the ddx binary to $HOME/.local/bin/ddx as the canonical location. But users can end up with multiple copies (e.g. /home/erik/bin/ddx from a stale install, /usr/local/bin/ddx, or a dev build on PATH ahead of the canonical one). This session had a real incident: /home/erik/bin/ddx was 053171bf (older) while /home/erik/.local/bin/ddx was 40f8db0d (current) — the shell picked the wrong one for hours, causing confusion about whether blocker fixes were actually active. ddx doctor should detect mismatches between the running binary's path and the canonical install location AND warn when multiple ddx copies exist on PATH with different SHAs.
acceptance: ddx doctor (1) reports the absolute path of the currently-executing binary via os.Executable(); (2) checks whether that path matches $HOME/.local/bin/ddx (the install.sh canonical location); (3) walks PATH and reports every other 'ddx' found, flagging any whose SHA-256 differs from the running binary as a warning; (4) suggests 'rm <stale> && cp <current> <stale>' or 're-run install.sh' to fix. Test covers the multi-binary-on-PATH scenario by shadowing a fake ddx in a temp dir earlier on PATH.

title: Add loop lifecycle acceptance tests for retry exhaustion and concurrent isolation
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Runtime behavior should be proven at the `Run()` boundary · Regression tests should encode edge-case termination behavior, not just happy paths · Concurrent safety claims need explicit tests</principles>
<concerns>loop lifecycle | retries | cancellation | compaction fail-closed</concerns>
<practices>Add focused tests in `loop_test.go` or a companion runtime acceptance file · Use mock providers/tools with explicit call counters and controllable blocking points · Assert both result state and absence of extra provider calls</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 for cost/telemetry semantics</adrs>
<governing>FEAT-001 AC-FEAT-001-03 and AC-FEAT-001-06</governing>
</context-digest>

Current loop coverage proves success, simple retries, cancellation before start, and compaction no-fit handling, but it does not explicitly cover retry exhaustion stopping at the configured runtime retry ceiling or concurrent `Run()` isolation. Add deterministic tests that:
- force repeated transient provider failures and prove the runtime stops after the documented retry budget without issuing a fourth provider call
- run multiple `Run()` calls concurrently with separate providers/tools/callbacks and prove session IDs, event streams, token totals, and tool logs do not leak across runs
- if needed to support the above, add a long-running mock tool path that can be cancelled so the runtime's documented cancellation semantics remain encoded at the boundary

Relevant files: `loop.go`, `loop_test.go`, `loop_external_test.go`.
acceptance: Tests covering AC-FEAT-001-03 and AC-FEAT-001-06 exist in the runtime test suite; they prove retry exhaustion stops at the runtime retry ceiling, concurrent runs keep independent state, and the default Go test suite passes.

title: feat(doctor): detect+fix core.bare and stray core.worktree (Phase 3)
description:
ddx doctor needs to detect existing repo corruption from prior incidents and offer auto-fix.

Add checks to cli/cmd/doctor.go (or wherever doctor lives):
1. core.bare = true on a repo with a working tree (not actually bare) — flag + offer 'git config --unset core.bare'
2. Stray core.worktree entries that don't match the actual worktree path — flag + offer 'git config --unset core.worktree'
3. extensions.worktreeConfig missing → warning (not fail), with note that it prevents per-worktree config corruption
acceptance: Test: deliberately corrupt a test repo with 'git config core.bare true', run doctor, assert it's detected. With --fix flag (or interactive), assert the bad config gets removed. Doctor still passes on a clean repo.

title: correctness: enforce post-compaction fit when prefix tokens are present
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: compaction/compactor.go:48
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The last-commit fix stops subtracting non-persisted prefix tokens from KeepRecentTokens before calling CompactMessages, which preserves the active prompt but also removes the only fit adjustment for the rebuilt request. CompactMessages still uses the full KeepRecentTokens budget at compaction/summarize.go:284 and never re-checks the final request against the effective window after the summary is appended. A direct repro in the current tree with ContextWindow=80, ReserveTokens=0, EffectivePercent=100, KeepRecentTokens=20, an 80-character oldest user message set, and a 40-character system prompt yields estimated_before=102, threshold=80, and tokens_after_with_prefix=90 after compaction. That means compaction fires, but the next provider call can still exceed the configured context budget and fail with large enough system or other non-persisted prefix context.
Suggested fix: preserve the last real user message verbatim, but add a separate post-compaction fit step that accounts for non-persisted prefix tokens when choosing the preserved recent-message budget or trimming the rebuilt history. Add a regression test that asserts TokensAfter plus prefix tokens stays within the effective window after compaction.
acceptance: With non-persisted prefix tokens present, compaction both preserves the active user prompt and rebuilds a request whose estimated token count including prefix tokens is <= ContextWindow*EffectivePercent/100-ReserveTokens; add regression coverage for the overflow case.

title: drift: tidy telemetry module dependencies
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: go.mod:24
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: The telemetry scaffolding commit added direct imports of OpenTelemetry modules, but go.mod still records go.opentelemetry.io/otel, go.opentelemetry.io/otel/metric, go.opentelemetry.io/otel/sdk, and go.opentelemetry.io/otel/trace as indirect dependencies. Running go mod tidy -diff produces a non-empty diff that promotes those modules to direct requirements, so the repo was not left in the concern-required tidy state after a dependency change.
Suggested fix: Run go mod tidy, commit the resulting go.mod and go.sum updates, and keep the module files clean so go mod tidy -diff is empty after the telemetry dependency addition.
acceptance: go mod tidy -diff exits cleanly with no diff, and go test ./... still passes after the tidied module files are committed

events.summary: Five load-bearing decisions from Stage 2.2 review that Stage 2.3 should lock in before the pattern propagates to 2.4-2.7
events.body:
Stage 2.2 (node/projects resolvers, commit 7e4ce6a) established the resolver pattern cleanly but left five decisions open that affect every subsequent resolver bead. Lock these in at Stage 2.3 (beads + beadsByProject) before the pattern propagates to 2.4-2.7.

1. StateProvider interface split
   Current: single StateProvider in graphql/resolver_node.go with 3 methods (GetNodeSnapshot/GetProjectSnapshots/GetProjectSnapshotByID). Stage 2.3 will add GetBeads/GetBeadByID/GetBeadsByProject — the interface will balloon to 50+ methods across all stages. Recommendation: split into per-resolver-group sub-interfaces (NodeState, BeadState, DocumentState, WorkerState, etc.) each defined next to its resolver file. The top-level Resolver.State field embeds a composite interface that combines them. File: introduce state_provider.go with the composite interface and move the existing StateProvider methods into a new NodeState interface.

2. Stable-key cursors (Relay connections)
   Current: resolver_node.go uses encodeCursor/decodeCursor that emit base64("cursor:<slice-index>"). Slice-index cursors break under insert/delete between pages — fine for projects (rarely mutate) but broken for beads (mutated constantly). Stage 2.3 MUST use a stable-key cursor: base64 of (sort-key, id) or similar. Do not copy-paste the slice-index helpers from resolver_node.go; introduce stable-key helpers in a shared graphql/cursors.go and use them for beads/documents/commits/sessions.

3. Snapshot struct duplication vs direct domain types
   Current: graphql/resolver_node.go defines ProjectSnapshot that mirrors server.ProjectEntry field-for-field. Fine for 3 fields. Will become painful if every state type needs a shadow struct (Bead, Document, Worker, Session, ExecutionRun, Persona, Commit, ...). Decide now: either accept the snapshot pattern and live with duplication, OR let the graphql package depend on narrowly-factored state types directly. Pick ONE before Stage 2.3 so we do not end up with both patterns.

4. r.State == nil nil-check
   Current: resolver_node.go has if r.State == nil { return ...} guards in every method. Will copy-paste into 50 resolvers. Refactor: Resolver constructor rejects nil state, drop the runtime check. Trivial fix (one constructor), huge win in readability across 50 call sites.

5. node(id) prefix dispatch
   Current: node(id) switches on "node-"/"proj-" prefixes with an if-else chain. Stage 2.3 adds bead- prefix. Stage 2.4-2.7 add doc-, worker-, session-, exec-, persona- prefixes. The chain grows to 9 branches. Better: prefix-to-loader map (or a registry that each resolver file registers into from its init()). Lock this at 2.3 before the chain grows.

None of these are defects in 2.2. They are shape decisions that SHOULD be made at 2.3 to avoid technical debt accumulation across 2.4-2.7.

events.summary: qwen/qwen3-coder-next stalled: 30 consecutive read-only tool calls with no write activity — stall detector triggered
events.body:
This is a distinct failure mode from the long-context hang seen on Stage 2.2. Here the model is actively producing tool calls (reading files) but never commits to a write/edit, so the stall detector after 30 reads cancels the run. Symptom of context-window exhaustion: the model is re-reading the same files or exploring too broadly without ever reaching a decision. Not a 500. Not a hang. Not a loop abort.

Escalated to claude sonnet per user directive. Local model is not viable for code-heavy beads with the current prompt shape (plan doc + 1908-line schema + bead context). Future cost-tiered routing epic should capture: prompt-context pre-filtering for local models, AND a check for "N consecutive reads without a write" that kicks in earlier than the 30-read cap.

Refactor bead ddx-7e2f2cb9 covers cursors + constructor + snapshot decision; node prefix + pagination test strengthening added below.
events.body:
Opus review of merge commit 2f46543 / rewrite 8fc5534.

SHAPE COMPLIANCE vs Stage 2.3 note (5 concerns, the note was on THIS bead):
1. StateProvider split — IGNORED. state_graphql.go:66 EXTENDED the monolithic interface with GetBeadSnapshots.
2. Stable-key cursors — CONTRADICTED (strict fail). resolver_beads.go:53 calls encodeCursor(i) slice-index helper. The bead that had the warning attached implemented precisely what the warning said not to do.
3. Snapshot struct vs domain types — IGNORED. Added BeadSnapshot + BeadDependencySnapshot full-copy in resolver_node.go:11-59 and state_graphql.go:61-87.
4. Nil-checks — IGNORED. resolver_beads.go:11-13 and :32-34 both start with if r.State == nil early-return.
5. node(id) prefix registry — IGNORED, AND node(id) does NOT handle bead- prefix. Functional gap: node(id: "bead-xxx") returns nil,nil despite beads being first-class now.

IMPLEMENTATION: correct. beads resolver handles first/after/last/before/status/label/projectID. beadsByProject delegates. hasNextPage logic correct (truncatedByFirst flag — genuine improvement over Projects resolver which lacks it). TestGraphQLBeadsPagination/StatusFilter/ByProject all PASS 0.033s.

ADDITIONAL GAP OPUS FLAGGED beyond the refactor bead (ddx-7e2f2cb9):
- Pagination test uses only 3 beads despite acceptance criterion requiring 15
- node(id) missing bead- prefix branch (functional not just shape)

Both added to refactor bead scope below.

title: agent/execute-bead: classify failed git push as a hard failure (do not close bead)
description:
**Reporter**: nexiq (downstream consumer). Observed across 2026-04-22 → 2026-04-26 on master.

## Observed

`ddx agent execute-bead` can land a commit locally (worktree merged into local master), then fail at the `git push origin master` step, and still classify the bead as `execute-bead: success`. The push failure shows up as `landed locally; push failed:` in the event body, but the bead transitions to closed and the loop moves on.

Concrete blast radius: ~969 unpushed commits accumulated on nexiq's master across multiple days because every bead that landed in that window had a 258 MB turbopack `.sst` file somewhere in its commit history. GitHub rejected each push with the 100 MB-per-file limit error. The execute-loop's success rate looked normal — operators saw bead = closed, dashboard = working — but origin was untouched.

Recovery required `git filter-branch --index-filter 'git rm --cached -r packages/web/.next .next'` across ~1000 commits plus a `.gitignore` hardening change. By that point the only signal that something was wrong was an operator manually running `git rev-list --count origin/master..master`.

## Root cause

The execute-bead orchestrator currently classifies the result of the *merge* step. The push step happens after, and a non-zero exit there is logged in the event body but does not bubble up to the bead's terminal status. As far as routing/state machines are concerned, the bead is done.

This is the worst-case classification: silent for operators, irreversible for state (bead closes, primary doesn't re-run, the work that needs to be re-pushed isn't tracked anywhere).

## Failure modes this masks

- Large blob in commit history (the case we hit) — every subsequent bead inherits the bad ancestor and fails to push.
- Auth expiration / credential rotation on the origin remote.
- Branch protection rules added on the remote between the loop's last successful push and now.
- HTTP 5xx / network flake against origin.
- Local divergence from origin (someone else pushed; non-fast-forward refused).

In all five cases the current behavior is: bead closes, loop picks the next bead, the divergence grows monotonically.

## Suggested fix

1. Promote push to a first-class step in execute-bead's outcome classification.
   - On non-zero exit from `git push`, emit `execute-bead: push_failed` (or similar terminal that explicitly is NOT success) with the push stderr captured in the event body.
   - Reopen the bead with `last_status=push_failed` and a retry_after that buys time for an operator to investigate.
2. Surface push failure on the next claim attempt: when a worker tries to claim a bead in `last_status=push_failed`, refuse the claim and show the operator the prior push stderr — don't blindly redo the primary work, since the local commit is already there.
3. (Optional, defense in depth) Have execute-loop maintain a per-repo divergence counter (`git rev-list --count origin/<branch>..<branch>`); if it crosses a threshold (e.g. >5), pause new claims and emit a loop-level warning event.

## Reproduction

1. On a local fork, install a `pre-receive` hook on the remote that rejects all pushes (or use a remote that requires a permission the runner doesn't have).
2. Queue any bead and run execute-loop normally.
3. Observe: bead closes as success despite the push failing. The merge commit lives only on local master.
4. Confirm: `git rev-list --count origin/master..master` is non-zero, but ddx still routed a new worker to the next bead.

## Files to inspect

- nexiq commit `f2c2ed33` (the .gitignore + .next scrub) is the recovery commit on the downstream side.
- nexiq's own follow-up bead (`nexiq-e57b41eb`) lists this as a blocker; I'll amend it once this ddx bead has an ID.

## Acceptance

- A forced push failure (e.g. pre-receive hook rejecting) results in a bead that does NOT show as closed and shows up in `ddx bead list --status open` with a `last_status=push_failed` field.
- The operator can see the push stderr in the bead's events.
- Subsequent claim attempts on that bead fail loudly until the operator clears the state (retry, force-skip, etc.).
acceptance: Push-failure produces non-closing terminal status; bead remains open with last_status=push_failed and the push stderr captured; subsequent claims refuse until operator clears.

title: test: migrate TestExecuteBead_RequiredGatePass_Merges to real git + script harness
description:
Migrate cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_gates_test.go TestExecuteBead_RequiredGatePass_Merges() from fakeExecuteBeadGit + fakeAgentRunner to real git + script harness.

Pattern:
- Use cli/internal/agent/integration_helper_test.go newScriptHarnessRepo(t, beadCount) to build an isolated repo with real git.
- Use runGitInteg (from cli/internal/git/git_basic_test.go) for any supplemental git invocations, so GIT_* env is scrubbed.
- Replace fakeExecuteBeadGit with the RealLandingGitOps flow used in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go.
- Replace fakeAgentRunner with the script harness (cli/internal/agent/script.go) driven by a per-attempt directive file. When the test needs to simulate 'agent emits no commits' or 'agent emits a dirty worktree', encode that as script directives rather than a canned Result struct.
- Preserve every assertion — the point is that the test now exercises real git + real merge/preserve semantics, not that the surface shrinks.

Scope limit: ONLY this test. Do not migrate any other test in the same file; touching another test expands blast radius outside the bead.

Verification: cd cli && go test ./cmd -run '^TestExecuteBead_RequiredGatePass_Merges$' -count=1 passes. No struct ending in -GitOps or -Runner that fakes the thing under test is referenced from this test after the migration.

Governing concern: concerns.md §testing 'no mocks, period' + 'never mock the thing you are testing'. Parent: ddx-d9df348d.
acceptance: 1) TestExecuteBead_RequiredGatePass_Merges no longer references fakeExecuteBeadGit or fakeAgentRunner. 2) Test dispatches through RealLandingGitOps + newScriptHarnessRepo (or equivalent real-git harness). 3) cd cli && go test ./cmd -run '^TestExecuteBead_RequiredGatePass_Merges$' -count=1 green. 4) No other test in agent_execute_bead_gates_test.go is modified.

title: review: last-commit a160677 provenance repair
description:

title: correctness: mixed unknown-cost sessions produce misleading aggregate cost totals
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: session/usage.go:145
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The usage aggregator keeps summing KnownCostUSD even when a row or the grand total also contains unknown-cost sessions. ADR-001 explicitly says totals must be treated as unknown if any contributing turn is unknown, but the current implementation and tests preserve a numeric KnownCostUSD and the CLI will print a partial dollar amount, which underreports spend for mixed known/unknown aggregates.
Suggested fix: Represent aggregate cost as unknown whenever UnknownCostSessions > 0 for that row or total, update the CLI/JSON rendering to surface that state instead of a partial dollar amount, and add tests covering mixed known/unknown-cost inputs.
acceptance: Aggregate usage marks row and grand-total cost as unknown whenever any contributing session has unknown cost, the CLI/JSON output does not print a misleading partial dollar total for mixed-cost aggregates, and tests cover mixed known/unknown inputs.

title: correctness: session.end still emits unknown cost as numeric sentinel
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: loop.go:360, loop_test.go:506, session/event.go:52, session/replay.go:108
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The last-commit fix removed DefaultPricing fallback from per-attempt telemetry, but the session rollup still serializes a numeric cost on every session.end event by copying result.CostUSD unconditionally. When any contributing attempt has unknown provenance, result.CostUSD becomes -1, so session.end now emits `cost_usd: -1` instead of omitting the amount. That still violates CONTRACT-001 and SD-001, which require unknown rollup cost amounts to be omitted. The new regression test in loop_test.go also codifies unconditional session.end cost emission, so a contract-compliant fix would currently fail tests. Downstream session consumers compound the issue because SessionEndData uses a plain float64 and Replay treats zero as `$0 (local)`, so omitted cost cannot be represented distinctly from free local execution.
Suggested fix: Make session.end omit cost_usd when the run cost is unknown, update session event/replay types to distinguish omitted cost from zero-cost local runs, and replace the current session-end assertion with regression coverage for both known-cost and unknown-cost sessions.
acceptance: When any contributing attempt has unknown cost provenance, session.end omits cost_usd instead of emitting -1; replay/session parsing distinguishes omitted cost from zero-cost local cost; regression tests cover both known-cost and unknown-cost session rollups.

title: Add ImportMetadata to config.Config
description:
Add ImportMetadata struct to config.Config per SD-007 Config Schema Additions.

New fields in Config:
- ImportedFrom *ImportMetadata

New ImportMetadata struct:
- Source string    // "pi" or "opencode"
- Timestamp string // RFC3339
- SourceHash string // truncated SHA-256 of source files

The config loader ignores imported_from — its metadata, not provider config.
acceptance: ImportMetadata added to Config struct; config round-trips correctly

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: routing: ModelRef/Profile paths must consult discovery for --provider + catalog override
description:
## Superseded scope — narrowed per fresh-eyes review 2026-04-18

The original bead framing ('fuzzy matcher collapses provider identity; must match against per-provider pool') is now wholly absorbed into ddx-0486e601 (routing: fuzzy matcher normalization + per-provider pool). Both reviewers (codex + opus) flagged the overlap.

What remains is the ROUTING-LAYER integration work that sits on top of the discovery-layer fix in ddx-0486e601: making the catalog/Profile paths actually CONSULT discovery when req.Provider is pinned. Today, fuzzy match fires only on the uncataloged ModelPin branch (routing.go:193-225). The ModelRef path (routing.go:233-247) and the Profile path (routing.go:248-262) are catalog-only; even after ddx-0486e601 lands, a Profile tier's model_override will still resolve straight from the catalog without checking whether the catalog-resolved concrete ID is actually served by the pinned provider.

## Narrowed scope

1. In routing.go's ModelRef branch: after catalog resolution, if req.Provider is set and discovery is available, check whether the catalog-resolved concrete ID appears on the pinned provider's discovered model list. If yes, proceed. If no, run fuzzy match (via ddx-0486e601's per-provider matcher) against the pinned provider using the ORIGINAL ref (pre-catalog) as input. On fuzzy hit, override the catalog result with the provider-native ID and emit a 'discovery_override' routing event.

2. Same integration in the Profile branch (routing.go:248-262) so tier overrides participate in the override.

3. **Bare-leaf refs** (per opus review): when --model-ref is a bare leaf with no vendor prefix (e.g. 'qwen3.6' vs 'qwen/qwen3.6') AND catalog lookup MISSES (falling into the ModelPin branch), the discovery-consultation hook must still fire when req.Provider is set. Prevents UX asymmetry where 'qwen/qwen3.6' works but 'qwen3.6' doesn't.

4. When req.Provider is NOT set, preserve catalog-only behavior (SD-015 intent for unpinned mode).

5. Emit structured routing events on every override: {kind:'discovery_override', requested_ref, catalog_resolved, discovery_matched, provider, reason}.

## Files likely touched

- cli/internal/agent/routing.go — ModelRef branch + Profile branch
- cli/internal/agent/routing_test.go — regression covering Profile+Provider+Catalog+Discovery interaction and bare-leaf refs

## Depends on

- ddx-8610020e (RouteRequest.Provider field) — routing needs to see req.Provider
- ddx-0486e601 (fuzzy normalization + per-provider matcher) — this bead calls it
acceptance:
1. With --provider vidar-omlx + config profile with cheap:qwen/qwen3.6, routing resolves to Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit via discovery override even though catalog's embedded-openai surface has a different concrete ID.

2. When the catalog-resolved ID IS served by the pinned provider, no override fires — catalog wins.

3. Bare-leaf refs (--model-ref qwen3.6, no vendor prefix) also trigger discovery consultation when Provider is pinned.

4. When --provider is NOT set, catalog-only behavior is unchanged (no regression in unpinned mode).

5. Routing event records 'discovery_override' with kind + requested_ref + catalog_resolved + discovery_matched + provider.

6. Regression test covers Profile+Provider+Catalog+Discovery interaction AND bare-leaf variant.

title: test: migrate TestExecuteBeadHiddenRefUniqueness to real git + script harness
description:
Migrate cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_test.go TestExecuteBeadHiddenRefUniqueness() from fakeExecuteBeadGit + fakeAgentRunner to real git + script harness.

Pattern:
- Use cli/internal/agent/integration_helper_test.go newScriptHarnessRepo(t, beadCount) to build an isolated repo with real git.
- Use runGitInteg (from cli/internal/git/git_basic_test.go) for any supplemental git invocations, so GIT_* env is scrubbed.
- Replace fakeExecuteBeadGit with the RealLandingGitOps flow used in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go.
- Replace fakeAgentRunner with the script harness (cli/internal/agent/script.go) driven by a per-attempt directive file. When the test needs to simulate 'agent emits no commits' or 'agent emits a dirty worktree', encode that as script directives rather than a canned Result struct.
- Preserve every assertion — the point is that the test now exercises real git + real merge/preserve semantics, not that the surface shrinks.

Scope limit: ONLY this test. Do not migrate any other test in the same file; touching another test expands blast radius outside the bead.

Verification: cd cli && go test ./cmd -run '^TestExecuteBeadHiddenRefUniqueness$' -count=1 passes. No struct ending in -GitOps or -Runner that fakes the thing under test is referenced from this test after the migration.

Governing concern: concerns.md §testing 'no mocks, period' + 'never mock the thing you are testing'. Parent: ddx-d9df348d.
acceptance: 1) TestExecuteBeadHiddenRefUniqueness no longer references fakeExecuteBeadGit or fakeAgentRunner. 2) Test dispatches through RealLandingGitOps + newScriptHarnessRepo (or equivalent real-git harness). 3) cd cli && go test ./cmd -run '^TestExecuteBeadHiddenRefUniqueness$' -count=1 green. 4) No other test in agent_execute_bead_test.go is modified.

rationale: closed focused bead with small acceptance surface
title: Design agent shared model catalog and updateable model manifest
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Make Intent Explicit · Validate Your Work · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>go-std | testing</concerns>
<practices>small reusable library packages in Go · explicit config/data boundaries · tests beside implementation</practices>
<governing>Recent FEAT-004 / SD-005 routing work exposed that agent already owns prompt presets, but no shared model catalog exists. The user wants agent, not DDx or HELIX, to be the reusable source of truth for canonical model families, aliases, current recommended targets, and deprecation metadata.</governing>
<evidence>
- prompt/presets.go already uses the term `preset` for system prompt behavior, so model policy must avoid colliding terminology.
- DDx currently duplicates model policy in cli/internal/agent/models.go and cli/internal/agent/registry.go.
- HELIX currently duplicates concrete model version strings in scripts/helix resolve_model().
- Model releases change rapidly, so keeping canonical model/version mappings only in Go code is too rigid.
- The desired direction is a agent-owned library package plus a separately maintained model manifest in the agent repo that downstream tools can consume.
</evidence>
</context-digest>

Design a agent-owned shared model catalog and updateable model manifest.

Scope:
- Define the package/API shape for a reusable agent model catalog that DDx and agent CLI can consume.
- Define the manifest format, storage location, and update workflow so model/version data can be maintained separately from Go logic.
- Define how the catalog represents aliases, model families, tiers/profiles (e.g. smart / fast / cheap), canonical current targets, and deprecated/stale model versions.
- Define how non-agent harness families (e.g. Claude Code / Codex) can still consume agent-owned catalog data without pushing harness orchestration into agent runtime.
- Clarify the responsibility split: agent owns catalog data/policy, DDx owns harness orchestration, HELIX owns stage intent.
- Identify how FEAT-004 / SD-005 and any neighboring agent artifacts should evolve to reference this shared catalog.

Expected deliverables:
- Governing agent docs updated with the shared model catalog concept.
- A design for data-vs-code separation so the model list can be updated independently of core code releases where practical.
- Consumer examples for DDx and agent CLI.
acceptance: DDX Agent design docs define a shared model catalog and separately maintained model manifest; ownership split between agent, DDx, and HELIX is explicit; aliases, tiers/profiles, canonical targets, and stale-model metadata are specified; the design explains how downstream tools consume the catalog without duplicating model policy.

title: grading: bounded evidence assembly including PostRunOut/PostRunOK/ToolCalls
description:
Stage C2 of FEAT-022. Modify buildGradingPrompt to use
cli/internal/evidence primitives with a minimum evidence floor,
include PostRunOut/PostRunOK/ToolCalls bounded via ClampOutput, and
emit a pre-dispatch short-circuit outcome class.

Implements FEAT-022 requirement §6 and §7 for the grader path.
Contributes to US-225.

Depends on Stage A1 (ddx-64299657) only. Independent of Stage B/C1.

PINNED event name (fixed here, consumed by Stage F): the pre-dispatch
short-circuit outcome event is emitted as 'compare-error:
context_overflow'. The same class name used in Stage C1 for review
(see FEAT-022 §12) is mirrored on the grading side, with the event
kind prefix adapted to the grading context. Stage F reads this name
when aggregating metrics.

In-scope files:
- cli/internal/agent/grade.go (buildGradingPrompt at :58: route arm
  Output, PostRunOut, ToolCalls through evidence.ClampOutput; route
  arm Diff through evidence.ClampDiff + evidence.DecomposeDiff;
  include arm.PostRunOK as a boolean field in the prompt; implement
  priority-ordered assembly with minimum floor: rubric + task prompt
  + per-arm identity always present)
- cli/internal/agent/grade.go (pre-dispatch short-circuit: if
  assembled prompt exceeds MaxPromptBytes after all trimming, skip
  provider dispatch and emit 'compare-error: context_overflow')
- Tests: fixture with multi-MB arm outputs and diffs exercising
  minimum floor preservation, PostRunOut/PostRunOK/ToolCalls
  inclusion (bounded), pre-dispatch short-circuit event emission

Out-of-scope:
- Review path (Stage C1)
- Section-level telemetry (Stage F)
- Ref-only-metadata alternative for grading (rejected in round 2:
  compare sandboxes are deleted before the record returns unless
  KeepSandbox is set; inline-with-cap is the correct strategy)

Rollback: revert grade.go changes; compare tests fall back to
prior behavior.
acceptance: cd cli && go test -run TestBuildGradingPrompt ./internal/agent/... passes including tests for: arm output trimming (fixture arm.Output = 10× MaxOutputBytes or equivalent cap, assert bounded + truncation marker), minimum floor preservation (fixture total = 20× MaxPromptBytes; output still contains rubric, task prompt, and each arm's identity (harness, model) verbatim), PostRunOut/PostRunOK/ToolCalls inclusion after ClampOutput (each appears in output with truncation marker when clamped), pre-dispatch short-circuit. cd cli && go test -run TestGradingContextOverflow ./internal/agent/... passes: given a fixture that forces residual overflow, the grader emits an event whose body contains the literal substring 'compare-error: context_overflow' and the event's recorded provider-dispatch count remains 0. AST/import check: cd cli && go test -run TestEvidencePrimitiveUsage ./internal/agent/... asserts grade.go imports cli/internal/evidence and contains no direct os.ReadFile calls unless annotated. Existing comparison tests under cli/internal/agent/ still pass unchanged.

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: provider-wire: enforce reasoning_wire to strip reasoning field on fixed-variant models
description:
Captures codex Q4 from the reasoning-variants synthesis (2026-04-26). Real bug, low blast radius today.

## Bug

internal/provider/openrouter/openrouter.go:20 declares 'Thinking: true' globally. internal/provider/openai/openai.go:307 emits a reasoning field on the wire for any non-empty Reasoning. For fixed-variant models like qwen/qwen3.6-plus where the wire endpoint can't honor an external reasoning toggle (the model name IS the encoding), this either gets silently ignored by the upstream or returns a confusing error.

Today the bug only triggers if a user explicitly pins:
  Provider=openrouter Model=qwen/qwen3.6-plus Reasoning=non-off
through the native agent. With auto-routing not yet exposing pi-aggregated candidates, this path is rarely taken.

## Fix

Provider/harness adapters consult the model's reasoning_wire field (added by agent-a0160cde) before encoding:
- provider → emit reasoning field as today.
- model_id → strip the reasoning field; model name is the encoding.
- none → strip + reject if user asked for non-off levels (catalog/wire mismatch).

## Blocked on

agent-a0160cde (catalog reasoning_wire field must exist before provider can consult it).

## In-scope files

- internal/provider/openai/openai.go (reasoning field emission at line 307 area)
- internal/provider/openrouter/openrouter.go (capability declaration; may need refinement)
- internal/provider/anthropic/anthropic.go (already has structured reasoning encoding; check it honors model-level wire metadata)
- New tests asserting wire body byte-content for each (control, wire) combination

## Out of scope

- Reasoning=auto resolution before gate (filed separately as agent-tbd-auto)
- Family-grouping in catalog (qwen3.6 with sub-variants for off/high) — deferred per codex
acceptance:
1. Native openai-compat dispatch consults the resolved model's reasoning_wire field (from manifest via PricingFor or direct catalog lookup).
2. New test go test ./internal/provider/openai -run TestOpenAIRespectsReasoningWireModelID asserts: when reasoning_wire=model_id and Reasoning=high is set, the captured HTTP request body contains NO reasoning field (httptest fixture).
3. New test go test ./internal/provider/openai -run TestOpenAIRespectsReasoningWireProvider asserts: when reasoning_wire=provider and Reasoning=high is set, the captured HTTP request body contains the reasoning field (existing behavior preserved).
4. New test go test ./internal/provider/openai -run TestOpenAIRejectsReasoningWireNoneWithLevel asserts: when reasoning_wire=none and Reasoning=high is set, dispatch fails pre-flight with a clear error.
5. go test ./... -count=1 passes.

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: test: migrate TestExecuteBead_RequiredGateFail_Preserves to real git + script harness
description:
Migrate cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_gates_test.go TestExecuteBead_RequiredGateFail_Preserves() from fakeExecuteBeadGit + fakeAgentRunner to real git + script harness.

Pattern:
- Use cli/internal/agent/integration_helper_test.go newScriptHarnessRepo(t, beadCount) to build an isolated repo with real git.
- Use runGitInteg (from cli/internal/git/git_basic_test.go) for any supplemental git invocations, so GIT_* env is scrubbed.
- Replace fakeExecuteBeadGit with the RealLandingGitOps flow used in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go.
- Replace fakeAgentRunner with the script harness (cli/internal/agent/script.go) driven by a per-attempt directive file. When the test needs to simulate 'agent emits no commits' or 'agent emits a dirty worktree', encode that as script directives rather than a canned Result struct.
- Preserve every assertion — the point is that the test now exercises real git + real merge/preserve semantics, not that the surface shrinks.

Scope limit: ONLY this test. Do not migrate any other test in the same file; touching another test expands blast radius outside the bead.

Verification: cd cli && go test ./cmd -run '^TestExecuteBead_RequiredGateFail_Preserves$' -count=1 passes. No struct ending in -GitOps or -Runner that fakes the thing under test is referenced from this test after the migration.

Governing concern: concerns.md §testing 'no mocks, period' + 'never mock the thing you are testing'. Parent: ddx-d9df348d.
acceptance: 1) TestExecuteBead_RequiredGateFail_Preserves no longer references fakeExecuteBeadGit or fakeAgentRunner. 2) Test dispatches through RealLandingGitOps + newScriptHarnessRepo (or equivalent real-git harness). 3) cd cli && go test ./cmd -run '^TestExecuteBead_RequiredGateFail_Preserves$' -count=1 green. 4) No other test in agent_execute_bead_gates_test.go is modified.

rationale: closed focused bead with small acceptance surface
title: Per-(provider,model) routing-signal cross-pollution test
description:
Sub-bead 'c' of agent-d9c358ba split. routeAttemptKey is already keyed by (Harness, Provider, Model, Endpoint) and routeMetricSignals emits per-(provider,model) signals via routing.ProviderModelKey at service_route_attempts.go:184. The keying is structurally correct already; this bead only adds the regression test that proves recording failures on one model does not pollute the signal observed for a sibling model on the same provider.

Smallest piece of the d9c358ba split (~40 LOC).

In-scope:
- service_route_attempts_test.go (new test)
acceptance:
1. New test go test . -run 'TestRouteAttempts_ProviderModelKeying' passes: record three failures on (Provider=A, Model=X), verify (Provider=A, Model=Y)'s signal is unchanged; record success on (Provider=A, Model=Y), verify (Provider=A, Model=X)'s signal is unchanged.
2. Test uses real route-attempt recording calls (not synthetic key construction).
3. go test ./... -count=1 passes.

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: drift: add fuzz coverage for prompt envelope parsing
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cmd/ddx-agent/main.go:322
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: The last-commit change extends parsePromptInput, which is a CLI boundary parser and user-input validator for DDx prompt envelopes, but the repo still has no fuzz coverage for this path. The active testing concern requires fuzzing parsers, deserializers, and user-input validators, and searching for Go fuzz targets only finds coverage in tool/fuzz_test.go. This leaves the prompt-envelope state machine protected only by example-based tests despite several recent regressions in the same code path.
Suggested fix: add Go fuzz tests for parsePromptInput or resolvePrompt in cmd/ddx-agent, seeded with valid envelopes, malformed envelopes, and plain JSON payloads. The fuzz target should at minimum assert no panic and stable classification between raw prompt passthrough versus invalid-envelope error for malformed envelope-shaped inputs.
acceptance: cmd/ddx-agent includes fuzz coverage for prompt parsing that seeds valid envelopes, malformed envelopes, and plain JSON payloads; fuzzing parsePromptInput or resolvePrompt does not panic; malformed envelope-shaped inputs continue to return deterministic invalid-envelope errors while non-envelope JSON remains raw prompt text

title: Plumb provider metadata and streaming timing into telemetry
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>go-std | testing</concerns>
<practices>go fmt ./... before every commit · go test ./... and go test -race ./... for concurrent code · golangci-lint run · gosec ./... · govulncheck ./... · log with slog, wrap errors with context, and keep tests table-driven with testify</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 keeps JSONL replay separate from OTel analytics, preserves reported cost when available, uses exact runtime pricing only for known systems/models, and leaves cost unknown otherwise so replay fidelity and cross-tool analytics both survive.</adrs>
<governing>CONTRACT-001 provider metadata and streaming timing — expose provider family/system, resolved model, cache tokens, TTFT, and reported cost/timing only when the SDK actually supplies them; review hx-0b94868b treats this as the next telemetry-chain link after the root package.</governing>
</context-digest>

Extend openai and anthropic provider paths, including streaming assembly, so the runtime can populate provider-family identity, provider-system identity, request/response model fields, server identity when known, cache token fields, first-token timing, and any provider-reported cost or timing details exposed by the SDKs. Capture TTFT and generation timing in stream_consume without guessing unavailable timing windows.
acceptance: OpenAI and Anthropic runtime paths surface provider/model/cache metadata needed by CONTRACT-001; stream_consume captures TTFT and generation timing when available; unavailable timing windows remain omitted; tests cover streaming and non-streaming metadata propagation

title: catalog: add reasoning_levels + reasoning_control + reasoning_wire to manifest v4 model entries
description:
Captures codex Q1/Q3/Q4 from the reasoning-variants vs tunable-models design synthesis (2026-04-26). Three new fields on internal/modelcatalog/manifest.go:35 model entries:

- reasoning_levels []string — capability advertisement; routing capability gate uses this.
- reasoning_control string — tunable | fixed | none. Whether the user can change reasoning per request.
- reasoning_wire string — provider | model_id | none. How the level is encoded on the wire.

The codex review made the case that reasoning_levels alone is insufficient: 'supports high' and 'emit high on the wire' are different facts. Tunable models (gpt-5.x reasoning_effort, claude thinking, gemini thinking budget) advertise multiple levels and expect the wire to carry the level. Variant models (qwen3-235b-a22b vs qwen3-235b-a22b-thinking-2507) encode reasoning in the model name itself; their wire has no reasoning field.

## Concrete examples

  gpt-5.5                       reasoning_levels: [off, low, medium, high, xhigh]   control: tunable    wire: provider
  claude-opus-4-7               reasoning_levels: [off, low, medium, high, max]     control: tunable    wire: provider
  qwen3-235b-a22b               reasoning_levels: [off]                              control: fixed      wire: model_id
  qwen3-235b-a22b-thinking-2507 reasoning_levels: [high]                             control: fixed      wire: model_id
  qwen3.6-plus (via openrouter) reasoning_levels: [high]                             control: fixed      wire: model_id

## Blocked on

agent-3663e287 (initial agent-vs-pi benchmark). Rationale: this catalog field expansion is data-gated — if the benchmark says agent loses to pi for shared backings, the catalog enrichment may shift to pi's per-(provider, model) candidates rather than our native catalog. Sequencing this after the benchmark avoids redoing the schema design based on which surface ends up needing it.

## In-scope files

- internal/modelcatalog/manifest.go (struct fields, validation)
- internal/modelcatalog/manifest_test.go (round-trip tests)
- internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml (populate the three fields for existing entries)
- internal/core/pricing.go (only if PricingFor projection needs the new fields exposed downstream)

## Out of scope

- Provider wire enforcement (filed as agent-tbd-wire-enforcement; blocks on this bead).
- Routing capability gate consuming reasoning_levels (already exists in internal/routing/gating.go via the older reasoning-level flow; this bead's gate-integration may be a no-op if the existing gate already reads from manifest).
acceptance:
1. ModelEntry in internal/modelcatalog/manifest.go has ReasoningLevels []string, ReasoningControl string, ReasoningWire string fields with yaml tags reasoning_levels, reasoning_control, reasoning_wire.
2. Validation rejects unknown reasoning_control values (must be in {tunable, fixed, none}) and unknown reasoning_wire values (must be in {provider, model_id, none}).
3. internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml has the three fields populated for at least gpt-5.5, claude-opus-4-7, sonnet-4.6, gemini-2.5-pro, gemini-2.5-flash, claude-haiku-5.5 (covering tunable + fixed + none cases).
4. New round-trip test go test ./internal/modelcatalog -run TestManifestPreservesReasoningCapabilityFields passes.
5. go test ./... -count=1 passes.

title: Re-scope ddx-70c48576: keep routing_signal_*.go as DDx evidence; only delete routing_signals.go orphans
description:
Surfaced by ddx-c0f6b19c. The original ddx-70c48576 said "delete routing_signal_*.go because moved upstream." But these are DDx-side evidence primitives:
- routing_signal_adapters.go — ProbeOpenRouterBalance, ReadCodexNativeSignals (DDx native CLI session reading)
- routing_signal_http.go — provider HTTP probes for DDx evidence
- routing_signal_tmux.go — DDx-side tmux scrape (the AGENT also has its own tmux scrape in internal/harnesses; the DDx-side variant reads native CLI logs from DDx-managed paths)
- observability_workdir.go (kept) depends on ~25 free functions from these files

Re-scope ddx-70c48576: KEEP these files in DDx as evidence-collection primitives (parallel to the kept routing_metrics.go which writes .ddx/agent-logs/routing-outcomes.jsonl). Only delete routing_signals.go ITSELF if its top-level types (RoutingSignalSnapshot, RoutingOutcome, etc.) are no longer referenced.

Outcome of this bead: ddx-70c48576 notes updated; clear scope going forward.
acceptance:
- [ ] ddx-70c48576 description updated with the keep-in-DDx classification per file
- [ ] Inventory done: which symbols in routing_signals.go are referenced where, classified keep vs delete

bead.notes:
INVENTORY (per codex review):

Signal-package files in cli/internal/agent/ (production callers in parens):
- routing_signals.go — defines RoutingSignalSnapshot, RoutingOutcome, source kind constants. Used by: cmd/agent_usage.go (RoutingOutcome aggregation), server/providers.go (RoutingSignalSnapshot fan-out), server/workers.go (RoutingOutcome write).
- routing_signal_adapters.go — ProbeOpenRouterBalance, ReadCodexNativeSignals, source-kind constants. Used by: routing_signals.go internally. DDx-evidence reading.
- routing_signal_http.go — provider HTTP probes. Used by: routing_signal_adapters.go internally.
- routing_signal_tmux.go — DDx-side tmux scrape (parallel to agents internal/harnesses/{claude,codex}/quota_tmux.go). Used by: routing_signals.go internally + observability_workdir.go RefreshXQuotaViaTmuxForWorkDir wrappers. SUBJECT TO ddx-f49d7d1a tmux-ownership decision.

DECISION:
- routing_signals.go, routing_signal_adapters.go, routing_signal_http.go: KEEP in DDx as evidence-collection primitives (parallel to routing_metrics.go which the cleanup explicitly preserves). They read DDx-managed data (provider native CLI logs, OpenRouter balance, etc.).
- routing_signal_tmux.go: PER ddx-f49d7d1a — agent owns tmux scrape; DELETE from DDx after observability_workdir.go retires the RefreshXQuotaViaTmuxForWorkDir wrappers.
- routing_metrics.go (separate file, already kept): stays. DDx evidence storage.

CONSEQUENCES:
- ddx-70c48576 RE-SCOPED: only routing_signal_tmux.go + routing_signal_tmux_test.go delete. The other signal_* files stay.
- routing_signals.go + RoutingSignalSnapshot type STAYS as DDx-internal type. server/providers.go consumers (ddx-c7fe4337) just need to drop the agent.NewRegistry usage; RoutingSignalSnapshot import stays.

title: Phase 5d: add agent-side observability surface (Probe/Signals/Quota) for DDx legacy callers
description:
Surfaced by ddx-81f71d44. DDx still calls Runner observability methods (r.ProbeHarnessState, r.LoadRoutingSignalSnapshot, r.RefreshClaudeQuotaViaTmux, r.RefreshCodexQuotaViaTmux, r.metricsStoreFromRunner) from agent_usage.go, agent_cmd.go doctor/list/log paths, agent_route_status.go, server/providers.go.

Service surface today does not expose these. Two paths:
A) Extend CONTRACT-003 with explicit observability methods (ProbeHarness, RefreshHarnessQuota, RoutingSignalSnapshot)
B) Subsume into existing methods (HealthCheck for probe; RouteStatus already returns routing snapshot; tmux refresh becomes implicit on HealthCheck)

Path B is cleaner — no contract growth, fewer methods. ListHarnesses + HealthCheck + RouteStatus together cover all observability needs the DDx callers have.

Process: audit DDx callers, map each to existing service methods (or expand HealthCheck/RouteStatus return shapes), update DDx callers, delete the Runner observability methods.
acceptance:
- [ ] All DDx observability callers migrated to service methods
- [ ] No new methods added to CONTRACT-003 (use existing methods, expand return shapes if needed)
- [ ] DDx Runner.ProbeHarnessState etc. delete

rationale: closed focused bead with small acceptance surface
title: refine vision + principles (in-place sharpening of frame docs)
description:
V1 of 3 children under epic ddx-53c96967.

REFINE (do not rewrite) docs/helix/00-discover/product-vision.md and docs/helix/01-frame/principles.md to reflect what's settled.

CURRENT STATE:
- product-vision.md (177 lines) — written before several decisions landed. Has aspirational language and outdated references.
- principles.md (72 lines) — short list of guiding principles. Some are load-bearing, some are filler.
- prd.md (370 lines) and concerns.md (257 lines) are reference context — read for background, but DO NOT modify them in this bead.

DECISIONS THAT HAVE SETTLED AND SHOULD BE REFLECTED:
1. DDx is the platform layer; HELIX is the workflow layer; Dun is the quality layer. Three-project stack with explicit boundaries.
2. Project-local everything: ddx init/install only touch <projectRoot>. The only global ddx artifact is ddx-server. (FEAT-015, just shipped.)
3. Cost-tiered work: cheap models do, strong models review, deterministic fixes at the top of the ladder. Goal is closed-bead throughput per dollar.
4. Endpoint-first routing redesign: providers are endpoints + live discovery + fuzzy model match (in progress; reference don't promise).
5. Single-skill consolidation: DDx ships ONE 'ddx' skill (not seven). Per FEAT-011.
6. Bead-driven work: beads are the unit of work; execute-loop drains the queue with auto-recovery and review.

REFINEMENT GOALS:
- Sharpen: every sentence load-bearing. Cut filler.
- Prune principles to ones that drive actual decisions. If a principle isn't cited in any spec, remove it.
- Surface the three-layer architecture early in the vision.
- Make the 'platform vs workflow' boundary explicit.
- Voice: confident, technical, concise. No marketing fluff.

CONSTRAINT: in-place edits via Edit tool. Preserve sections that work. Do not full-rewrite. Do not change frontmatter/IDs.
acceptance: 1. docs/helix/00-discover/product-vision.md updated; net length within +/- 30% of current 177 lines. 2. docs/helix/01-frame/principles.md updated; principles list reduced to load-bearing items only. 3. Three-layer architecture (DDx/HELIX/Dun) introduced in vision overview. 4. 'Platform vs workflow' boundary explicit in vision and principles. 5. The 6 settled decisions (project-local, cost-tiered, endpoint-first, single-skill, bead-driven, three-layer) are reflected — not necessarily by name, but the underlying ideas are present and consistent. 6. No filler, no marketing language, no aspirational features that haven't shipped. 7. Frontmatter and HELIX IDs preserved.

- prd.md (370 lines) and concerns.md (257 lines) are reference context — read for background, but DO NOT modify them in this bead.

DECISIONS THAT HAVE SETTLED AND SHOULD BE REFLECTED:
1. DDx is the platform layer; HELIX is the workflow layer; Dun is the quality layer. Three-project stack with explicit boundaries.
2. Project-local everything: ddx init/install only touch <projectRoot>. The only global ddx artifact is ddx-server. (FEAT-015, just shipped.)
3. Cost-tiered work: cheap models do, strong models review, deterministic fixes at the top of the ladder. Goal is closed-bead throughput per dollar.
4. Endpoint-first routing redesign: providers are endpoints + live discovery + fuzzy model match (in progress; reference don't promise).
5. Single-skill consolidation: DDx ships ONE 'ddx' skill (not seven). Per FEAT-011.
6. Bead-driven work: beads are the unit of work; execute-loop drains the queue with auto-recovery and review.

REFINEMENT GOALS:
- Sharpen: every sentence load-bearing. Cut filler.
- Prune principles to ones that drive actual decisions. If a principle isn't cited in any spec, remove it.
- Surface the three-layer architecture early in the vision.
- Make the 'platform vs workflow' boundary explicit.
- Voice: confident, technical, concise. No marketing fluff.

CONSTRAINT: in-place edits via Edit tool. Preserve sections that work. Do not full-rewrite. Do not change frontmatter/IDs.
bead.acceptance: 1. docs/helix/00-discover/product-vision.md updated; net length within +/- 30% of current 177 lines. 2. docs/helix/01-frame/principles.md updated; principles list reduced to load-bearing items only. 3. Three-layer architecture (DDx/HELIX/Dun) introduced in vision overview. 4. 'Platform vs workflow' boundary explicit in vision and principles. 5. The 6 settled decisions (project-local, cost-tiered, endpoint-first, single-skill, bead-driven, three-layer) are reflected — not necessarily by name, but the underlying ideas are present and consistent. 6. No filler, no marketing language, no aspirational features that haven't shipped. 7. Frontmatter and HELIX IDs preserved.
