rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: Overflow-triggered auto-compaction and retry
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for simplicity · No speculative abstractions</principles>
<concerns>go-std | testing</concerns>
<practices>Go stdlib · testify/assert</practices>
<governing>SD-001 §agent loop; SD-006 §compaction</governing>
</context-digest>

When a provider call fails with a context overflow error, automatically trigger compaction and retry the failed turn rather than returning ErrCompactionNoFit to the caller.

**Problem**: When context overflows mid-session, ddx-agent returns `ErrCompactionNoFit` and stops. Pi detects the overflow error, removes it from agent state, triggers compaction, and retries the turn transparently. Long-running tasks on small-context local models just die in ddx-agent.

**Reference implementation**: pi `agent-session.ts` lines 1765-1788 — detects overflow in the LLM error response, removes the overflow message, invokes compaction, then re-queues the turn.

**Files to change**:
- `loop.go` — in the provider call error path, detect context overflow errors (pattern match on "context length", "token limit", "maximum context", "context window") and if a Compactor is configured, invoke it and retry the current turn once
- `compaction/` — verify `ErrCompactionNoFit` is propagated correctly so the retry can distinguish "compaction helped" from "still too large"

**Behaviour**:
- On overflow: attempt compaction → retry turn once
- If compaction fails or post-compaction turn still overflows: return error (no infinite loop)
- Only triggers when a Compactor is configured; otherwise behaves as before
acceptance: go test ./... passes; loop_test covers overflow→compact→retry path; overflow without compactor returns error immediately; double-overflow after compaction returns error without looping; go vet ./... clean

title: Step 5 — Cost-cap observation procedure + guardrails
description: Implement cost guards per v7 Step 5. Caps are derived from observation, not formula: (1) run a single (ddx-agent, smoke, 1 rep) on git-leak-recovery (highest-burn canary task) three times; (2) record observed token streams × pricing -> measured cost; (3) per-run cap = p95 × 2.0 safety, floor $1.00, ceiling $5.00; (4) per-matrix cap = per-run × n_runs × 1.2; (5) numbers written into the matrix's costs.json so memos can cite the derivation. Track all four token streams (input, output, cached-input, retried-input). Over-budget runs -> process_outcome=budget_halted (not silent truncation). Document procedure in scripts/benchmark/cost-guards/README.md.
acceptance: Observation procedure documented + executed; per-run + per-matrix caps derived from observed p95 with the floor/ceiling; synthetic over-budget provider stub triggers process_outcome=budget_halted; matrix continues on budget_halted runs.

title: integration: no-fit prefix compaction still sends an over-budget provider request
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: loop.go:114; loop.go:153; loop.go:179
Category: integration
Severity: high
Description: The new no-fit path in compactMessages now returns result=nil for over-budget prefix cases, but Run treats a nil compaction result as a no-op and immediately builds providerMessages from the original history. In the same repro covered by TestCompactor_SystemPromptPrefixNoFitFailsClosed, the unchanged history still estimates to 135 tokens with the system prompt prefix included (67 prefix + 31 user + 33 assistant + 4 active user), which exceeds the 80-token effective window and will still be sent to the provider. The patch therefore fixes the compactor return value but does not actually prevent an over-budget LLM request in the agent loop.
Suggested fix: when compaction was needed but the compactor cannot produce an in-budget history, fail the iteration before calling the provider or add a compactor/loop contract that surfaces an explicit over-budget outcome. Add a Run-level regression test that asserts no provider Chat call occurs with the 80-token/260-char-prefix repro unless the request fits within budget.
acceptance: For the 80-token context-window repro with a 260-character system prompt prefix, agent.Run must not call Provider.Chat with provider messages whose estimated token count exceeds the effective window; add regression coverage for the no-fit compaction path at the Run integration level.

Fresh-eyes review complete for commit 5349a98c02f5ce84945994038bd32511e8d365b7.
Finding filed: hx-a30d162c.
Pass 1 correctness: the patch fixes hx-1f1e2472's same-harness undercount path and the updated unit tests pass.
Pass 2 integration: aggregateUsageFromRoutingMetrics now relies entirely on mirror-key identity matches when merging sessions.jsonl with routing-outcomes.jsonl. External harness writes do not guarantee native_session_id, trace_id, or span_id, so a current session row and its mirrored routing outcome can both remain unkeyed and get counted twice. Reproduced through the shipped CLI with one codex sessions.jsonl row plus one matching routing-outcomes.jsonl row lacking all mirror IDs; ddx agent usage --since 2026-04-01 --format json reported sessions=2, input_tokens=400, output_tokens=40, cost_usd=5 for that single run.
Pass 3 concern-aware quality: implementation and tests stay within the Go/testing stack and introduce no new attack surface, but the compatibility merge now violates the intended no-double-count guarantee for current runs without correlation metadata.
Pass 4 operational learnings: no AGENTS.md update required; no separate learnings bead required.
Verification: reread AGENTS.md; loaded active principles plus go-std, security-owasp, and testing concerns/practices; inspected bead hx-1f1e2472; reviewed the last-commit diff and surrounding merge logic in cli/cmd/agent_usage.go plus cli/cmd/agent_usage_test.go; inspected cli/internal/agent/runner.go and cli/internal/agent/routing_metrics.go write semantics; ran cd cli && go test ./cmd -run TestAgentUsageIncludesLegacySessionsAndRoutingOutcomes -count=1; ran cd cli && go test ./cmd -run TestAgentUsage -count=1; built a temporary ddx CLI binary; and reproduced the double-count using a fixture workspace with unkeyed codex session and routing-outcome rows.
No AGENTS.md update required.

title: compaction: suppress no-op compaction telemetry (supersedes agent-22c95151)
description:
Per CONTRACT-001 event schema: type=compaction events fire ONLY when actual work was performed (messages_after < messages_before OR token count drops). No-op compactions emit nothing at default verbosity.

Existing agent-22c95151 documents the original need; this bead is the realization in the new contract context. Close agent-22c95151 when this lands.
acceptance:
- [ ] Session log of a 100% no-op-compaction execution contains zero compaction events
- [ ] Real compactions still emit events with the same data shape (messages_before, messages_after, tokens_freed)
- [ ] Test asserts the suppression at default verbosity

title: service: implement DdxAgent.TailSessionLog (subscribe to in-progress session events)
description:
Implement DdxAgent.TailSessionLog(ctx, sessionID) per CONTRACT-001. Streams events from a previously-started or in-progress session by ID. Used by DDx workers/UI to subscribe to a run started elsewhere (server-managed worker that DDx CLI wants to follow).

Backed by the same internal session-log writer Execute uses. Multi-subscriber-safe: multiple TailSessionLog callers on the same sessionID each get the full event stream.

Replaces DDx-side session_log_tailer.go (109 lines) which currently tails the JSONL file directly.
acceptance:
- [ ] DdxAgent.TailSessionLog(ctx, sessionID) returns <-chan Event closed when session completes
- [ ] Subscriber attached mid-execution receives all subsequent events
- [ ] Subscriber attached after completion receives the final event then channel closes
- [ ] Multiple subscribers on the same sessionID each receive full streams

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: Migrate navigation and layout e2e tests to fixture routes
description:
## Context

The frontend route tests need to run against the same fixture-backed Go DDx server as the rest of the e2e suite. The current node/project router uses concrete paths such as `/nodes/<fixture-node-id>/projects/<fixture-project-id>/...`, and tests that still rely on static preview or implicit local data drift fail under the real app. `e2e/navigation.spec.ts` already contains the target route style around line 177, but the navigation/layout coverage must consistently use fixture IDs and seeded backend data.

Update the navigation and layout e2e tests so they pass against the new Playwright harness and its seeded fixture while preserving their existing route/shell coverage.

## In-scope files

- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/navigation.spec.ts` — TC-006, TC-007, and any route setup in this file needed to use fixture node/project IDs.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/layout.e2e.ts` — layout route e2e expectations that depend on project/node data.

## Out-of-scope

- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/app.spec.ts`.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/beads-smoke.spec.ts`, `e2e/executions.spec.ts`, `e2e/workers.spec.ts`, and `e2e/screenshots.spec.ts`.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/playwright.config.ts` and fixture creation except reading the fixture IDs/data created by the prerequisite bead.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/demo-recording.spec.ts` and `playwright.demo.config.ts`.
- Frontend route/component changes and Go server changes.
- Skipping tests or deleting assertions to get a green run.

## Rollback / cleanup

Keep route expectations tied to explicit fixture node/project IDs or shared test helpers. Do not introduce dependencies on `$HOME`, `~/.config/ddx`, or the developer's live repository state.
acceptance:
cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bun run test:e2e e2e/navigation.spec.ts src/routes/layout.e2e.ts
cd cli/internal/server/frontend && git diff --exit-code -- playwright.demo.config.ts e2e/demo-recording.spec.ts

title: Fix codex harness: refresh token expired, agent exits with code -1
description: codex exec fails with 401 Unauthorized because the refresh token was already used. The ddx agent harness reports exit code -1 with no diagnostic message. Two issues: (1) codex needs re-auth (codex login), (2) ddx agent run should surface the auth error rather than silently reporting exit code -1.
acceptance:
1. codex login succeeds and codex exec can run prompts again.
2. ddx agent run --harness codex surfaces auth errors (401, token_expired) in its error output rather than just reporting exit code -1.
3. ddx agent check detects codex auth issues and reports them.

title: Update DDx to ddx-agent v0.2.0
description:
<context-digest>
Upstream project: /Users/erik/Projects/agent.
Target release: ddx-agent v0.2.0, to be consumed only after the release tag and GitHub release workflow are complete.
This follows the earlier DDx adoption bead ddx-d4594de5 for v0.1.1.
</context-digest>
Update DDx to consume ddx-agent v0.2.0 once the upstream release is complete.

## Why this update exists
DDx embeds or invokes ddx-agent and should move forward to the next released minor version after the agent release is published cleanly. This bead is intentionally queued now so the DDx-side adoption work is ready as soon as the upstream release is available.

## Upstream changes since the prior DDx adoption point
- acceptance-test coverage was expanded substantially across CLI, runtime, provider, routing, updater, installer, telemetry, and integration surfaces
- CI/build gates were hardened and unified: lint, gosec, govulncheck, format, normal tests, and race tests now share one canonical repo-local/CI command surface
- repo-local lefthook pre-push gates were added so DDx contributors working on ddx-agent can run the same checks locally before push
- Go toolchain pin moved to 1.26.2 to pick up standard-library security fixes required by CI vulnerability scanning
- provider JSON/config output redaction was tightened for CLI security-scan compliance
- telemetry, retry ownership, session continuity, usage/reporting, and related runtime work landed earlier in the same release train and are now part of the released minor version

## DDx work expected
- update the ddx-agent dependency/version pin to the released v0.2.0 artifact or module reference used by DDx
- run DDx integration and acceptance checks against the released version, not a local SHA
- verify that DDx expectations around CLI commands, output formats, usage reporting, and agent-session handling still match upstream behavior
- verify that any DDx automation or docs referencing ddx-agent setup mention the new Go/tooling and hook expectations only if DDx vendors or contributes directly in that repo; do not leak agent-repo-only setup into DDx user docs unless DDx actually depends on it
- capture any DDx-side compatibility fixes separately if the upgrade exposes integration drift

## Notes
Do not start this work until ddx-agent v0.2.0 is actually released and visible as a completed upstream release.
acceptance: DDx updates to the released ddx-agent v0.2.0 artifact/module reference after the upstream release completes; DDx verification passes against that released version; and any DDx-side compatibility fixes or follow-up beads are recorded if the upgrade reveals integration drift

title: Design server agent dashboard for provider availability and utilization
description:
<context-digest>
Review area: server agent observability and operator controls. Evidence covers FEAT-002 server surfaces, FEAT-008 web UI, FEAT-014 token-awareness/routing metrics, the embedded-agent progress lane, and the requirement that a host+user ddx-server expose actionable provider availability and utilization state across configured harnesses/providers.
</context-digest>
Define the server-side agent dashboard that lets operators inspect configured providers and make informed execution decisions.

## Goals
- Show configured providers/harnesses, effective routing availability, auth/health state, and current quota or headroom when known
- Show utilization and performance signals already normalized by DDx: token usage, cost when available, recent latency/success, burn estimates, and freshness timestamps
- Support search/sort/filter/reporting across provider, model, profile, status, and time window
- Distinguish provider-native facts from DDx-derived estimates and from unknown values

## Required spec work
- Update FEAT-002, FEAT-008, and FEAT-014 so the dashboard and its backing read model are explicitly governed
- Define the API/read-model fields exposed to UI consumers and reporting surfaces
- Clarify how live provider availability, cached quota snapshots, and historical usage are presented without inventing certainty

## Required implementation planning
- Define sample/fixture data needed to Playwright-test the dashboard states
- Define how the dashboard relates to worker progress, execution history, and routing diagnostics without collapsing them into one page
acceptance: FEAT-002, FEAT-008, and FEAT-014 define an agent dashboard showing configured providers, availability/health, utilization metrics, freshness, and unknown-state semantics; the design includes a queryable read model and deterministic fixture scenarios for Playwright coverage

title: test: migrate TestExecuteBeadHarnessNoiseNotSynthesized to real git + script harness
description:
Migrate cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_test.go TestExecuteBeadHarnessNoiseNotSynthesized() from fakeExecuteBeadGit + fakeAgentRunner to real git + script harness.

Pattern:
- Use cli/internal/agent/integration_helper_test.go newScriptHarnessRepo(t, beadCount) to build an isolated repo with real git.
- Use runGitInteg (from cli/internal/git/git_basic_test.go) for any supplemental git invocations, so GIT_* env is scrubbed.
- Replace fakeExecuteBeadGit with the RealLandingGitOps flow used in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go.
- Replace fakeAgentRunner with the script harness (cli/internal/agent/script.go) driven by a per-attempt directive file. When the test needs to simulate 'agent emits no commits' or 'agent emits a dirty worktree', encode that as script directives rather than a canned Result struct.
- Preserve every assertion — the point is that the test now exercises real git + real merge/preserve semantics, not that the surface shrinks.

Scope limit: ONLY this test. Do not migrate any other test in the same file; touching another test expands blast radius outside the bead.

Verification: cd cli && go test ./cmd -run '^TestExecuteBeadHarnessNoiseNotSynthesized$' -count=1 passes. No struct ending in -GitOps or -Runner that fakes the thing under test is referenced from this test after the migration.

Governing concern: concerns.md §testing 'no mocks, period' + 'never mock the thing you are testing'. Parent: ddx-d9df348d.
acceptance: 1) TestExecuteBeadHarnessNoiseNotSynthesized no longer references fakeExecuteBeadGit or fakeAgentRunner. 2) Test dispatches through RealLandingGitOps + newScriptHarnessRepo (or equivalent real-git harness). 3) cd cli && go test ./cmd -run '^TestExecuteBeadHarnessNoiseNotSynthesized$' -count=1 green. 4) No other test in agent_execute_bead_test.go is modified.

title: Use native ddx-agent config for the embedded harness
description:
<context-digest>
Review area: embedded-agent configuration ownership. Evidence covers ddx's partial `agent.agent_runner` mirror in `.ddx/config.yaml`, the native `ddx-agent` config model in `.agent/config.yaml`, and the need to support existing provider/backends such as OpenRouter without duplicating or losing configuration surface.
</context-digest>
Replace DDx's lossy embedded-agent config mirror with native `ddx-agent` configuration loading.

## Goals
- Make `.agent/config.yaml` the authoritative config source for the embedded `agent` harness
- Define precedence between native agent config, environment variables, DDx orchestration config, and CLI overrides
- Remove or narrow DDx-owned embedded-agent config fields that duplicate native runtime config
- Ensure DDx `agent run`, `execute-bead`, and `execute-loop` can consume existing OpenRouter/native agent setups without bespoke DDx translation
- Add regression coverage for embedded-agent config resolution and override semantics
acceptance: The design stack defines native `.agent/config.yaml` as the authoritative embedded-agent config source, documents precedence with environment / `.ddx` orchestration config / CLI overrides, and queues implementation work to remove DDx's lossy embedded-agent config mirror while preserving OpenRouter/native runtime compatibility

rationale: closed focused bead with small acceptance surface
title: Remove CLI-side provider construction and route-wrapper helpers
description:
After execution moved behind service.Execute, cmd/agent/provider_build.go and the remaining route-wrapper helpers are architectural leftovers. provider_build.go rebuilds providers from resolved config via internal/config + internal/core. cmd/agent/routing_provider.go still implements Chat/ChatStream/RoutingReport around CLI-owned route candidates. Claude review identified this as a hard boundary violation: provider construction belongs in service/provider-adapter modules only, and the CLI should never see agentcore.Provider values.

Once the service owns route resolution and failover, delete the remaining CLI-side provider construction helpers and make any needed behavior reachable only through service-owned code paths. Keep tests focused on the public service contract and public result/event types rather than CLI-owned provider wrappers.

In-scope files:
- cmd/agent/provider_build.go
- cmd/agent/routing_provider.go
- any cmd/agent tests that assert provider-wrapper behavior instead of service behavior
- service-side replacement code if a helper truly belongs there

Out of scope:
- Session-log persistence changes
- Benchmarking/tool prompt changes unrelated to the boundary cleanup
acceptance:
1. cmd/agent no longer contains provider-building helpers or route-provider wrapper types that depend on internal/core provider interfaces.
2. Execution/routing tests validate service behavior through public requests/events/results instead of CLI-owned provider wrappers.
3. Targeted tests pass: go test ./cmd/agent ./... -run "Test(CLI.*Route|ResolveRoute|Execute.*Routing|ListProviders|ListModels)"

rationale: closed focused bead with small acceptance surface
title: project-local plugin installs + remove ddx install --global
description:
Bead 2 of 4 children under epic ddx-3562c48c. Depends on bead 1 (ddx-c3efa754).

Move plugin installs to project-local; route through skills.Install; reject home-rooted manifests; delete the install --global command.

REGISTRY (cli/internal/registry/installer.go):
- Add projectRoot string parameter to InstallPackage and SymlinkSkillsFromRoot (rename to InstallSkillsFromRoot).
- Extraction target (lines 61-78): change from ~/.ddx/plugins/<plugin>/ to <projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/<plugin>/. ZERO writes to ~/.ddx/plugins/, ~/.agents/skills/, ~/.claude/skills/.
- Manifest with Root.Target starting '~/' returns non-nil error: 'FEAT-015: Root.Target must be project-relative; got <value> in package <name>; update the manifest to use a relative path'. Non-zero exit propagates through ddx install.
- DELETE the <projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/<plugin> → ~/.ddx/plugins/<plugin> symlink (lines 83-91).
- DELETE symlinkSkills (lines 326-423), SymlinkSkillsFromRoot, pruneStaleSkillLinks. All callers route through skills.Install(os.DirFS(<projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/<plugin>), projectRoot, opts).
- DELETE writes to ~/.agents/skills/, ~/.claude/skills/ in registry.go:80-81 and 107-108.

LOCAL INSTALL (cli/cmd/install.go:234-389, the installLocal function):
- Same treatment: extract to <projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/<plugin>/.
- DELETE the legacy ~-symlink at line 318.
- Replace SymlinkSkillsFromRoot call with skills.Install.

REMOVE GLOBAL INSTALL:
- DELETE cli/cmd/install_global.go.
- DELETE cli/cmd/install_global_test.go.
- Remove --global flag from cli/cmd/install.go (cobra default error on unknown flag is acceptable).

COMMIT HYGIENE:
- commitPluginChanges: stage <projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/<plugin>/ in addition to .agents/skills/ and .claude/skills/.

TESTS:
- TestSymlinkSkills_* (cli/internal/registry/registry_test.go:240-391) → rewrite as TestInstallSkills_*. TestSymlinkSkills_PluginRootSymlinkDoesNotEscape replaced with stronger test: walk all installed paths, assert (a) no symlinks, (b) no path resolves outside <projectRoot>.
- cli/cmd/installation_acceptance_test.go: rewrite to expect project-local layout (currently asserts ~/.ddx/plugins/ddx).
- NEW TestInstall_NoSymlinksCreated: post-install of 'ddx init && ddx install <plugin>', walk <projectRoot>/.agents/skills/, .claude/skills/, .ddx/plugins/<plugin>/ — assert ZERO symlinks anywhere. Locks the cross-platform invariant.
- NEW TestInstall_RejectsHomeRootedManifestTarget: package with Root.Target: ~/.ddx/plugins/x → InstallPackage returns the FEAT-015 error.
- NEW TestInstall_GlobalFlagRemoved: ddx install --global <pkg> exits non-zero with cobra unknown-flag error.
- state.Files records project-relative paths after install (assertion in updated TestInstallPackage_*).

OUT OF SCOPE: persona fallback, GraphQL pluginRootCandidates, doctor, ddx update behavior, .gitignore, docs. (Beads 3 and 4.)
acceptance: 1. registry.InstallPackage accepts projectRoot string. 2. Extraction target is <projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/<plugin>/; zero writes to ~/.ddx/plugins/, ~/.agents/skills/, ~/.claude/skills/. 3. Manifest with ~-prefixed Root.Target returns the specified FEAT-015 error; ddx install exits non-zero. Verified by TestInstall_RejectsHomeRootedManifestTarget. 4. installLocal (install.go:234-389) does same; legacy ~-symlink at line 318 deleted. 5. symlinkSkills, SymlinkSkillsFromRoot, pruneStaleSkillLinks all deleted; callers route through skills.Install. 6. <projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/<plugin> → ~/.ddx/plugins/<plugin> symlink no longer created. 7. cli/cmd/install_global.go and install_global_test.go deleted; --global flag removed from install.go. TestInstall_GlobalFlagRemoved passes. 8. commitPluginChanges stages .ddx/plugins/<plugin>/. 9. TestSymlinkSkills_* rewritten as TestInstallSkills_*; PluginRootSymlinkDoesNotEscape replaced with no-symlinks + no-escape walk. 10. installation_acceptance_test.go rewritten to project-local layout. 11. TestInstall_NoSymlinksCreated walks all three target trees and asserts zero symlinks. 12. state.Files records project-relative paths. 13. End-to-end: 'ddx init && ddx install helix' in tmp dir produces tree with zero symlinks. 14. cd cli && go build ./... && go test ./... passes.

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: Phase 5c: migrate ExecuteBead + DefaultBeadReviewer to service.Execute (drop AgentRunner parameter)
description:
Surfaced by ddx-81f71d44 (commit 5b933103). The Runner replacement migrated production callers but ExecuteBead and DefaultBeadReviewer still take an AgentRunner parameter. Until those signatures change, m.buildAgentRunner and f.agentRunner() must construct a Runner.

Scope:
1. ExecuteBead in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go — drop AgentRunner parameter; instead take a service constructor or workDir. Internal: call service.Execute directly.
2. DefaultBeadReviewer in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go — same pattern.
3. Update all callers (workers.go runWorker, ExecuteBead test scaffolding, agent_execute_bead.go).

This is the biggest remaining unblocker. After it lands: f.agentRunner() and m.buildAgentRunner go away, Runner needs only the test-scaffolding callers.
acceptance:

- [ ] DefaultBeadReviewer.Runner field replaced with service-based execution
- [ ] All production callers (workers.go, agent_execute_bead.go) migrated
- [ ] Tests pass; build clean

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: routing: add statement-backed policy invariant tests
description: Add a robust routing policy test suite tied directly to simple human-readable statements. In-scope: internal/routing tests and service routing tests only. Each table row should include a policy_statement string so failures map back to rules such as 'if local LLMs are available and satisfy constraints, use them', 'hard pins are exclusive', and 'provider/deployment class prevents local/community benchmark ties with cloud frontier models'. This bead adds coverage; it should not introduce new routing semantics beyond what prior routing beads implemented.
acceptance: 1. A table-driven policy invariant test suite exists with policy_statement names for each case. 2. Tests cover: config only sets up provider sources/endpoints while routing uses discovered inventory; no-power default chooses best/cheapest viable auto-routable model; local/free available and satisfies constraints wins over paid/cloud; local/free unavailable falls back; local/free below MinPower loses; exact model pin overrides local preference; provider-source/endpoint pin and harness pin are exclusive; unknown-power models are exact-pin-only; provider/deployment class keeps local/community gpt-oss/minimax-class candidates below cloud Sonnet/Opus/GPT when benchmark inputs tie; agent does not retry candidate 2. 3. Test failure messages include the policy_statement. 4. go test ./internal/routing ./... -run 'Policy|Invariant|Routing' passes or a documented narrower equivalent passes.

Updated the service no-retry regression to include policy_statement in failure messages. Verification: go test ./internal/routing ./... -run 'Policy|Invariant|Routing' -count=1 passed; go test ./... passed.

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: execute-loop: --harness flag silently ignored when --model is a provider name
description:
When execute-loop is invoked with both --harness claude and --model <provider-name> (e.g. --model claude-sonnet-4-6), the --harness flag is silently ignored and the embedded agent harness is used instead, routing to whichever LM Studio provider is configured as default.

Observed: started execute-loop --local --harness claude --model claude-sonnet-4-6. Every session in the output showed 'model: vidar' or 'model: bragi' and all LLM responses were from qwen3.5-27b. No claude traffic was generated. The claude binary is healthy (ddx agent doctor reports OK). When execute-loop is started with --harness claude and NO --model flag, the claude binary is correctly invoked (confirmed by claude --print process appearing in ps output).

Root cause hypothesis: the --model flag value is being interpreted as a provider/route key by the embedded agent harness dispatch layer. When the model string matches a known provider name or a catalog entry that maps to the embedded-openai surface, the harness selection silently switches to 'agent' regardless of what --harness specifies. The --harness flag loses the race.

Required fix: --harness must take strict precedence over any routing implied by --model. If --harness claude is specified and the model string is not a valid claude model ID, the loop should either (a) pass the model string through to the claude binary as-is and let it fail with a clear error, or (b) emit a startup warning 'model X is not a recognized claude model; ignoring --model flag' and proceed with the harness default. Silent rerouting to a different harness is never acceptable.

Additionally: when the harness override causes a routing mismatch, the error or warning must appear before any bead is claimed, not after execution has started.
acceptance: ddx agent execute-loop --harness claude --model claude-sonnet-4-6 invokes the claude binary, not ddx-agent; session log shows model: claude-sonnet-4-6 not model: vidar or model: bragi; if --model specifies an unrecognized claude model, the error is surfaced before any bead is claimed; ddx agent execute-loop --harness agent --model vidar continues to work as before (regression guard)

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: Server: dedupe and garbage-collect the project registry; tombstone unreachable projects
description:
GET /api/projects currently returns 4186 entries on the eitri node. The real number of projects on this machine is single-digit. Root cause: the per-CLI-invocation auto-registration in serverreg.TryRegisterAsync registers the project on EVERY ddx command run with no dedupe, AND historical bugs (CWD drift in linked worktrees, the stale beads-store-write bug fixed in 26c0266) caused thousands of phantom 'projects' to land with paths like /Users/erik/Projects/ddx/.claude/worktrees/agent-XYZ. The registry has no GC, no path canonicalization, no reachability check.

Fix scope:
1. Path canonicalization on register: filepath.Abs + EvalSymlinks before storing, so /tmp and /Users/erik/Projects/.... are normalized
2. Detect linked-worktree paths (via git rev-parse --git-common-dir) and store the PRIMARY worktree path, never the linked worktree path itself — this is the same fix we did for FindNearestDDxWorkspace in commit 26c0266 but applied to project registration
3. Dedupe on register: if a project with the same canonical path already exists, update its last_seen instead of creating a new entry
4. Reachability sweep: a periodic background goroutine (or a manual ddx server projects gc command) that stat()'s each registered project's path and either (a) marks it as 'unreachable' with a tombstone timestamp or (b) removes entries whose path has been gone for >24h
5. Migration: on server startup, run dedupe + path canonicalization + reachability sweep ONCE against the existing state file so the eitri 4186 entries collapse to the real count
6. GET /api/projects gains a ?include_unreachable= query (default false) so the UI can hide tombstones unless asked
acceptance: Backend Go test in cli/internal/server/server_test.go: (1) registers the same project path 5 times with t.Setenv-isolated state dir, asserts GET /api/projects returns exactly 1 project with last_seen = the most recent registration. (2) registers a path inside a linked worktree (via git worktree add fixture), asserts the stored path is the PRIMARY worktree, not the linked path. (3) registers a project, deletes the path on disk, runs the reachability sweep (expose a Server.SweepProjects helper for tests), asserts the project is marked unreachable with a tombstone timestamp, then asserts GET /api/projects with default params hides it and ?include_unreachable=true shows it. Migration test: writes a state file with 50 duplicate entries for the same canonical path, starts the server, asserts the post-startup state file has exactly 1 entry. Acceptance smoke: running the migration on the eitri server's current state file reduces the count from 4186 to single-digit, verifiable by curl https://127.0.0.1:7743/api/projects | jq length.

title: prompt-ingress: hard-fail oversize on agent-side file readers (Stage D1)
description:
Stage D1 of FEAT-022. Route agent-side --prompt file readers through
evidence.ReadFileClamped. Hard-fail on oversize with an actionable
error naming source, observed size, and configured cap.

Implements FEAT-022 requirements §8 and §9 for the agent-side sites
(CLI and server-dispatch file readers). Satisfies US-223 for the CLI
path.

Depends on Stage A1 (ddx-64299657) only.

In-scope files (5 sites, all in cli/internal/agent/):
- runner.go (resolvePrompt at :340)
- compare_adapter.go (defaultResolvePromptForCompare at :181)
- compare_adapter.go (benchmark suite reader at :614)
- service_run.go (PromptFile reader at :83) — confirmed path
- execute_bead.go (promptOverride raw read at :1291)

Each site replaces the raw os.ReadFile with
evidence.ReadFileClamped(path, evidence.MaxPromptBytes). On oversize:
return a non-nil error whose message contains the file path, observed
size in bytes, configured cap in bytes, and a hint pointing at
.ddx/config.yaml:evidence_caps.max_prompt_bytes.

Out-of-scope:
- Server-side inline prompt ingress (req.Prompt, DDX_AGENT_PROMPT env,
  docgraph prompt metadata) — separate bead Stage D2.
- Server text egress — Stage E.
- Any new evidence primitives — if one is needed, add to Stage A1 via
  a blocked-on dependency; do NOT inline a one-off here.

Rollback: revert the ReadFileClamped calls to os.ReadFile at each
site.
acceptance: cd cli && go test -run TestPromptIngressOversize ./internal/agent/... passes with one subtest per in-scope site: each subtest writes a fixture file = 2× MaxPromptBytes to a temp dir, invokes the site under test, asserts the call returns a non-nil error whose message contains (a) the fixture path, (b) the observed size, (c) the cap in bytes, (d) the string 'evidence_caps.max_prompt_bytes'. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... passes. Integration smoke: run 'ddx agent run --prompt /tmp/huge-prompt.bin' where the file is 2× MaxPromptBytes; assert exit code != 0 and stderr contains the expected error-message substrings. AST assertion (via TestEvidencePrimitiveUsage in cli/internal/agent/): every os.ReadFile / ioutil.ReadFile call inside runner.go, compare_adapter.go, service_run.go, execute_bead.go whose argument is an opts.PromptFile-style symbol carries an //evidence:allow-unbounded annotation OR has been replaced with evidence.ReadFileClamped.

title: Step 2 — Profile schema + Go loader (smoke + noop test profiles)
description: Define the v7 profile YAML schema at scripts/benchmark/profiles/<id>.yaml (id; provider.{type,model,base_url,api_key_env}; pricing.{input,output,cached_input}_usd_per_mtok; limits.{max_output_tokens,context_tokens,rate_limit_rpm,rate_limit_tpm}; sampling.{temperature,reasoning}; versioning.{resolved_at,snapshot}). Implement Go loader at internal/benchmark/profile/. Ship two profiles: smoke (cheapest OpenAI-compat for plumbing) and noop (test fixture for adapter unit tests). The anchor profile lands separately as NEW18 after the Step 0 census closes.
acceptance: Loader + smoke profile + noop test profile land; unit test loads and validates required fields; ddx-agent-bench profiles list prints a table including smoke and noop.

title: drift: fix stale frontend build paths in ADR-002 examples
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-002-web-stack.md:197
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: ADR-002's build and Docker examples still use root-level 'frontend/' and 'server/' directories (lines 197-214), but this repository's frontend lives at 'cli/internal/server/frontend/' and there is no root 'frontend/' or 'server/' directory. Following the ADR would send implementers to paths that do not exist.
Suggested fix: Rewrite the build and Docker examples to use the repository's actual frontend and Go build paths, or mark the snippets as abstract pseudocode instead of concrete commands.
acceptance: ADR-002 no longer references nonexistent root 'frontend/' or 'server/' paths in its build or Docker examples; the documented paths either match this repository or are clearly labeled as illustrative only.

title: [read-coverage] add ddx_bead_blocked + ddx_bead_dep_tree MCP tools
description: FEAT-004/002 gap. HTTP has GET /api/beads/blocked and GET /api/beads/dep/tree/{id}. Add both tools.

rationale: closed focused bead with small acceptance surface
title: fix: agent exits non-zero after successful task completion in benchmark harness
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Tests first · Design for simplicity</principles>
<concerns>benchmark | harness</concerns>
<practices>Go · ddx-agent CLI</practices>
<governing>SD-009 benchmark mode</governing>
</context-digest>

When ddx-agent completes a task successfully (Harbor verifier scores it 1.0), the process still exits with a non-zero exit code. Harbor records this as NonZeroAgentExitCodeError and counts it as an error in n_errors, even though the reward is 1.0.

Observed in benchmark run 20260412T172048Z:
- build-pmars: score=1.0, NonZeroAgentExitCodeError
- code-from-image: score=1.0, NonZeroAgentExitCodeError

Root cause to investigate: the agent likely exits non-zero when the session ends with no more iterations (ErrNoMoreAttempts or similar) or when the --json flag causes the process to exit with a status reflecting internal state rather than task outcome. In benchmark mode the exit code should be 0 on clean completion regardless of whether the model hit iteration limits.

Files to check: cmd/ddx-agent/main.go exit path, loop.go termination conditions. The fix is likely setting exit code 0 when the agent loop ends cleanly (exhausted iterations or received done signal) vs. exits on an unrecoverable error.
acceptance: Running ddx-agent --json --preset benchmark on a task that produces correct output exits with code 0. Harbor no longer records NonZeroAgentExitCodeError for solved tasks. Verified by re-running build-pmars and code-from-image tasks and observing n_errors=0 in result.json alongside score=1.0.

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: Add structured embedded-agent progress output for workers and UI
description:
<context-digest>
Review area: embedded ddx-agent runtime observability and live worker progress. Evidence covers FEAT-006 execution/evidence ownership, FEAT-002 server activity surfaces, FEAT-008 web UI expectations, execute-loop worker supervision, and the need for DDx-owned progress telemetry beyond coarse session logs when DDx runs the embedded harness.
</context-digest>
Define the progress-event contract for managed embedded-agent runs so DDx can surface meaningful live execution status in the CLI and server/UI.

## Goals
- Define structured phase/heartbeat output for embedded-agent runs (queueing, launch, running, post-checks, landing, preserved, done)
- Define the minimal per-event fields needed for operator visibility and later reporting: worker/project/bead/run identity, phase, elapsed/heartbeat timing, harness/provider/model/profile, and token/cost deltas when available
- Clarify which live progress artifacts remain runtime-only and which summaries/indexes are exposed through DDx-owned APIs and UI surfaces
- Keep provider-native transcript ownership external; DDx progress output should be normalized runtime telemetry, not transcript duplication

## Required spec work
- Update FEAT-006, FEAT-002, and FEAT-008 so embedded-agent progress visibility is an explicit contract rather than an implementation detail
- Clarify how progress events relate to tracked execution artifacts, session logs, and server-managed worker state

## Required implementation planning
- Define the server/API/UI read model for live worker progress and recent phase history
- Define how CLI and UI consume the same structured progress feed without introducing hidden workflow policy
acceptance: FEAT-006, FEAT-002, and FEAT-008 define a structured embedded-agent progress contract for managed runs; the contract names the required phases and fields, distinguishes runtime-only progress from tracked execution evidence, and defines one shared read model for CLI and server/UI consumers

Fresh-eyes review complete for commit 07d641b52a1fa8bca01585856713b4d9f66fd4df.
Finding filed: hx-1619d2ac.
Pass 1 correctness: the commit repairs hx-6a7a8bde so replay provenance points at the actual fix commit 473d4a1f12678fc238838d2ba678086214168df6 instead of the prior c81792c metadata row.
Pass 2 integration: the rewritten closing_commit_sha is stored as the 7-character prefix 473d4a1 rather than the full object ID. FEAT-019 replay resolves this field with git rev-parse and expects durable commit-boundary provenance, so the abbreviation is replayable today but not stable over time if the prefix becomes ambiguous.
Pass 3 concern-aware quality: no new runtime attack surface or tooling drift; concern-aware review focused on tracker-provenance correctness and replay durability for a tracker-only change.
Pass 4 operational learnings: no AGENTS.md change required; no learnings bead required.

No AGENTS.md update required.

title: correctness: reject malformed prompt envelopes missing prompt
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cmd/ddx-agent/main.go:331
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Tightening envelope detection to require all three keys in the JSON probe fixes plain JSON prompts with a top-level kind field, but it also stops rejecting malformed DDx envelopes that omit the required prompt field. A payload like '{"kind":"prompt","id":"task-42","inputs":{"paths":["main.go"]}}' is now treated as raw prompt text and execution proceeds to provider selection instead of returning the existing invalid prompt envelope error. That breaks the structured-I/O contract and the feature bead's acceptance requirement to cover invalid envelope input deterministically.
Suggested fix: keep the backward-compatibility fix for plain JSON, but continue treating objects that look like DDx envelopes (at minimum kind plus another envelope field such as id/inputs/response_schema/callback) as envelope candidates so missing required fields still fail validation; add a regression test for the missing-prompt case.
acceptance: A JSON payload intended as a DDx prompt envelope but missing the required prompt field fails with an invalid prompt envelope error for -p, stdin, and @file input paths, while non-envelope JSON objects like {"kind":"note","text":"hello"} still pass through as raw prompt text; regression tests cover both behaviors.

title: workersByProject compares project id to project path: per-project workers view always empty
description:
## Observed

User clicks "Drain queue" on the project home page. Mutation returns success with a worker id. Navigating to `/nodes/.../projects/<id>/workers` shows no workers — an empty table.

Verified on disk: the worker record exists and is running.

```
$ cat .ddx/workers/worker-20260422T205846-f23f/status.json
{
  "id": "worker-20260422T205846-f23f",
  "kind": "execute-loop",
  "state": "running",
  "project_root": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx",
  ...
}
```

So the dispatch worked, but the per-project workers list filters it out.

## Root cause

`cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:156` in `GetWorkersGraphQL`:

```go
if projectID != "" && rec.ProjectRoot != projectID {
    continue
}
```

- `projectID` is the GraphQL argument — a project **id** like `proj-96d7ea83`.
- `rec.ProjectRoot` is the worker record field — a project **path** like `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx`.

These are different representations and never match. With any non-empty `projectID`, every worker is filtered out.

The global `workers` resolver (`resolver_agent.go:9`) passes `""` and works correctly. Only the per-project path (`workersByProject` → `GetWorkersGraphQL(projectID)`) is broken. The user's flow (dispatch from project home → navigate to project workers) hits exactly this path.

Note: `GetBeadSnapshots` (state_graphql.go:49-105) handles the same concept correctly by iterating `s.Projects` and filtering on `proj.ID != projectID` — comparing id to id. The worker resolver does not use that pattern.

## Proposed direction

Two options; prefer (1) for minimal change, (2) for long-term clarity.

### Option 1 — Resolve id → path at the filter site

```go
expectedPath := ""
if projectID != "" {
    proj, ok := s.GetProjectByID(projectID)
    if !ok {
        return nil // unknown project id → no workers
    }
    expectedPath = proj.Path
}
...
if expectedPath != "" && rec.ProjectRoot != expectedPath {
    continue
}
```

### Option 2 — Store project id on the worker record

Add `ProjectID string` to `WorkerRecord`. Populate at creation time in `WorkerManager.StartExecuteLoop` via `s.GetProjectByPath(effectiveRoot)`. Filter on `rec.ProjectID != projectID`. Cleaner — the worker record carries its own identity and the query doesn't depend on runtime resolution. Requires a tiny migration (for older records missing the field, fall back to path-resolve as in Option 1).

Recommend Option 2. It removes the representation mismatch for good and is cheap.

## Scope note

This is a one-line functional bug with outsized impact: it's the reason the Workers page looks broken whenever used as the dashboard intends. Prioritizing P1.

## Out of scope

- Workers pane lifecycle controls (`ddx-69789664`).
- Cross-project workers view — the global `workers` query already handles that.
acceptance:
**User story:** As an operator, clicking Drain queue on a project home page starts a worker that I can immediately see in that project's Workers view, with its state and current bead updating live.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. **Root-cause fix.** The `workersByProject(projectID:)` resolver returns all workers whose target project matches the requested id. Both Option 1 (id→path resolution) and Option 2 (store project id on record) satisfy this; implementation chooses one with a one-line justification in the PR description.

2. **Regression test — project-scoped query.**
   - Seed a ServerState with two registered projects (different paths and ids).
   - Start one worker for each project.
   - `workersByProject(projectID: <id-1>)` returns exactly worker 1.
   - `workersByProject(projectID: <id-2>)` returns exactly worker 2.
   - `workers` (global) returns both.
   - Unknown project id returns empty list, not an error.

3. **Regression test — backward compat.**
   - A worker record on disk without a `ProjectID` field (simulating a pre-migration record) is correctly matched by the path-resolution fallback. Only required if Option 2 is chosen.

4. **End-to-end validation.**
   - Playwright: navigate to project home, click Drain, confirm, wait 2s, navigate to Workers tab. Assert exactly one new row with `state: running` and matching worker id.
   - Same test run against a second project asserts that project's Workers tab does not show project-1's worker.

5. **No regression to unrelated surfaces.**
   - Global `workers` query still returns all workers.
   - Worker detail page still loads by worker id.
   - Workers live-progress subscription still patches rows in place.

6. **Cross-reference.**
   - Note on `ddx-69789664` (Workers lifecycle controls): that bead's acceptance assumed Workers pane worked correctly. Point to this bead as the prerequisite for those tests to be meaningful.

bead.notes:
**AC4 — Playwright e2e test missing.** The acceptance criteria require a Playwright test that navigates to project home, clicks Drain, confirms the worker appears in the Workers tab, and verifies cross-project isolation. No Playwright test was added. File to add: `cli/internal/server/frontend/tests/` (or wherever existing Playwright tests live). The test should cover: (1) Drain → Workers tab shows new row with `state: running`, (2) second project's Workers tab does not show project-1's worker.

REVIEW:BLOCK

`cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go` / `GetWorkersGraphQL` is not changed in the diff, so AC1’s id-to-path root-cause fix is not implemented or evidenced.
`cli/internal/server/*test.go` has no diff adding the required two-project `workersByProject` regression coverage, global `workers` assertion, or unknown-project assertion for AC2.
No worker-record compatibility test is added for AC3, and the diff does not show whether Option 1 or Option 2 was chosen.
`cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/*` has no diff adding the required Drain queue to Workers tab Playwright flow or second-project isolation check for AC4.
No evidence is added for AC5 that global workers, worker detail loading, or live-progress row patching still work.
No note or tracker/documentation update cross-references `ddx-69789664` as depending on this bead for AC6.

Bead ddx-05b4cc9d: no new changes needed — all AC already satisfied by prior
execute-bead attempts merged to main and present at the base rev of this
worktree (a21580e1).

## What is done

AC1 — Root-cause fix (Option 1, id→path at filter site)
    cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go lines 267-299, landed in commit
    818b5c9e "fix(server): workersByProject filters by project path, not id
    [ddx-05b4cc9d]". GetWorkersGraphQL now calls s.GetProjectByID(projectID),
    derives expectedPath = proj.Path, and compares rec.ProjectRoot to the
    resolved path. Unknown project id returns empty (not error).

AC2 — Two-project regression test
    cli/internal/server/graphql_backfill_test.go —
    TestGraphQLWorkersByProjectScopedByID (new) seeds two registered projects
    with distinct paths/ids, writes a worker targeting each, and asserts:
      * workersByProject(id=A) returns only worker A
      * workersByProject(id=B) returns only worker B
      * workers (global) returns both
      * workersByProject(id=unknown) returns empty (no GraphQL error)
    TestGraphQLWorkers was also updated to query by resolved project id,
    so it actually exercises the fix.

    Verified locally:
      $ cd cli && go test -run "TestGraphQLWorkersByProjectScopedByID|TestGraphQLWorkers" ./internal/server/ -v
      --- PASS: TestGraphQLWorkers (0.01s)
      --- PASS: TestGraphQLWorkersByProjectScopedByID (0.01s)
      ok  github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/server

AC3 — Backward-compat test for pre-migration worker records
    N/A. AC3 is conditional on choosing Option 2 (add ProjectID field to
    WorkerRecord). Option 1 was chosen; WorkerRecord schema is unchanged
    and no migration path exists, so the compatibility concern does not
    apply.

AC4 — Playwright e2e validation
    cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/actions.spec.ts lines 129-226, landed
    in commit 5f234aad "test(ui): cover project-scoped drain worker flow
    [ddx-05b4cc9d]". The test navigates to the project home page, clicks
    Drain queue, confirms the dialog, waits, navigates to the Workers tab,
    and asserts exactly one row with state "running" and the matching
    worker id prefix. Then it navigates to a second project's Workers tab
    and asserts that project-1's worker does not appear and the empty-state
    message is visible.

AC5 — No regression to unrelated surfaces
    * Global workers query still returns all workers — covered by the
      "global workers returns both" assertion in AC2's regression test.
    * Worker detail page loads by worker id — GetWorkerGraphQL(id) was
      not touched by the fix; it keys on worker id, not project id/path.
    * workerProgress subscription patches rows in place — the subscription
      resolver was not modified; only the list-filter comparison changed.

AC6 — Cross-reference to ddx-69789664
    Present in the 818b5c9e commit message:
      "Unblocks the Workers-lifecycle-controls bead (ddx-69789664) whose
       acceptance assumed the Workers pane worked correctly."

## Why nothing changes in this attempt

The bead's <notes> section contains BLOCKING review findings from the first
attempt (before 818b5c9e landed). Attempts 1 and 2 already addressed each
finding:

  818b5c9e — server fix + first regression test (AC1, AC2, AC6)
  5f234aad — Playwright e2e (AC4)

Both commits are ancestors of HEAD (a21580e1):
  $ git merge-base --is-ancestor 818b5c9e HEAD  # true
  $ git merge-base --is-ancestor 5f234aad HEAD  # true

The files in the worktree already contain the fix and the tests. Running
the tests shows they pass. Re-committing the same changes would produce
an empty diff or duplicate history. A well-justified no-changes is the
correct outcome here; the bead should be closed by the reviewer on the
basis of the evidence already in main.

## What a reviewer should check to confirm

  git log --oneline -- cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go \
      cli/internal/server/graphql_backfill_test.go \
      cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/actions.spec.ts
  # expect to see 818b5c9e and 5f234aad

  grep -n expectedPath cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go
  # expect the id→path resolution in GetWorkersGraphQL

  cd cli && go test -run TestGraphQLWorkersByProjectScopedByID ./internal/server/ -v
  # expect PASS

title: test: migrate TestExecuteBeadNoMerge to real git + script harness
description:
Migrate cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_test.go TestExecuteBeadNoMerge() from fakeExecuteBeadGit + fakeAgentRunner to real git + script harness.

Pattern:
- Use cli/internal/agent/integration_helper_test.go newScriptHarnessRepo(t, beadCount) to build an isolated repo with real git.
- Use runGitInteg (from cli/internal/git/git_basic_test.go) for any supplemental git invocations, so GIT_* env is scrubbed.
- Replace fakeExecuteBeadGit with the RealLandingGitOps flow used in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go.
- Replace fakeAgentRunner with the script harness (cli/internal/agent/script.go) driven by a per-attempt directive file. When the test needs to simulate 'agent emits no commits' or 'agent emits a dirty worktree', encode that as script directives rather than a canned Result struct.
- Preserve every assertion — the point is that the test now exercises real git + real merge/preserve semantics, not that the surface shrinks.

Scope limit: ONLY this test. Do not migrate any other test in the same file; touching another test expands blast radius outside the bead.

Verification: cd cli && go test ./cmd -run '^TestExecuteBeadNoMerge$' -count=1 passes. No struct ending in -GitOps or -Runner that fakes the thing under test is referenced from this test after the migration.

Governing concern: concerns.md §testing 'no mocks, period' + 'never mock the thing you are testing'. Parent: ddx-d9df348d.
acceptance: 1) TestExecuteBeadNoMerge no longer references fakeExecuteBeadGit or fakeAgentRunner. 2) Test dispatches through RealLandingGitOps + newScriptHarnessRepo (or equivalent real-git harness). 3) cd cli && go test ./cmd -run '^TestExecuteBeadNoMerge$' -count=1 green. 4) No other test in agent_execute_bead_test.go is modified.

rationale: three or more attempts without a closing commit
title: Implement execute-loop-first help, skill, and operator guidance
description:
<context-digest>
Review area: consumer-facing execute-bead / execute-loop guidance. Evidence covers the shipped CLI help surfaces, the open docs gaps tracked by ddx-a4195a8e and ddx-7a01ba6c, and the need to teach humans and agents one coherent model: execute-bead is the primitive, execute-loop is the normal queue-driven surface.
</context-digest>
Implement the first complete consumer-facing guidance surface for DDx agent execution.

## Required changes
1. Update CLI help text and any nearby command docs so `ddx agent execute-bead` is described as the single-bead primitive and `ddx agent execute-loop` is described as the queue/supervisor command built on top of it.
2. Add or update user-facing operator docs and CLI reference surfaces so users discover `execute-loop` first for normal queue-driven work, with concrete examples for:
   - `ddx agent execute-loop`
   - `ddx agent execute-loop --once`
   - `ddx agent execute-loop --poll-interval <duration>`
   - `ddx agent execute-bead <bead-id>` for bounded/manual runs
3. Update the ddx-bead skill and any relevant internal skill/operator guidance so agents are told explicitly:
   - planning/document-only beads are valid execution targets
   - execute-loop is the default queue-driven entrypoint
   - execute-bead is the lower-level primitive for targeted/manual execution
4. Document the result/close semantics operators need in practice:
   - which outcomes/statuses close a bead
   - which outcomes preserve and leave the bead open
   - which JSON fields automation should read (`status`, `outcome`, `preserve_ref`, `result_rev`, `session_id`)
5. Make the operator guidance consistent with the current merge model:
   - merge is the default successful outcome
   - explicit required execution documents are the automatic merge gates
   - `--no-merge` is the manual preserve override

## Boundaries
- Do not invent a new bead shape or merge-gate field
- Use the existing execute-bead / execute-loop / execution-document contract
- Keep the user-facing guidance aligned with the actual shipped command behavior and documented result surface
acceptance: CLI help, operator docs, and ddx-bead skill guidance all teach the same model: execute-bead is the primitive, execute-loop is the normal queue-driven surface, planning/document-only beads are valid execution targets, merge is default unless explicit required execution docs block landing or --no-merge is set, and the documented JSON/result semantics tell operators and automation how close/preserve outcomes behave

title: B22d-f: SessionIndexEntryFromResult takes discrete args, not RunOptions
description:
Step f/h of B22d decomposition.

Change SessionIndexEntryFromResult's RunOptions parameter to take only the small subset of fields it actually uses (Harness, Model, Provider, Correlation, PromptSource, etc.) as discrete arguments or a small internal struct. Update session_index_test.go and the four service-run callers in the same commit.

In-scope:
- cli/internal/agent/session_index.go SessionIndexEntryFromResult signature
- cli/internal/agent/session_index_test.go
- cli/internal/agent/service_run.go callers

Out-of-scope:
- Other session_index changes
acceptance: field RunOptions removed from cli/internal/agent/session_index.go. cd cli && go build ./... passes. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... passes.

events.body:
no_changes
rationale: Bead ddx-9f4b1a8c (B22d-f) cannot be landed in isolation against base 4267be6c.

What is done
- The signature change itself is straightforward and verified locally:
  SessionIndexEntryFromResult(projectRoot string, harness, model, provider, effort string,
                              correlation map[string]string, result *Result,
                              startedAt, endedAt time.Time) SessionIndexEntry
  - cli/internal/agent/session_index.go: removed the RunOptions field/parameter
    and rewired the body to read from the discrete params (harness/model/provider/
    effort/correlation). After this change, RunOptions no longer appears in
    session_index.go (AC #1).
  - The single existing caller in cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:257
    (the bead's "four service-run callers" overcounts; only one call site exists)
    was updated to:
      SessionIndexEntryFromResult(workDir, opts.Harness, opts.Model,
                                  opts.Provider, opts.Effort, opts.Correlation,
                                  result, start, finishedAt)
  - cli/internal/agent/session_index_test.go (TestSessionIndexPreservesWorker-
    IDCorrelation, line ~195) was updated to call the new signature with
    discrete args. The test passes.
  - `cd cli && go build ./...` passes (AC #2).
  - `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/...` runs clean for everything that
    isn't TestIntegration_CodexEcho. That test fails for an unrelated reason
    (real codex CLI returns "thread not found") on this machine — it is not
    a regression introduced by this change, and -short does not skip it.

What is blocking
- The lefthook pre-commit `runtime-lint` rule (lefthook.yml) runs the
  SD-024 runtimelint analyzer and filters its diagnostics down to the set
  of staged files. Because the bead's in-scope files
  (cli/internal/agent/service_run.go, cli/internal/agent/session_index_test.go)
  contain many out-of-scope RunOptions usages — RunViaService,
  runFixtureHarnessViaRunner, RunWithConfigViaService, RunViaServiceWith,
  the `opts := RunOptions{...}` composite at service_run.go:82, and the
  TestRunViaServiceWithAppendsOneSessionIndexRow composite at
  session_index_test.go:274 — the lint blocks the commit even though none
  of those usages are touched by this bead.
- The bead's stated out-of-scope explicitly excludes "service_run.go public
  API deletions (step h)" (per the parent step-d bead description). Those
  call sites and signatures are what the lint is flagging.
- Running the analyzer over ./internal/agent/... reports many other
  RunOptions references across the package (compare_adapter.go,
  execute_bead.go, execute_bead_review.go, grade.go, runner.go,
  agent_runner.go, agent_runner_service.go, claude_stream.go, models.go,
  etc.) that depend on later B22d steps and the broader Stage-4 retirement.
- Per project policy, --no-verify / hook-skipping is forbidden; per the
  bead instructions, modifying out-of-scope files is forbidden. There is no
  in-scope way to make the lint pass on the staged-file set.

What a follow-up attempt would need
- Land step h (or the broader RunOptions retirement) FIRST so that
  service_run.go and session_index_test.go no longer contain pre-existing
  RunOptions usages, then this bead can run and the lint will pass on the
  staged-file set; OR
- Re-decompose the lint scope: change runtime-lint in lefthook.yml to
  filter diagnostics to only the staged hunk lines (e.g. via a diff-aware
  pass) rather than the whole staged file, so a partial migration step
  isn't blocked by file-level peers; OR
- Expand this bead's in-scope to include the full RunOptions removal from
  service_run.go and session_index_test.go (effectively folding step h into
  step f), and re-queue with a model that can carry the broader change.

The diff that was prepared and then reverted is reproducible from the bead
description in 5 minutes; the blocker is project-level lint policy versus
bead scope, not implementation difficulty.

rationale: no_changes/review text says bead needs splitting
title: exec: tier-based auto-escalation driven by resource discovery
description:
The CLI should not require the user to enumerate harness/model pairs. The tool already knows which tiers exist (model-catalog.yaml: cheap/standard/smart) and which harnesses are configured. Given a live probe of each provider's health at attempt time, the loop can auto-escalate on its own.

## Principle

Discovery over configuration. The user shouldn't write a fallback chain — the tool should figure it out.

## Default policy (no flags)

- Start at **cheap** tier, execute the bead
- If merge succeeds AND post-review (gap #1) APPROVES → close
- If anything fails (execution_failed, no_changes after N retries, post_run_check_failed, REQUEST_CHANGES from review) → escalate one tier and retry
- Chain: cheap → standard → smart → exhausted (preserve, flag for human)
- Review agent runs at max(impl_tier+1, smart). Never review cheap with cheap.

## Resource discovery at attempt time

- Before picking a concrete harness/model for a tier, probe each candidate provider (local inference host, API key validity, network reachability)
- If provider returns 502 / 500 / timeout / auth-rejected within a short budget, mark it unhealthy with a cooldown and try the next provider in the tier
- If every provider in a tier is unhealthy, skip the tier and escalate
- Per-provider health state is process-local and visible via a new 'ddx agent providers' sub-command (ddx-09ea1884 Visibility 3)

## Rare overrides

- --min-tier standard (skip cheap — useful for hard beads known to need it)
- --max-tier standard (never reach smart — cost control)
- --harness <name> / --model <name> (debug override; disables escalation)

## Surface

Default invocation becomes:

  ddx agent execute-loop --once

Everything else is implicit. Review, escalation, and resource discovery are on-by-default.

## Events

Each attempt appends an execute-bead event: { harness, model, tier, provider, probe_result, attempt_status, review_status }. The event stream shows exactly why a tier/provider was picked or skipped.

## Context

Observed during axon ADR-018 loop:
- bragi was down (502) — I manually probed with curl and switched to vidar-coder. Tool should do this.
- qwen3-coder-next merged but opus review rejected — I manually re-invoked with sonnet. Tool should escalate automatically.
- Resulting CLI invocation in my notes was too complex (—-fallback-chain a/b,c/d,e/f --post-review g/h). User feedback: too many knobs. Reach for a default that just works.

## Acceptance criteria replaced with discovery-driven ones (see accept field)
acceptance:
- [ ] execute-loop with zero flags escalates across cheap → standard → smart on failure
- [ ] Review agent auto-selects a tier >= impl tier, defaulting to smart
- [ ] Provider health probe runs at attempt time; unhealthy providers skip cleanly with cooldown
- [ ] Each attempt records tier + provider + probe_result + outcome on the bead
- [ ] Integration test: cheap provider 502 + standard succeeds → bead closes with escalation trail visible
- [ ] --min-tier / --max-tier overrides work and are documented
- [ ] --harness / --model debug overrides disable escalation
- [ ] Zero-flag 'ddx agent execute-loop' is the documented happy path

bead.notes:
<operator-feedback 2026-04-15>
Additional observation: --local is too prominent as a flag. The default IS server-worker mode, but I (an experienced DDx operator) still reached for --local reflexively because I wanted a synchronous handle to the subprocess. The root cause: I didn't know how to tail a server-side worker from the CLI.

Possible fixes (minor, could be part of this bead's scope):
- 'ddx agent execute-loop --once --follow' that submits to server and tails the worker log to stdout until completion, then returns. Gives the synchronous-feel without forking a subprocess.
- 'ddx server workers wait <worker-id>' that blocks until the named worker is in a terminal state.
- Document in execute-loop --help that --local is for debugging only; the primary surface is server-worker.

Not worth a separate bead — just ergonomics for the already-correct default.
</operator-feedback>

rationale: three or more attempts without a closing commit
title: work: ready epics are silently skipped by execution loop
description:
In the agent repo on 2026-04-20, `ddx bead ready --json` reported three ready open beads: two epics and one task. The task (`agent-05d81638`) was under retry cooldown until 2026-04-20T06:08:22Z. Running `ddx work --once` started a worker (`worker-20260420T004727-aff9`) that exited with 0 attempts and no log output. Only after running `ddx bead ready --execution --json` was it clear that the execution-ready queue was empty.

This is confusing operator behavior: `ready` says there is work, but `work` silently does nothing because epics are not execution-eligible. If epics are intentionally container-only, the default ready surface or the work-loop output should make that distinction explicit. If epics are supposed to be executable planning/decomposition targets, the work loop should handle them rather than dropping them.

Expected behavior: when `ddx work` skips ready epics, the operator gets a clear reason and next action, e.g. "0 execution-ready beads; skipped 2 epics because epics require child task decomposition; 1 task blocked by retry cooldown until <timestamp>". Alternatively, `ddx bead ready` should default to execution-ready or visibly mark non-execution-eligible epics so it does not imply `ddx work` can process them.
acceptance:
1. A regression test covers a tracker state with at least one open ready epic and no execution-eligible task beads; `ddx work --once` does not silently exit with an empty log/status.
2. The operator-facing output or JSON result explains why ready epics were skipped and includes the count/IDs or issue type reason.
3. `ddx bead ready` and `ddx bead ready --execution` semantics are documented in CLI help or status output so users can predict what `ddx work` will process.
4. Existing queue/work tests pass.

rationale: repeated reviewer blocks cite decomposition scope
title: telemetry: emit override and rejected_override events from Execute
description:
Bead 1 of ADR-006. Emit override and rejected_override events from Execute so the routing-quality signal becomes measurable.

When ExecuteRequest has any of Harness, Provider, or Model set, the service:
1. Runs ResolveRoute as today, producing the constrained decision (the dispatched candidate).
2. Runs a second ResolveRoute with the override axes stripped, producing the unconstrained auto decision.
3. Emits an 'override' event before the final event with the payload shape from ADR-006 §3 (user_pin, auto_decision, axes_overridden, match_per_axis, auto_score, auto_components, prompt_features, reason_hint, outcome).
4. Outcome is populated post-execution from the existing final-event status fields.

When a pin fails pre-dispatch (orphan model, unknown provider, harness/provider incompatibility), emit a 'rejected_override' event with the same shape minus outcome.

prompt_features.estimated_tokens uses ServiceExecuteRequest.EstimatedPromptTokens if provided, else best-effort harness-level tokenizer (nullable).

reason_hint accepts a free-form string from Metadata["override.reason"] (programmatic) or a future --override-reason CLI flag.

In-scope files:
- service.go (event payload struct definitions)
- service_events.go (event type constants + decoded event mapping)
- service_execute.go (override capture + event emission, second ResolveRoute call)
- service_execute_test.go (event-shape and emission-order tests)

Out of scope:
- RouteStatus / UsageReport metric exposure (bead 2)
- CLI surface (bead 3)
- Any change to existing routing-decision events
- Automatic learning loop
acceptance:
1. ExecuteRequest with no override axes set produces no override event (verified in TestExecuteEmitsNoOverrideEventForUnpinnedRequest).
2. ExecuteRequest with Harness, Provider, or Model set produces exactly one override event before the final event. Verified in TestExecuteEmitsOverrideEventBeforeFinal across all three axes individually and in combination.
3. The override event's auto_decision field is computed from a second ResolveRoute call with the override axes stripped. When pinning matches what unconstrained auto would produce, match_per_axis values are true; the event still fires (coincidental-agreement case). Verified in TestOverrideEventCoincidentalAgreement.
4. axes_overridden lists exactly the axes the caller pinned (subset of {harness, provider, model}). Verified in TestOverrideEventAxesOverriddenIsExplicit.
5. Outcome is populated post-execution with status, cost_usd, duration_ms matching the final event's values. Verified in TestOverrideEventOutcomePopulatedFromFinal.
6. Pre-dispatch failure of a pin (orphan model, unknown provider) produces a rejected_override event and no override event. Verified in TestRejectedOverrideEventOnOrphanModel.
7. prompt_features.estimated_tokens reflects ServiceExecuteRequest.EstimatedPromptTokens when set; nullable otherwise. Verified in TestOverrideEventPromptFeaturesPopulation.
8. reason_hint reflects Metadata["override.reason"] if set; empty string otherwise.
9. go test ./... -count=1 passes.

bead.acceptance:
1. ExecuteRequest with no override axes set produces no override event (verified in TestExecuteEmitsNoOverrideEventForUnpinnedRequest).
2. ExecuteRequest with Harness, Provider, or Model set produces exactly one override event before the final event. Verified in TestExecuteEmitsOverrideEventBeforeFinal across all three axes individually and in combination.
3. The override event's auto_decision field is computed from a second ResolveRoute call with the override axes stripped. When pinning matches what unconstrained auto would produce, match_per_axis values are true; the event still fires (coincidental-agreement case). Verified in TestOverrideEventCoincidentalAgreement.
4. axes_overridden lists exactly the axes the caller pinned (subset of {harness, provider, model}). Verified in TestOverrideEventAxesOverriddenIsExplicit.
5. Outcome is populated post-execution with status, cost_usd, duration_ms matching the final event's values. Verified in TestOverrideEventOutcomePopulatedFromFinal.
6. Pre-dispatch failure of a pin (orphan model, unknown provider) produces a rejected_override event and no override event. Verified in TestRejectedOverrideEventOnOrphanModel.
7. prompt_features.estimated_tokens reflects ServiceExecuteRequest.EstimatedPromptTokens when set; nullable otherwise. Verified in TestOverrideEventPromptFeaturesPopulation.
8. reason_hint reflects Metadata["override.reason"] if set; empty string otherwise.
9. go test ./... -count=1 passes.
bead.notes:
REVIEW:BLOCK

`service_execute.go:isExplicitPinError` does not classify unknown provider pin failures as rejected overrides, so AC #6’s “unknown provider” case will not emit `rejected_override`.
`service_override_test.go:220` does not verify coincidental agreement. The test comment says the stripped auto route would not match and only asserts that `match_per_axis` has entries, so AC #3 is not actually covered.
`service_override_test.go:134` does not test all three axes individually. It covers harness-only, harness+model, and all three, but omits provider-only and model-only cases required by AC #2.
`service_override_test.go:102` currently fails in this workspace: `TestExecuteEmitsNoOverrideEventForUnpinnedRequest` timed out after receiving one event, so AC #9 cannot pass.

Reopened after gpt-5.5 review on commit f6e7487 returned BLOCK with four substantive defects (NOT environmental). All four must be addressed in the next attempt. Fixes are small but require care:

1. **TestExecuteEmitsNoOverrideEventForUnpinnedRequest times out** (review-cited: service_override_test.go:102). The test uses Profile-only and times out after one event. Fix: either route the test to a path that produces a complete final event (e.g. set the unconfigured-route fast-fail path explicitly), or use a virtual-harness fixture that completes deterministically. The test must finish; AC #1 cannot be satisfied by a hanging test.

2. **isExplicitPinError does NOT classify unknown provider as a rejected_override** (review-cited: service_execute.go:isExplicitPinError). AC #6 requires rejected_override on unknown provider; current implementation only fires on orphan-model. Extend isExplicitPinError (or its callers) to identify the unknown-provider failure path and emit rejected_override. Test must cover both orphan-model AND unknown-provider cases.

3. **TestOverrideEventCoincidentalAgreement is fake** (review-cited: service_override_test.go:220). The test's own comment admits it does NOT synthesize a coincidental-agreement scenario — it asserts the event fires and that MatchPerAxis has entries, NOT that the entries are true. AC #3 is explicit: 'when pinning matches what unconstrained auto would produce, match_per_axis values are true; the event still fires.' Replace with a real synthesis: build a fakeServiceConfig with a single provider that auto-routing would pick anyway (e.g. only configured local provider with no other candidates), pin Provider=that-provider, assert match_per_axis[provider]=true and the event still fires. Routing-test-rigor memory applies — no synthetic stand-ins.

4. **TestExecuteEmitsOverrideEventBeforeFinal omits provider-only and model-only cases** (review-cited: service_override_test.go:134). AC #2 requires 'each axis individually and in combination.' The test covers harness-only, harness+model, and all three but skips provider-only and model-only. Add those subtests. Provider-only may need a non-virtual fixture since virtual gates on Harness; consider using a fakeServiceConfig + fake provider for unit-level coverage if integration is awkward.

Acceptance unchanged from the original bead; this is just a deficiency list against the existing AC.

rationale: three or more attempts without a closing commit
title: Detect execute-bead worktree edits even when the agent makes no commits
description: A Codex execute-loop run on ddx-28c15f0e updated FEAT-002, SD-019, and website server docs inside the managed worktree, but DDx classified the attempt as no_changes because it only looked for agent-created commits. Execute-bead must treat a dirty worktree with tracked edits as real output, preserve or land it appropriately, and record accurate result status instead of discarding useful work.
acceptance: execute-bead and execute-loop no longer report no_changes when the worktree contains tracked file modifications without agent-created commits; the attempt preserves or lands the changes and result artifacts reflect the actual diff state

bead.title: Detect execute-bead worktree edits even when the agent makes no commits
bead.description: A Codex execute-loop run on ddx-28c15f0e updated FEAT-002, SD-019, and website server docs inside the managed worktree, but DDx classified the attempt as no_changes because it only looked for agent-created commits. Execute-bead must treat a dirty worktree with tracked edits as real output, preserve or land it appropriately, and record accurate result status instead of discarding useful work.
bead.acceptance: execute-bead and execute-loop no longer report no_changes when the worktree contains tracked file modifications without agent-created commits; the attempt preserves or lands the changes and result artifacts reflect the actual diff state
bead.notes: IsDirty uses git diff-index which misses untracked files. SynthesizeCommit uses git add -u which also misses them. Fix: IsDirty should check git status --porcelain; SynthesizeCommit should use git add -A.
