title: test: migrate TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorMessageInOutput to real git + script harness
description:
Migrate cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_test.go TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorMessageInOutput() from fakeExecuteBeadGit + fakeAgentRunner to real git + script harness.

Pattern:
- Use cli/internal/agent/integration_helper_test.go newScriptHarnessRepo(t, beadCount) to build an isolated repo with real git.
- Use runGitInteg (from cli/internal/git/git_basic_test.go) for any supplemental git invocations, so GIT_* env is scrubbed.
- Replace fakeExecuteBeadGit with the RealLandingGitOps flow used in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go.
- Replace fakeAgentRunner with the script harness (cli/internal/agent/script.go) driven by a per-attempt directive file. When the test needs to simulate 'agent emits no commits' or 'agent emits a dirty worktree', encode that as script directives rather than a canned Result struct.
- Preserve every assertion — the point is that the test now exercises real git + real merge/preserve semantics, not that the surface shrinks.

Scope limit: ONLY this test. Do not migrate any other test in the same file; touching another test expands blast radius outside the bead.

Verification: cd cli && go test ./cmd -run '^TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorMessageInOutput$' -count=1 passes. No struct ending in -GitOps or -Runner that fakes the thing under test is referenced from this test after the migration.

Governing concern: concerns.md §testing 'no mocks, period' + 'never mock the thing you are testing'. Parent: ddx-d9df348d.
acceptance: 1) TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorMessageInOutput no longer references fakeExecuteBeadGit or fakeAgentRunner. 2) Test dispatches through RealLandingGitOps + newScriptHarnessRepo (or equivalent real-git harness). 3) cd cli && go test ./cmd -run '^TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorMessageInOutput$' -count=1 green. 4) No other test in agent_execute_bead_test.go is modified.

Bead: agent-ed9bc423 — Step 8b — Adapter: opencode Outcome: no changes (blocked on prerequisite) What is done ------------ - Read the bead contract and confirmed via NEW14 spike (docs/research/oss-harness-install-2026-04-29.md) that opencode is KEPT (not dropped). The spike records pinned version 1.3.17, install command, non-interactive flags, profile mapping, and base-URL routing for opencode — sufficient to write opencode.py once base.py exists. - Verified worktree state: * `scripts/benchmark/harness_adapters/` does NOT exist. * No `base.py` / `BaseInstalledAgent` superclass anywhere in the tree. * No shared `_test/fake_provider.py` helper anywhere in the tree. * `.ddx/beads.jsonl` shows agent-86dc6092 ("Step 3 — Adapter base + ddx-agent + noop + dumb_script + FakeProvider", a.k.a. NEW9) is still `open`. - Cross-referenced sibling bead agent-cb608a4f ("Step 8a — Adapter: pi"), which was closed with the same blocker on the immediately prior attempt (commit 95f5a6a tracker-only, no code). This bead has identical prerequisites; the same blocker applies. What is blocking ---------------- The bead description is explicit that opencode.py implements the contract "declared in NEW9 base.py" and that "Calibration matrix from NEW9 still passes alongside". Both prerequisites are unmet: 1. There is no base.py to subclass. SD-010 §4.1 specifies the protocol (`class Agent(BaseInstalledAgent)` with install / command / apply_profile / parse_telemetry / redact_secrets), but the concrete `BaseInstalledAgent` shim — which Harbor's adapter loader binds to — is owned by NEW9 (agent-86dc6092). Implementing it here would either (a) duplicate a contract NEW9 will land, forcing a reconciling rewrite, or (b) silently fold NEW9's scope into this bead, violating the single-bead boundary. 2. There is no shared `FakeProvider`. SD-010 §4.2 states the helper "is shared across adapter tests, not reimplemented per adapter", and NEW9's AC includes shipping it. Writing an opencode-local fake here would guarantee a duplicate / divergent helper when NEW9 lands. 3. The bead AC line "Calibration matrix from NEW9 still passes alongside" cannot be evaluated: the calibration matrix (noop + dumb_script adapters under harness_adapters/) does not yet exist. What a follow-up attempt would need ----------------------------------- - NEW9 (agent-86dc6092) closed first, landing: * scripts/benchmark/harness_adapters/base.py with the BaseInstalledAgent superclass + telemetry/redaction helpers * scripts/benchmark/harness_adapters/_test/fake_provider.py * noop + dumb_script + ddx_agent reference adapters and their tests * a way to run the calibration matrix locally so this bead can prove "still passes alongside" - Once base.py exists, this bead is a straightforward translation of docs/research/oss-harness-install-2026-04-29.md §opencode into opencode.py: * install: `curl -fsSL https://opencode.ai/install | VERSION=1.3.17 bash` (apt: curl, unzip), PATH `/root/.opencode/bin` * command: `opencode run --print-logs --model

/ ""` with stdin=/dev/null (per spike's non-interactive flags) * apply_profile: route openai-compat / OpenRouter via OPENCODE_API_BASE_URL + provider-keyed env, mapping the v7 profile pricing fields * parse_telemetry: emit v7 D4 schema from opencode's --print-logs JSON-lines stdout/stderr split per the spike * redact_secrets: scrub provider api keys from log payloads - pytest test under harness_adapters/_test/test_opencode.py uses the shared FakeProvider to assert command + env without invoking opencode or paid APIs. Recommendation -------------- Re-queue this bead after NEW9 (agent-86dc6092) closes. rationale: no_changes/review text says bead needs splitting
title: Align specs for prompt templates, tracked execution artifacts, and commit provenance
description:
<context-digest>
Review area: governing contract for execute-bead prompts and execution evidence. Evidence covers the current FEAT-006/API-001 workflow, the prompt fallback bug, the desire to keep beads authored and relatively stable, and the new requirement that execution evidence be tracked in git so autoresearch can replay, compare, and analyze runs.
</context-digest>
Align the governing docs around one execute-bead evidence contract.

## Goals
- Define the execute-bead prompt template as a DDx-owned rationalizer over bead fields plus resolved governing references, not a bead mutation and not a speculative summary of whole specs
- Define the stable tracked artifact set for each execution attempt: prompt, manifest, result, checks, normalized log, and usage/provider pointers
- Define the split between tracked execution evidence and ignored local scratch/runtime state
- Define the default commit policy for successful and preserved attempts
- Define the rule that all programmatically-added commit message metadata must be rendered from tracked machine-readable files
acceptance: FEAT-006, FEAT-012, FEAT-014, FEAT-015, and API-001 describe the same contract: execute-bead compiles a deterministic prompt from bead data plus resolved references; each attempt produces tracked machine-readable execution artifacts; ignored runtime scratch is clearly separated from tracked evidence; and commit-message metadata is projected from tracked files rather than ad hoc runtime state

rationale: repeated reviewer blocks cite decomposition scope
title: Bead detail GraphQL resolver scans every project and every bead on each request
description:
## Observed

Opening a bead in the sidepane on `/nodes/.../projects/.../beads?q=test` takes 5+ seconds. The beads list renders quickly; the delay is on single-bead lookup.

## Root cause

`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver.go:93-100` — the `Bead(id)` resolver calls `r.State.GetBeadSnapshots("", "", "", "")` with no filters, which at `cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:49-105` walks **every project**, constructs a `bead.Store` per project, calls `ReadAll()` (full directory scan + JSONL parse of every bead file), then iterates the result in Go to find one matching ID.

So every sidepane open = `O(projects × beads_per_project)` disk reads + parses, for a lookup that should be `O(1)` or `O(log n)`.

`bead.Store` already exposes `Get(id)` (`cli/internal/bead/store.go:450`), but the resolver doesn't use it — nor does it know which project the bead lives in, because the GraphQL schema exposes `bead(id:)` without a project scope.

## Proposed direction (not prescriptive)

Any of these would resolve it; pick based on measurements:

1. **Project scope the query.** Change schema to `bead(projectId:, id:)` (or accept a composite global id) and call `Store.Get` directly.
2. **Maintain an in-memory index** `beadID → projectID` refreshed on bead mutations and on a debounced filesystem watcher; resolver does one map lookup + one `Store.Get`.
3. **Short-TTL snapshot cache** behind `GetBeadSnapshots`, invalidated on bead writes.

Before picking: add a baseline measurement harness (see acceptance) so we know the actual p50/p95 before and after, not just anecdote.
acceptance:
**User story:** As a developer browsing the web UI beads view, I want clicking a bead to open its detail pane within ~200ms on a repo with hundreds of beads across several projects, so the UI feels interactive.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. A benchmark/harness exists (Go benchmark or scripted GraphQL client) that measures end-to-end `bead(id:)` latency against a fixture repo with ≥ 5 projects and ≥ 500 beads total. Output is a p50/p95 number, not a pass/fail bool.
2. Baseline is recorded in the bead notes (actual number measured on the current `main` binary at the time of fix).
3. After the fix, p95 for `bead(id:)` on the fixture is ≤ 50ms in-process / ≤ 200ms over HTTP on a dev laptop.
4. The fix does not introduce a stale-read regression: writing a bead via `ddx bead update` and then fetching it via GraphQL returns the new value on the next request (verified by a test that does write-then-read).
5. Concurrency-safe: an integration test spawns N concurrent `bead(id:)` queries interleaved with `bead update` mutations and asserts no panics, no torn reads, and consistent responses.
6. Playwright e2e: open `/nodes/.../beads`, click a bead row, assert the sidepane shows the bead title within 1s (generous budget; guards against regressions not the p95).

6. Playwright e2e: open `/nodes/.../beads`, click a bead row, assert the sidepane shows the bead title within 1s (generous budget; guards against regressions not the p95).

   → TestServerStateBeadSnapshotLatencyBudget asserts in-process

   → TestGraphQLBeadLookupReadsExternalCLIUpdateOnNextRequest
     (graphql_bead_lookup_perf_test.go). Test shells out to

   → TestGraphQLBeadLookupConcurrentQueriesAndUpdates spins up 8 workers ×

     queries on the same fixture bead, asserting no panics, no torn reads,
     and consistent title/updatedAt fields. Passes this pass.

       await expect(page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'First bead' }))
         .toBeVisible({ timeout: 1000 });

- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver.go:93-99
    queryResolver.Bead now calls r.State.GetBeadSnapshot(id) instead of

- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_node.go
    StateProvider interface gained GetBeadSnapshot(id).
- cli/internal/server/state.go
    ServerState gained beadIndexMu + beadIndex (beadID → {projectID, path}).
- cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go
    - GetBeadSnapshot uses the index + bead.Store.Get for O(1)/O(log n) lookup,
      falling back to a single per-project probe + index repair on index miss
      or stale-index mismatch.
    - rememberBeadLocation / forgetBeadLocation / lookupBeadLocation manage
      the index, warmed both by list traffic (GetBeadSnapshots) and by
      successful singleton lookups.

    testStateProvider gained GetBeadSnapshot to satisfy the interface.

the review findings are outdated relative to c1822352. If the orchestrator
still re-queues this bead, the next attempt should re-run the above tests
against the current base-rev and compare line-by-line with the AC — the
artifacts will still be there.

rationale: no_changes/review text says bead needs splitting
title: test: migrate TestExecuteBeadResolvesPathStyleSpecID to real git + script harness
description:
Migrate cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_test.go TestExecuteBeadResolvesPathStyleSpecID() from fakeExecuteBeadGit + fakeAgentRunner to real git + script harness.

Pattern:
- Use cli/internal/agent/integration_helper_test.go newScriptHarnessRepo(t, beadCount) to build an isolated repo with real git.
- Use runGitInteg (from cli/internal/git/git_basic_test.go) for any supplemental git invocations, so GIT_* env is scrubbed.
- Replace fakeExecuteBeadGit with the RealLandingGitOps flow used in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go.
- Replace fakeAgentRunner with the script harness (cli/internal/agent/script.go) driven by a per-attempt directive file. When the test needs to simulate 'agent emits no commits' or 'agent emits a dirty worktree', encode that as script directives rather than a canned Result struct.
- Preserve every assertion — the point is that the test now exercises real git + real merge/preserve semantics, not that the surface shrinks.

Scope limit: ONLY this test. Do not migrate any other test in the same file; touching another test expands blast radius outside the bead.

Verification: cd cli && go test ./cmd -run '^TestExecuteBeadResolvesPathStyleSpecID$' -count=1 passes. No struct ending in -GitOps or -Runner that fakes the thing under test is referenced from this test after the migration.

Governing concern: concerns.md §testing 'no mocks, period' + 'never mock the thing you are testing'. Parent: ddx-d9df348d.
acceptance: 1) TestExecuteBeadResolvesPathStyleSpecID no longer references fakeExecuteBeadGit or fakeAgentRunner. 2) Test dispatches through RealLandingGitOps + newScriptHarnessRepo (or equivalent real-git harness). 3) cd cli && go test ./cmd -run '^TestExecuteBeadResolvesPathStyleSpecID$' -count=1 green. 4) No other test in agent_execute_bead_test.go is modified.

rationale: no_changes/review text says bead needs splitting
title: Remove provider-native parsers and GlobalProviderHealth shadow (atomic)
description:
Land agent-b896b406 (provider-native parser removal) + agent-bcd2ff47 (GlobalProviderHealth removal) together. The atomic-pair framing is from the parent bead ddx-7bc0c8d5: splitting creates a window where operator surfaces (doctor, /api/providers, MCP tools, agent usage) read from different sources than execute-loop, so both land in one PR.

## Files to delete / rewrite

- cli/internal/agent/session_evidence.go (357 LOC): Codex native session JSONL, Claude stats/cache, OpenRouter /auth/key, LM Studio /v1/models.
- cli/internal/agent/routing_signals.go (~450 LOC Runner-method variant; loadCodex/Claude/LMStudio helpers).
- cli/internal/agent/routing_signal_adapters.go, routing_signal_http.go.
- cli/internal/agent/claude_quota_cache.go — entire ReadClaudeQuotaSnapshot / WriteClaudeQuotaSnapshot / resolveClaudeQuotaSnapshotPath subsystem.
- cli/internal/agent/state.go — ReadClaudeQuotaRoutingDecision and ClaudeQuotaDecision type.
- cli/internal/escalation/GlobalProviderHealth singleton + ProviderHealthTracker.

## Call sites to migrate

- workers.go:511 (IsHealthy) / :593 (Mark)
- agent_cmd.go:1702 (IsHealthy) / :1791 (Mark)
- agent_cmd.go:680 (routing_signal.CurrentQuota.State == "blocked")
- agent_cmd.go:704 (ReadClaudeQuotaRoutingDecision)

## Migration target

- svc.ListHarnesses — HarnessInfo.Quota / UsageWindows / Account
- svc.ListProviders — ProviderInfo.EndpointStatus
- svc.RouteStatus — RouteStatusReport with RouteCandidateStatus.Healthy
- svc.RecordRouteAttempt — RouteAttempt with status/reason

Vocabulary translation shim in buildProviderSummary / buildProviderDetail maps current "fresh/stale" to upstream's "ok/stale/unavailable" (consumer-side mapping only; not an upstream API change).

## Observable behavior changes (consumer surfaces)

- ddx agent doctor --routing --json: state.claude_quota_decision field disappears.
- ddx agent doctor --routing human text: claude-quota-cache line disappears.
- ProbeHarnessState via state.go.
- ddx agent usage enrichment.
- REST /api/providers.
- MCP ddx_provider_list / ddx_provider_show.

Not affected: SvelteKit frontend providers page uses GraphQL providerStatuses only (no cache fields).
acceptance:
(1) session_evidence.go, routing_signals.go, routing_signal_adapters.go, routing_signal_http.go, claude_quota_cache.go deleted.
(2) state.go: ReadClaudeQuotaRoutingDecision and ClaudeQuotaDecision removed.
(3) cli/internal/escalation: GlobalProviderHealth singleton and ProviderHealthTracker removed.
(4) workers.go, agent_cmd.go call sites migrated to svc.ListHarnesses / svc.ListProviders / svc.RouteStatus / svc.RecordRouteAttempt.
(5) buildProviderSummary / buildProviderDetail perform fresh/stale → ok/stale/unavailable translation.
(6) go -C cli build ./... and go -C cli test ./... green.
(7) ddx agent doctor --routing and /api/providers exercised manually; behavior matches upstream RouteStatusReport semantics.

title: Adopt XML-tagged execute-bead prompt template
description:
<context-digest>
Review area: execute-bead prompt-template structure. Evidence covers the shipped synthesized prompt in cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go, the new tracked execution bundle under .ddx/executions/, and the need for a more deterministic machine-readable prompt contract for replay, diffing, and future autoresearch.
</context-digest>
Evolve the synthesized execute-bead prompt from markdown-heading sections to an explicit XML-tagged structure.

## Goals
- Replace markdown section headings in the synthesized execute-bead prompt with XML-style tags for machine-significant structure
- Preserve the current human-readable content while making the prompt easier to parse, diff, and validate deterministically
- Define which sections are required, optional, and repeatable in the prompt template
- Keep the prompt aligned with the tracked execution manifest and future commit-provenance rendering

title: cmd/work: migrate ddx work CLI dispatch to LoadAndResolve + RunWithConfig + behavioral e2e
description:
Stage 1 of SD-024. Migrates the CLI dispatch site at cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1997 to use config.LoadAndResolve + ExecuteBeadWorker.RunWithConfig instead of constructing ExecuteBeadLoopOptions from CLI flags. Adds a behavioral e2e test that proves a .ddx/config.yaml change reaches the running CLI loop.

This bead unblocks FEAT-022 G's CLI path: ReviewMaxRetries from .ddx/config.yaml will actually drive review-retry behavior on 'ddx work' invocations.

In-scope:
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go (around line 1997 — runAgentExecuteLoop)
- cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop_e2e_test.go (new) — TestReviewRetryThresholdFromConfigCLI

Out-of-scope:
- Server worker dispatch (bead 8)
- GraphQL StartWorker dispatch (bead 9)

Depends on bead 6.5 (review-failure fixture). See TD-024 §Test plan.
acceptance: cd cli && go test -run TestReviewRetryThresholdFromConfigCLI ./cmd/... passes: with .ddx/config.yaml setting review_max_retries=5, a deterministic test runner that fails the first 4 reviews and succeeds the 5th, ddx work --once on a seeded bead closes successfully on attempt 5 and emits no review-manual-required event. cd cli && go test ./... passes.

bead.notes:
cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:2010 — `rcfg, _ := config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, overrides)` silently swallows the error. If LoadAndResolve fails (malformed YAML, etc.), a zero-value ResolvedConfig flows into RunWithConfig and SD-024's sealed-construction sentinel will panic at first accessor read instead of surfacing a clean CLI error. Capture and return/log the error.
AC #2 verification: produce a `go test ./...` run (or scope exclusion list) demonstrating the cmd-package failures are byte-identical pre/post this bead, or fix them in-scope. Pointing at LEFTHOOK_EXCLUDE in the commit body is not a substitute for the AC's literal requirement.
Test pragmatics: `store.Unclaim(beadID)` between iterations is a hand-driven re-pickup that the production poll loop would do naturally. Acceptable for a unit-style e2e but worth a one-line comment that this stands in for the poll-loop reclaim, not a behavior under test.

AC #2 strict reading: pre-existing failures in `cmd/` (TestBeadReviewCollapseGolden golden drift; TestAcceptance_US028/029/030/031/034; TestInstallationPerformance) cause `go test ./...` to fail. The evidence file argues these are unrelated and identical before/after — accept that argument or scope the AC, but a downstream bead should either (a) restore a green tree or (b) explicitly amend the AC to exclude the documented environmental tests so future beads aren't forced to keep `LEFTHOOK_EXCLUDE=go-test` as standard practice.

title: [artifact-run-arch] update prd.md for artifact + 3-layer architecture
description:
Per plan: docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-29-artifact-and-run-architecture.md.

Scope of PRD update:

(1) Restructure Problem section into 6 clusters keyed to thesis principles in product-vision.md:
- Abstraction (no structure / no composition / no integrity guarantees / no transferability)
- Iteration over tracked work (no reusable work-item store / no reusable execution evidence)
- Methodology plurality (no reusable agent dispatch — every workflow tool reinvents)
- LLM physics (NEW cluster — token cost as a permanent constraint; nothing in current PRD)
- Evidence/provenance (no provenance for generated artifacts; no measurement; no feedback capture)
- Human-AI collaboration (no composition for handoffs; no discoverability; no network access)

Existing problem bullets re-grouped under these headers; add the LLM-physics cluster.

(2) Goals (primary): reword #1; add three-layer-architecture goal; add generated-artifact-regeneration goal; add 100% read coverage on HTTP/MCP goal.

(3) Non-goals: sharpen loop wording — DDx owns mechanical queue drain (ddx work); content-aware supervisory decisions remain plugin/HELIX. Add: 'DDx does not catalog run types beyond the three layers.'

(4) Feature blurbs: FEAT-001 (CLI: run/try/work top-level + ddx agent passthrough); FEAT-005 (multi-media identity); FEAT-006 (layer-1 consumer + structural passthrough); FEAT-007 (sidecar + generated_by + separate staleness); FEAT-008/021 (media-type rendering + regenerate + layer-aware run views); FEAT-010 (3-layer architecture + substrate unification); FEAT-019 (child of FEAT-010 — evaluation UX, workflow shapes -> skills).

Summary: artifacts (multi-media); three-layer run architecture; one substrate; invocation upstream.

Do NOT touch docs/helix/01-frame/principles.md.

- Define the separate epic lane in queue views
- Define epic worker records, branch/worktree visibility, and child-progress visibility
- Define how users inspect epic merge-gate results and the final merge candidate
- Keep single-ticket workers and epic workers distinct in the server model

title: agent: migrate internal helpers off RunOptions/CompareOptions/QuorumOptions
description:
Stage 2 prereq for B22 (SCOPE EXPANDED 2026-04-26 per worker analysis on attempt 1).

Migrate ALL ddx-side callers off the three legacy options structs to AgentRunRuntime + ResolvedConfig. After this bead lands, the legacy types in cli/internal/agent/types.go are unreachable from any caller — B22 can then trivially delete them.

In-scope (expanded from original 10-file list):
- cli/internal/agent/ helpers: virtual.go, script.go, claude_stream.go, agent_runner.go, agent_runner_service.go, models.go, grade.go, service_run.go, session_index.go, routing_metrics.go, compare_adapter.go, runner.go
- cli/cmd/ callers: agent_cmd.go (RunViaService → RunWithConfigViaService)
- cli/internal/agent/ dispatch sites: execute_bead.go (dispatchAgentRun), execute_bead_review.go (dispatchReviewRun)
- All *_test.go files that construct RunOptions{}/CompareOptions{}/QuorumOptions{} composite literals

Original 10-file scope was internally inconsistent (worker found that migrating a subset would either leave intermediate states the runtimelint gate fires on, or require touching out-of-scope callers anyway). Expanded scope authorizes the worker to perform the full migration in a single atomic change.

Out-of-scope (still):
- The three type DEFINITIONS at types.go:36/81/171 (B22 final deletes these)
- Any change unrelated to this options-struct migration

no_changes
rationale: ddx-af187382 — agent: migrate internal helpers off RunOptions/CompareOptions/QuorumOptions

NO_CHANGES decision after analysis.

## What is done

- Read all 10 in-scope helper files (virtual.go, script.go, claude_stream.go,
  agent_runner.go, models.go, grade.go, service_run.go, session_index.go,
  routing_metrics.go, compare_adapter.go) and inventoried every reference
  to the three legacy options types.
- Read the SD-024 Stage 4 runtime-lint analyzer
  (cli/tools/lint/runtimelint/analyzer.go) and its lefthook hook
  (lefthook.yml runtime-lint block, lines 157–190).
- Read the cross-package callers that the helpers serve: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go,
  cli/internal/agent/runner.go, execute_bead.go, execute_bead_review.go,
  agent_runner_service.go, run_internal.go, plus all *_test.go in the
  agent package, plus cli/internal/server/server.go.
- Prototyped a "minimal-blast" migration that moves the RunOptions /
  CompareOptions / QuorumOptions-typed dispatch entry points out of
  grade.go, service_run.go, and compare_adapter.go into sibling files
  service_run_legacy.go and compare_adapter_legacy.go (with grade.go
  switching to the runInternal/RunArgs seam introduced by ddx-dd640c18).
  The prototype builds (`go build ./...`) and passes
  `go test ./internal/agent/...` except for the pre-existing
  TestIntegration_CodexEcho codex-CLI environment failure that already
  fails on base rev 12a2b57f (verified via
  `git stash --include-untracked` round-trip; matches the same
  pre-existing failure documented in commit 1718bdb5's message).

## What is blocking

The bead's AC clauses
  - "field RunOptions removed from cli/internal/agent/service_run.go"
  - "field RunOptions removed from cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go"
collide with two enforced project gates that this bead's stated
in-scope/out-of-scope split does not authorize me to break:

1. SD-024 Stage 4 runtime-lint (cli/tools/lint/runtimelint, wired into
   lefthook pre-commit at lefthook.yml:157). Pattern 2 of the analyzer
   forbids any composite literal of RunOptions / CompareOptions /
   QuorumOptions *anywhere* in staged Go code, and pattern 3 forbids
   any function parameter typed as one of those legacy types in the
   cli/internal/agent/ package. The lefthook block filters diagnostics
   to staged file paths, so untouched files keep working but every
   freshly staged file is checked in full. My prototype's new
   compare_adapter_legacy.go and service_run_legacy.go contain
   verbatim copies of the legacy entry points (RunViaService,
   RunViaServiceWith, RunCompareViaService, RunCompareWithAgent,
   RunQuorumViaService, RunQuorumWithAgent, RunBenchmarkViaService,
   RunBenchmarkWithAgent, RunCompareWith, RunQuorumWith,
   runCompareArmWith, defaultResolvePromptForCompare,
   RunWithConfigViaService, runFixtureHarnessViaRunner, RunFunc, plus
   the *Config wrappers that build CompareOptions/QuorumOptions
   internally), and the runtime-lint gate fires on every one of them
   (pattern-2 composite literals plus pattern-3 function params,
   17 violations total). Touching session_index_test.go to redirect
   TestProductionAgentExecutionPathsUseIndexedServiceWriter at the new
   sibling files also re-stages the file and trips pattern-2 on the
   pre-existing RunOptions{} literals at session_index_test.go:195 and
   :278.

2. The bead's own out-of-scope list. Eliminating the gate-violating
   sites without moving them requires migrating the *callers* of the
   public entry points off the legacy types — and those callers are
   the explicitly out-of-scope packages:
     - cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go (1 call to RunViaService at :1462) — the
       bead lists "cli/cmd/ migration" as out-of-scope.
     - cli/internal/server/server.go — listed as B22c (out-of-scope).
     - cli/internal/exec/store.go — listed as B22b (out-of-scope).
     - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go (dispatchAgentRun at :927,
       calls RunViaServiceWith at :938) and execute_bead_review.go
       (dispatchReviewRun at :692, RunViaServiceWith at :704) — neither
       file is in the bead's 10-file in-scope list, so per the
       constraint "Do not modify files outside the scope the bead
       description names" they are off-limits.
     - cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go
       (runAgentViaService at :72) and agent_runner.go (RunAgent at
       :13) — both in scope per the description but their signatures
       are called from out-of-scope runner.go (Runner.Run dispatches
       to RunAgent at runner.go:110, runVirtualFn at :106, runScriptFn
       at :114, runClaudeWithFallbackFn at :207, finalizeClaudeResult
       at :209, recordRoutingOutcome at :231) and changing those
       signatures cascades back into out-of-scope runner.go.

The two constraints are jointly unsatisfiable in a single attempt:
removing RunOptions from compare_adapter.go and service_run.go either
requires keeping the legacy entry points somewhere (and runtime-lint
fires on whatever staged file holds them) or migrating all callers
(and the bead forbids touching the out-of-scope files where most of
them live).

## What a follow-up attempt would need

A clean landing requires either:

a) **Bead resplit.** Split this bead into per-caller migration beads
   that each cover one call site plus its package, so the runtime-lint
   gate stays satisfied at every step. Concretely: one bead each for
   (i) cmd/agent_cmd.go RunViaService → RunWithConfigViaService,
   (ii) execute_bead.go dispatchAgentRun + RunViaServiceWith,
   (iii) execute_bead_review.go dispatchReviewRun + RunViaServiceWith,
   (iv) compare/quorum/benchmark callers (find sites first), and only
   then a final bead that retires the now-unreachable RunOptions /
   CompareOptions / QuorumOptions-typed entry points from
   service_run.go and compare_adapter.go. Each step is independently
   buildable, lint-clean, and AC-checkable.

b) **In-scope list expansion.** Authorise this bead to touch the
   currently out-of-scope cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go, execute_bead.go,
   execute_bead_review.go, agent_runner_service.go, runner.go callers
   (and the *_test.go files that still construct RunOptions{}), so the
   migration can collapse the legacy entry points in one pass without
   the runtime-lint gate firing on intermediate states.

The grade.go change in isolation — switching the lone
`r.Run(RunOptions{Harness, Prompt})` call to
`r.runInternal(RunArgs{Harness, Prompt})` via the dispatch seam from
ddx-dd640c18 — is clean (build green, runtime-lint green, tests green
modulo the pre-existing codex-CLI environment failure), but landing
just that change would satisfy only 3 of the 7 AC items (the
RunOptions/CompareOptions/QuorumOptions removals from grade.go) and
leaves the AC items for service_run.go and compare_adapter.go
unsatisfied, which per the bead's "do not commit partial code" rule is
worse than a no_changes hand-off.

rationale: ddx-af187382 — agent: migrate internal helpers off RunOptions/CompareOptions/QuorumOptions

Second-attempt no_changes after re-analysis with the EXPANDED scope (option b
authorized 2026-04-26).

## What is done

- Confirmed prior worker's analysis (attempt 1, base 12a2b57f). The runtime-lint
  pre-commit gate (lefthook.yml:164–186) filters violations to *staged* files.
  Any staged Go file that mentions a RunOptions / CompareOptions / QuorumOptions
  composite literal or function-parameter signature fails the gate.
- Inventoried all ref sites under the expanded scope. 38 .go files touch the
  three legacy types (excluding tools/lint testdata). Of those:
    Production:
      cli/internal/agent/grade.go (1 site — trivial, AC item 1)
      cli/internal/agent/service_run.go (RunViaService, RunViaServiceWith,
        runFixtureHarnessViaRunner — public API surface)
      cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go (RunCompareViaService,
        RunCompareWithAgent, RunCompareWith, RunQuorumViaService,
        RunQuorumWithAgent, RunQuorumWith, RunBenchmarkViaService,
        RunBenchmarkWithAgent, RunBenchmarkWith, runCompareArmWith,
        defaultResolvePromptForCompare, RunFunc, BenchmarkArmsToCompare)
      cli/internal/agent/runner.go (Run, RunWithConfig, BuildArgs, resolveHarness,
        resolveModel, resolveTimeout, resolveWallClock, resolvePrompt,
        TestProviderConnectivity — RunOptions is the central type)
      cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go, agent_runner_service.go,
        claude_stream.go, virtual.go, script.go (all consume RunOptions)
      cli/internal/agent/session_index.go (SessionIndexEntryFromResult signature)
      cli/internal/agent/routing_metrics.go, models.go (lesser uses)
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:647 dispatchAgentRun
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:531 dispatchReviewRun
      cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1462 (RunViaService) and :1243 (RunBenchmarkViaService)
    Tests (must be migrated to keep build green per AC item 6/7):
      cli/internal/agent/agent_test.go (15+ BuildArgs sites)
      cli/internal/agent/virtual_test.go (3)
      cli/internal/agent/claude_stream_test.go (6)
      cli/internal/agent/prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go (3)
      cli/internal/agent/session_index_test.go (2)
      cli/internal/agent/models_test.go (1)
      cli/internal/agent/run_internal_test.go, agent_run_runtime_test.go,
        execute_bead_runtime_test.go, execute_bead_review_test.go,
        execute_bead_review_evidence_test.go, execute_bead_env_isolation_test.go,
        execute_bead_e2e_test.go, execute_bead_artifacts_test.go
      cli/cmd/agent_run_config_test.go, agent_quorum_config_test.go,
        agent_execute_bead_test.go, agent_compare_config_test.go,
        cli/internal/server/workers_stop_propagation_test.go
      cli/cmd/command_factory.go (production), cli/internal/config/testconfig.go,
        cli/internal/exec/types.go (mostly comments)

  Net surface: ~4,000 LoC of net change across the agent package, the cmd
  package, the server package, the exec package, the config package, plus
  ~10,000 LoC of test code that constructs the legacy types or accepts them
  as helper parameters. Every public-API surface change cascades to its
  callers, including BuildArgs (15+ test call sites) and
  SessionIndexEntryFromResult (consumed by session-index machinery and the
  oversize-prompt ingress test).

## What is blocking

The migration is too large for a single execute-bead worker window even with
the expanded scope. Three concrete reasons:

1. **Per-bead size budget.** SD-024 §Migration discipline (lines 234–256)
   explicitly mandates "<500 LoC of net change, <20 minutes of agent time,
   under $10 of model spend" per bead, with a footnote that "Anything that
   wants to 'do the whole loop migration in one shot' is sized wrong and
   must be split." This bead's expanded scope is ~4,000 production LoC plus
   ~10,000 test LoC — 8x to 28x the SD-mandated budget. The previous attempt
   spent 16.6 minutes and $6.75 producing no_changes; even doubling that
   budget cannot produce a green tree across this surface in one pass.

2. **The migration is not one atomic change but a sequence of seam swaps.**
   Each affected public API (RunViaService → RunWithConfigViaService,
   RunCompareViaService → RunCompareWithConfigViaService, BuildArgs's
   RunOptions parameter, SessionIndexEntryFromResult's RunOptions parameter,
   RunFunc closure type) has its own caller graph. SD-024's own discipline
   (lines 248–256) prescribes per-caller migration beads precisely so each
   step leaves the tree green and lint-clean. Bundling them into one bead
   re-creates the bug class the SD's migration discipline was written to
   avoid: a half-finished refactor with the lint gate firing on the
   intermediate state, where the worker either has to disable hooks (forbidden)
   or revert most of the work to commit anything.

3. **Test-file lint cascade.** The runtime-lint pre-commit gate (lefthook.yml
   pattern 2) flags any staged Go file containing a RunOptions{} / CompareOptions{}
   / QuorumOptions{} composite literal. Migrating BuildArgs's signature
   alone (to take a non-RunOptions parameter) re-stages every one of the
   15+ agent_test.go test files, and the gate fires on each. The same
   cascade fires when SessionIndexEntryFromResult's signature changes
   (re-staging session_index_test.go), when RunFunc's closure type changes
   (re-staging compare/quorum/benchmark tests), and so on for each public-API
   seam swap. Every signature change requires migrating its consumers in
   the *same commit* — but the consumers' test files already contain
   composite literals from sibling test cases that aren't in this bead's
   AC. Those literals stay valid (BuildArgs still works) but the gate fires
   because the file is now staged. Untangling this requires touching every
   composite-literal site in every staged test file — hence the ~10,000 LoC
   of test work above.

## What a follow-up attempt would need

Per SD-024 §Stage 2 (lines 281–286) and §Migration discipline (lines 222–256),
this bead should be split along the seam-swap boundaries the SD itself
prescribes. Concretely, the recommended split is:

a) **B22d-grade.go** (~10 LoC): switch the single `r.Run(RunOptions{...})`
   call in cli/internal/agent/grade.go to
   `r.runInternal(RunArgs{...})` via the existing dispatch seam from
   ddx-dd640c18. Satisfies AC items 1, 4, 5 (RunOptions/CompareOptions/
   QuorumOptions removed from grade.go — items 4 and 5 are vacuous as the
   types aren't present today). Build and tests green; runtime-lint green
   because the staged file contains no legacy literals after the swap.
   Independently buildable, lint-clean, AC-checkable.

b) **B22d-cmd-RunViaService** (~30 LoC): switch
   cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1462's RunViaService call to
   RunWithConfigViaService, threading a ResolvedConfig through the call.
   Independently buildable, lint-clean.

c) **B22d-cmd-RunBenchmarkViaService** (~30 LoC): same migration for
   :1243 RunBenchmarkViaService → a new RunBenchmarkWithConfigViaService
   that takes ResolvedConfig.

d) **B22d-execute_bead-dispatch** (~80 LoC + 2-3 test files):
   migrate execute_bead.go dispatchAgentRun (line 647 RunOptions
   composite, line 938 RunViaServiceWith) and execute_bead_review.go
   dispatchReviewRun (line 531 RunOptions composite, line 704
   RunViaServiceWith) onto a new internal seam that takes
   ResolvedConfig + AgentRunRuntime. Update the corresponding
   execute_bead_*_test.go files in this bead (they already import the
   functions and stage as siblings).

e) **B22d-BuildArgs-signature** (~150 LoC, mostly tests):
   change BuildArgs's parameter from RunOptions to a new internal
   buildArgsInput struct (or a slice of explicit args). Update
   agent_test.go's 15+ call sites in the same commit. Adjacent runner.go
   call sites stay on RunOptions internally — runner.go is not in the AC
   removal list.

f) **B22d-SessionIndexEntryFromResult-signature** (~80 LoC):
   change SessionIndexEntryFromResult's RunOptions parameter to take
   the small subset of fields it actually uses (Harness, Model, Provider,
   Correlation, PromptSource, etc.) as discrete arguments or a small
   internal struct. Update session_index_test.go and the four
   service-run callers in the same commit.

g) **B22d-RunFunc-and-compare-internals** (~250 LoC):
   change RunFunc's signature, runCompareArmWith, defaultResolvePromptForCompare,
   the public RunCompareWith/RunQuorumWith/RunBenchmarkWith signatures
   and BenchmarkArmsToCompare's return shape. Update prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go,
   models_test.go, claude_stream_test.go in the same commit. After this
   step, compare_adapter.go has no RunOptions/CompareOptions/QuorumOptions
   remaining (AC items 2, 3 satisfied).

h) **B22d-service_run-public-API** (~150 LoC):
   delete RunViaService, RunViaServiceWith, runFixtureHarnessViaRunner and
   inline their bodies into the new RunWithConfigViaService internal helper.
   At this point all callers have already moved (steps b, c, d) so the
   deletion is mechanical. service_run.go has no RunOptions remaining
   (AC item 2 satisfied).

After all eight steps, the legacy types are unreachable from any caller,
runner.go's internal RunOptions usage can be retired in B22 final, and
the type definitions in types.go:36/81/171 can be deleted as planned.

The grade.go change in isolation (step a) is genuinely a 10-LoC win and
satisfies 3 of the 7 AC items (1, 4, 5) — but the bead's stated rule
"Do not commit partial, exploratory, or red code hoping the reviewer will
accept it" forbids landing it without also satisfying items 2, 3, 6, 7.
Per attempt-1's findings, the only path that satisfies *all* AC items in
*one commit* is the ~14,000 LoC change above, which both exceeds the
SD-024 size budget and is unworkable in a single execute-bead worker
window.

The right shape for this work is the eight-bead split above. Each step
is independently <500 LoC, satisfies SD-024 §Migration discipline, and
ends with a green tree, a passing build, and no runtime-lint violations
on its staged files.

title: execute-bead: agents make edits but skip git commit; iteration commits land only execution evidence
description:
Observed today across 5 attempts on the axon repo (claude/sonnet, claude/opus, codex/gpt-5.4-mini): the agent does real work in the worktree (multiple Edit tool calls touching real source files) but never runs git commit. Result: iteration commit is built by ddx-land-coordinator with ONLY execution-evidence files (.ddx/executions/<id>/result.json, manifest.json, usage.json), no actual code or doc changes. The post-merge codex reviewer correctly flags BLOCK with rationale 'change set only adds execution metadata files'.

Concrete evidence (axon repo, 2026-04-29):

  Attempt: 20260429T235353-d14f6b91 (axon-80979cb8 / A1 sub-bead)
  Harness: claude / model: sonnet
  Duration: 817 sec, cost $3.61, 132 tool calls
  Tool breakdown: 79 Bash, 34 Read, 11 Grep, 8 Edit
  Edit targets: ui/src/lib/api.ts, ui/src/lib/policy-evaluator.ts,
    ui/src/routes/.../policies/+page.svelte, ui/tests/e2e/link-policy-retry.spec.ts
  Iteration commit (ddx-land-coordinator): contains exactly 2 files —
    .ddx/executions/20260429T235353-d14f6b91/result.json (23 lines)
    .ddx/executions/20260429T235353-d14f6b91/usage.json (9 lines)
  Net: zero actual edits landed despite $3.61 of model time spent making them.

The same pattern appeared on:
  20260429T235406-20eeaf2c (axon-db7a6d0a, claude/sonnet, $0.57, 4.2 min, BLOCK)
  20260429T190657-fa91f49b (axon-ab2e52e0, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.08, 24s, stalled)
  20260429T190634-579df880 (axon-80979cb8, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.12, 104s, BLOCK)
  20260429T190646-5aa5b46e (axon-db7a6d0a, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.06, 25s, BLOCK)

Total burn today: ~$4.50 across 5 attempts on 3 beads, 0 closed.

The execute-bead prompt template tells the agent to commit:
  '4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with git add <specific-paths>...'

So agent self-commit is the contract. But:

(a) When typecheck/lint/test fails, the agent is told to fix or write no_changes_rationale.txt — but apparently many agents are bailing silently without writing the rationale either, so the harness sees a clean worktree (no diff staged or committed) and treats it as 'agent made no commits'. The iteration then carries only the auto-generated execution evidence.

(b) There may also be a path where the agent commits but a pre-commit hook silently rejects it. Have not confirmed; the worktree was reaped before I could inspect.

The issue spans multiple harnesses (claude, codex via embedded openrouter), so it is not specific to one provider's commit semantics. Either:
  - The harness should reject 'agent made no commits AND no no_changes_rationale.txt' as a separate failure class instead of silently letting ddx-land-coordinator land an empty iteration
  - The agent prompt should make rationale-writing on incomplete work non-optional with stronger language
  - The harness should run a final-stage check 'did the agent produce ANY signal — commit OR rationale — and if not, treat as harness-execution-failed and surface to the operator'
acceptance:
AC1. When an execute-bead session exits without committing in the worktree AND without writing .ddx/executions/<id>/no_changes_rationale.txt, the harness records a NEW dedicated status (e.g. 'agent_silent_exit' or 'no_evidence_produced') instead of letting ddx-land-coordinator land an iteration containing only execution evidence.

AC2. The codex reviewer's BLOCK rationale ('change set only adds execution metadata files') becomes redundant — the harness catches this case before the reviewer is even invoked, saving a redundant review pass and its cost.

AC3. The execute-bead prompt template is updated so the rationale-on-incomplete-work instruction is harder to skip — ideally a tool the agent must invoke (e.g. SubmitNoChangesRationale tool) rather than 'write a file and exit'.

AC4. When the agent did run Edit tool calls but the worktree has no commit, the harness logs a warning naming each file the agent edited that is now uncommitted, so operators can recover the work or diagnose the silent-commit-failure.

AC5. Documentation in docs/ explains the agent-self-commit contract, the rationale-file fallback, and the new 'agent_silent_exit' classification.

bead.title: execute-bead: agents make edits but skip git commit; iteration commits land only execution evidence
bead.description:
Observed today across 5 attempts on the axon repo (claude/sonnet, claude/opus, codex/gpt-5.4-mini): the agent does real work in the worktree (multiple Edit tool calls touching real source files) but never runs git commit. Result: iteration commit is built by ddx-land-coordinator with ONLY execution-evidence files (.ddx/executions/<id>/result.json, manifest.json, usage.json), no actual code or doc changes. The post-merge codex reviewer correctly flags BLOCK with rationale 'change set only adds execution metadata files'.

Concrete evidence (axon repo, 2026-04-29):

  Attempt: 20260429T235353-d14f6b91 (axon-80979cb8 / A1 sub-bead)
  Harness: claude / model: sonnet
  Duration: 817 sec, cost $3.61, 132 tool calls
  Tool breakdown: 79 Bash, 34 Read, 11 Grep, 8 Edit
  Edit targets: ui/src/lib/api.ts, ui/src/lib/policy-evaluator.ts,
    ui/src/routes/.../policies/+page.svelte, ui/tests/e2e/link-policy-retry.spec.ts
  Iteration commit (ddx-land-coordinator): contains exactly 2 files —
    .ddx/executions/20260429T235353-d14f6b91/result.json (23 lines)
    .ddx/executions/20260429T235353-d14f6b91/usage.json (9 lines)
  Net: zero actual edits landed despite $3.61 of model time spent making them.

The same pattern appeared on:
  20260429T235406-20eeaf2c (axon-db7a6d0a, claude/sonnet, $0.57, 4.2 min, BLOCK)
  20260429T190657-fa91f49b (axon-ab2e52e0, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.08, 24s, stalled)
  20260429T190634-579df880 (axon-80979cb8, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.12, 104s, BLOCK)
  20260429T190646-5aa5b46e (axon-db7a6d0a, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.06, 25s, BLOCK)

Total burn today: ~$4.50 across 5 attempts on 3 beads, 0 closed.

The execute-bead prompt template tells the agent to commit:
  '4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with git add <specific-paths>...'

So agent self-commit is the contract. But:

(a) When typecheck/lint/test fails, the agent is told to fix or write no_changes_rationale.txt — but apparently many agents are bailing silently without writing the rationale either, so the harness sees a clean worktree (no diff staged or committed) and treats it as 'agent made no commits'. The iteration then carries only the auto-generated execution evidence.

(b) There may also be a path where the agent commits but a pre-commit hook silently rejects it. Have not confirmed; the worktree was reaped before I could inspect.

The issue spans multiple harnesses (claude, codex via embedded openrouter), so it is not specific to one provider's commit semantics. Either:
  - The harness should reject 'agent made no commits AND no no_changes_rationale.txt' as a separate failure class instead of silently letting ddx-land-coordinator land an empty iteration
  - The agent prompt should make rationale-writing on incomplete work non-optional with stronger language
  - The harness should run a final-stage check 'did the agent produce ANY signal — commit OR rationale — and if not, treat as harness-execution-failed and surface to the operator'
bead.acceptance:
AC1. When an execute-bead session exits without committing in the worktree AND without writing .ddx/executions/<id>/no_changes_rationale.txt, the harness records a NEW dedicated status (e.g. 'agent_silent_exit' or 'no_evidence_produced') instead of letting ddx-land-coordinator land an iteration containing only execution evidence.

AC2. The codex reviewer's BLOCK rationale ('change set only adds execution metadata files') becomes redundant — the harness catches this case before the reviewer is even invoked, saving a redundant review pass and its cost.

AC3. The execute-bead prompt template is updated so the rationale-on-incomplete-work instruction is harder to skip — ideally a tool the agent must invoke (e.g. SubmitNoChangesRationale tool) rather than 'write a file and exit'.

AC4. When the agent did run Edit tool calls but the worktree has no commit, the harness logs a warning naming each file the agent edited that is now uncommitted, so operators can recover the work or diagnose the silent-commit-failure.

AC5. Documentation in docs/ explains the agent-self-commit contract, the rationale-file fallback, and the new 'agent_silent_exit' classification.

events.body:
no_changes
rationale: Bead ddx-ce7a796e (B22d-g) cannot be landed in isolation against base
41d464b03c4e5d12b7a9244c3c59ee312e64e255. Same fundamental blocker as
B22d-f (ddx-9f4b1a8c, no_changes 2026-04-27): lefthook `runtime-lint`
hook (lefthook.yml) filters analyzer diagnostics to the set of staged
files, and the in-scope test files contain pre-existing RunOptions /
CompareOptions composite literals whose underlying APIs are explicitly
named out-of-scope by this bead's parent decomposition (step h owns
service_run.go; runner.go and claude_stream.go aren't in this step's
in-scope at all).

What is done (verified locally, then reverted)
- compare_adapter.go is straightforward to drain of RunOptions /
  CompareOptions / QuorumOptions:
    * RunFunc redeclared as `func(rcfg config.ResolvedConfig,
      runtime AgentRunRuntime) (*Result, error)`.
    * defaultResolvePromptForCompare(runtime AgentRunRuntime).
    * runCompareArmWith(run RunFunc, rcfg, runtime CompareRuntime,
      armIdx, harnessName, baseDir, prompt, worktreePath).
    * RunCompareWith(run, rcfg, runtime CompareRuntime, resolvePrompt,
      cleanupSandbox).
    * RunQuorumWith(run, rcfg, runtime QuorumRuntime).
    * RunBenchmarkWith(runCompare func(config.ResolvedConfig,
      CompareRuntime) (*ComparisonRecord, error), suite).
    * RunCompareViaService / RunCompareWithAgent / RunQuorumViaService /
      RunQuorumWithAgent / RunBenchmarkViaService / RunBenchmarkWithAgent
      rewritten to take (rcfg, runtime) instead of CompareOptions /
      QuorumOptions and to delegate through the new RunFunc.
    * RunCompareWithConfigViaService / RunQuorumWithConfigViaService
      collapsed into a single forwarding hop (they no longer need to
      assemble a CompareOptions/QuorumOptions intermediary).
    * RunQuorumWithConfig retained, now calling RunQuorumWith with the
      new shape.
- models.go BenchmarkArmsToCompare changes return shape from
  CompareOptions to CompareRuntime (and accepts an AgentRunRuntime
  baseline rather than a RunOptions). compare_adapter.go's
  RunBenchmarkWith uses that shape directly.
- models_test.go updates the BenchmarkArmsToCompare call to pass
  `AgentRunRuntime{Prompt: "test"}` and assert
  CompareRuntime.{Harnesses, ArmModels, ArmLabels}. This is the only
  RunOptions-related drift in models_test.go — the rest of that file
  doesn't reference RunOptions.
- After the in-package edits, `cd cli && go build ./...` passes and
  `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/...` is clean once the test
  files in the next bullet are also updated.
- agent_test.go (out of scope) needs one closure-shape update at
  line 584 because RunFunc's signature changed; with that one-line
  diff applied locally, TestQuorumRunsAllHarnesses passes.

What is blocking (lint policy vs. bead scope)

1. claude_stream_test.go (in-scope) contains RunOptions composite
   literals at lines 206, 212, 218, 224, 291, and 324. These are all
   inside test cases that call helpers declared in claude_stream.go —
   resolveClaudeProgressLogDir(opts RunOptions, cfg Config) at
   claude_stream.go:284 and runClaudeStreamingFn(r, ctx, harness,
   "claude", "", opts RunOptions, "hi", "", 10*time.Second) at
   claude_stream.go:304/477/507. claude_stream.go is NOT in this
   bead's in-scope; B22d's per-step decomposition reserves it for a
   separate step. With claude_stream.go's RunOptions surface
   unchanged, the test cannot construct anything other than
   RunOptions{...} to drive resolveClaudeProgressLogDir, and the
   runtime-lint hook flags every one of those literals when the
   file is staged.

2. prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go (in-scope) has three RunOptions
   composite literals:
     * line 78  — r.resolvePrompt(RunOptions{PromptFile: fixture})
                  drives Runner.resolvePrompt in runner.go:976.
                  runner.go is out of scope.
     * line 85  — defaultResolvePromptForCompare(RunOptions{...})
                  drives the in-scope helper. CAN be updated.
     * line 112 — RunViaServiceWith(ctx, svc, dir, RunOptions{...})
                  drives service_run.go:104. service_run.go's public
                  API is explicitly named out-of-scope ("step h").
   Even after migrating line 85 and updating line 98's RunBenchmarkWith
   closure to the new (rcfg, runtime CompareRuntime) shape, the file
   still carries lines 78 and 112 as pre-existing RunOptions
   composite literals. runtime-lint scopes diagnostics by file (not
   by hunk), so the staged file fails the gate on lines that this
   bead is not authorized to touch.

3. agent_test.go (out of scope, but transitively required) — once
   RunFunc's signature changes, agent_test.go:584's
   `func(opts RunOptions) (*Result, error)` no longer matches
   RunQuorumWithConfig's RunFunc parameter, so the package fails to
   build. The minimal fix is one line, but agent_test.go has 18
   other RunOptions composite literals (BuildArgs call sites at
   lines 118, 130, 148, 157, 165, 188, 196, 209, 214, 677, 684,
   718, 727, 856, 864, 971, plus 678, 684) that are pre-existing,
   unrelated to this bead, and depend on BuildArgs (runner.go) being
   migrated separately. Staging agent_test.go would surface every
   one of those as a lint violation.

The runtime-lint hook is mandatory (per project policy: --no-verify is
forbidden). Diagnostic filtering is whole-file, not hunk-aware, so
"only edit my own lines" doesn't help when the file already contained
violations that this bead's named scope can't reach.

What a follow-up attempt would need (any of the following)

A. Re-decompose B22d-g to also include claude_stream.go (so
   resolveClaudeProgressLogDir / runClaudeStreamingFn migrate off
   RunOptions in the same commit, draining claude_stream_test.go),
   AND service_run.go's RunViaServiceWith + runner.go's
   resolvePrompt (so prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go drains), AND
   agent_test.go's BuildArgs call sites (so the RunFunc signature
   change compiles cleanly across the package). This is essentially
   the previously-attempted atomic B22d scope (worker-3f7a's
   recommendation, applied 2026-04-26 in commit 97561a6e); the
   subsequent re-decomposition into steps a-h reintroduced this
   exact tension.

B. Land step h FIRST (and in fact most of the rest of the
   per-file decomposition) so that claude_stream.go, runner.go,
   service_run.go, and agent_test.go no longer contain pre-existing
   RunOptions usages, then run step g — at that point the in-scope
   test files have no out-of-scope violations and lint passes on the
   staged set. The current bead-queue ordering puts step g before
   step h, so this requires a tracker reordering.

C. Change runtime-lint's filter from whole-file to hunk-scoped (so
   pre-existing violations on lines this bead doesn't touch don't
   block the commit). This is a lint-policy change in lefthook.yml;
   not in this bead's scope and not authorized by the bead
   description.

D. Authorize the worker (model-side) to extend in-scope to cover
   the transitively-required files (claude_stream.go,
   runner.go.resolvePrompt + BuildArgs, service_run.go.RunViaServiceWith,
   and agent_test.go), the same way B22d itself was authorized in
   2026-04-26 (commit 97561a6e). The diff for that expansion is
   already prepared and verified locally; only the bead-scope
   constraint stands in the way.

The implementation is mechanical and verified locally; the blocker is
project-level lint policy versus per-step bead scope, not
implementation difficulty. No partial commit is justified, since
landing only the in-scope-clean half (compare_adapter.go +
models.go + models_test.go) breaks the package build by leaving
agent_test.go with an incompatible RunFunc closure, and updating
that closure trips the lint on agent_test.go's pre-existing 18+
RunOptions literals.
